title: Phase 7: Real-time & Polish
description:
## Verification Checklist

This phase adds polish, real-time features, and responsive design to the **Homespun.Web** project (`src/Homespun.Web`).

**Child Issues:**
- [ ] **p7FZ7e** - Loading States and Skeletons - Consistent loading UX
- [ ] **uS7SBP** - Notifications System - Toast notifications and notification hub
- [ ] **QPlm4U** - Responsive Design - Mobile-friendly layouts

**Agent Verification Instructions:**
When all child issues are complete, verify the following in `src/Homespun.Web`:
1. Confirm all loading states use appropriate skeleton components
2. Verify content doesn't jump/shift on load
3. Confirm toast notifications appear for SignalR events
4. Verify notification history dropdown shows recent notifications
5. Check that the application works on mobile viewports (375px, 768px)
6. Confirm sidebar converts to drawer/hamburger menu on mobile
7. Verify all features are accessible via touch
8. Check that optimistic updates feel instant

Mark each child issue checkbox above as verified, then mark this phase complete.

title: Remove horizontal edges between the base line and siblings of next issues
description: When there is a "next" issue with multiple siblings in series, a horizontal line connects all of the sibling issues with the root edge (the vertical line in the leftmost lane). This is not really correct as the issues below the first sibling are blocked from being executed next - only show a horizontal line connecting the root edge with the topmost issue which is definitively marked "next". This does not apply to the parallel issues.

title: Cache graph
description:
The graph should be locally cached rather than always reloaded when the project page is opened as the retrieval of PR data takes some time to reload.

Serialize and cache the PR information on every load (the issue data is fast to load so can be refreshed), then update it asynchronously after the graph is displayed.

title: Add front end observability
description: Add logging to the front end. In Azure Container Apps mode this should log to the Application Insights resource that is set up in the Azure infrastructure. Let me know your thoughts on logging for the Docker and local (i.e. dotnet run) modes.

title: Show plan ready status in status indicators at top of page
description: Currently the agent status indicators in the top bar of the page don't show agents that are currently in "Plan Ready" status - the agent is not included in the number either. They should be shown as agents with an orange indicator, similar to agents in a "question" status. Update the status display handler to fail gracefully. If the status is unknown, show a question mark and log this as an error.

title: Update the Fleece library version to v1.6.0
description: Update Fleece.Core from v1.4.0 to v1.6.0 and adapt Homespun to use the new features: key-value tag support, Verify issue type, and LinkedPR migration to keyed tags.

title: Move session details to collapsible top section
description:
On mobile the session details panel is too large and blocks view of the session. Reduce it to a collapsible section at the top of the  pane when viewed on mobile. When collapsed it should effectively replace the text "Claude Code Session" with the title of the issue.

On desktop, replace the text Claude Code Session with the title of the issue.

title: Fix permissions sent from client
description: The Homespun.Web client seems to consistently send messages with plan mode regardless of the selected option. Ensure the selected option is passed all the way through to the worker. The mode is set per session and not per message, so the server should be the main place that this state is tracked.

title: Create Settings page with GitHub/git authentication health display
description: Create a Settings page accessible from the sidebar that displays GitHub CLI authentication status, Git credential helper status, Masked GITHUB_TOKEN display, Configuration instructions, and Refresh/re-authenticate button.

title: Add comprehensive logging to worker status transitions
description: Add logging to track all worker status changes, stream events, and pending states to help diagnose issues where displayed status doesn't match actual worker activity. This includes logging for idle/streaming/closed transitions, tool executions, A2A protocol state changes, and pending question/plan states.

title: Review and consolidate session IDs
description: Review the worker container in particular and confirm the method by which session IDs are being created and managed. I understand that the container and agent have an id `{project-id}-{issue-id}` and Claude Code generates a unique session ID that is used for identifying and resuming sessions. Are there any other IDs that are being used in the Homespun application to identify sessions, and if so do we need them or can we just use the container ID and Claude Code session ID?

title: Create toolbar
Allow local only repositories
description: I would like to be able to run Homespun without the GitHub integrations as well - mostly for testing. Please review the architecture and plan to be able to detach the GitHub service for certain local-only projects. In this instance, creating a new project should just create the source folder adjacent to the other GitHub related projects. There will need to be a flag added to the project registration in homespun-data.json to note that it is not a GitHub backed project.

title: Add project links to side bar
description: The project links should be replicated in the side bar - one per project. This was done in the Blazor application but should be replicated in the React Homespun.Web client.

title: Fix issues agent diff
Consolidate ID types
Update branch ID in issue when an agent is run
Change graph colours
description:
The graph colours should be changed as follows:

- Merged PRs: Show these in a light grey
- Current PRs: Green if they have all checks passing, yellow if checks are currently running, orange if there are merge conflicts, red if checks are failing.
- Issues: Colour by status, except for "bug" types - blue for idea, yellow for spec, green for next, red for bugs.

title: Run hooks
description: Please review the Claude Agent SDK implementation in this repository and create a plan for parsing the .claude/settings.json file for hooks and running them all when a new session is started. See https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/hooks for documentation on the Agent SDK and hooks.

title: Install Typescript LSP and Claude Code plugin
description: In the worker container Dockerfile, install the Typescript LSP and Claude Code Typescript plugin. The plugin should be installed with `claude plugin install typescript-lsp@claude-plugins-official` but the Typescript LSP server may also need to be installed. Review this by installing it locally first and verifying that it works, then updating the Dockerfile.

title: Add design routes for component testing
description: A set of /design/{component-name} routes should be added that show all individual components with mock data when the application is run in the `mock` environment. This can then be used by Claude Code and the Playwright MCP tool to iterate on UI design.

title: Upgrade timeline rendering
description:
Create a custom timeline renderer to replace the git-grapj.js renderer. It should be functionally similar, a simple two column grid drawn. Each row should be one Blazor component.

The left column should be the graph itself, drawn as individual SVG. The right column will include the text and any annotations to denote issue, agent and PR status.

Implement a graph layout algorithm that performs a similar function. Keep track of "lanes" that run vertically and perform a DFS of the graph to determine the lane structure. This can be done in two passes, first to calculate the maximum number of lanes (to determine the SVG width) and then to lay out the lanes.

Use simple straight lines with bends to show lane switching.

title: Create workflow routes and pages
description:
Create TanStack Router routes for workflow management.

## Files to Create
\`\`\`
src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/projects/\$projectId/
├── workflows/
│   ├── index.tsx              # Workflow list page
│   ├── \$workflowId/
│   │   ├── index.tsx          # Workflow editor page
│   │   └── executions/
│   │       ├── index.tsx      # Execution list
│   │       └── \$executionId.tsx  # Execution detail
│   └── new.tsx                # New workflow page
\`\`\`

## Pages
1. **Workflow List** (`/projects/:projectId/workflows`)
   - List all workflows for project
   - Create new workflow button
   - Quick actions (edit, delete, run)

2. **Workflow Editor** (`/projects/:projectId/workflows/:workflowId`)
   - Visual/code editor canvas
   - Save/run buttons
   - Execution history sidebar

3. **New Workflow** (`/projects/:projectId/workflows/new`)
   - Workflow name/description
   - Template selection (optional)
   - Initial trigger configuration

4. **Execution Detail** (`/projects/:projectId/workflows/:workflowId/executions/:executionId`)
   - Live execution visualization
   - Node status indicators
   - Context viewer
   - Approval controls for gates

## Pattern Reference
Follow existing routes in `src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/`

title: Inline Issue Creation
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement inline issue creation within the task graph using Vim-like keyboard commands.

**NOTE: This replaces the original 'bottom bar' concept. The Blazor implementation uses inline creation triggered by keyboard commands.**

**Primary Component: InlineIssueEditor**
A text input that appears in-place within the task graph when creating/editing an issue.

**Creation Commands (from Viewing mode):**
- `o` - Create issue below current selection
- `Shift+O` - Create issue above current selection

**Editing Commands (while viewing):**
- `i` - Start editing at beginning of title
- `a` - Start editing at end of title
- `r` - Replace title (clear and edit)

**Hierarchy Commands (while editing):**
- `Tab` - Make new issue PARENT of reference (indent)
- `Shift+Tab` - Make new issue SIBLING of reference parent (outdent)

**Editor Actions:**
- `Enter` - Save and return to viewing mode
- `Shift+Enter` - Save and open full edit page (for description)
- `Escape` - Cancel edit, discard changes

**Tasks:**
1. Create InlineIssueEditor component with:
   - Text input for title
   - Action buttons (Save, Save+Edit, Cancel)
   - Lane-based positioning matching selected row
2. Implement cursor position control (start, end, replace)
3. Handle Tab/Shift+Tab for hierarchy changes during creation
4. Create useCreateIssue mutation hook
5. Focus management and keyboard event handling
6. Smooth insertion animation
7. Optimistic updates (show new issue immediately)
8. Type cycling with Tab (in viewing mode): Task → Bug → Feature → Chore

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `o`/`O` opens inline editor at correct position
- Tab/Shift+Tab changes hierarchy relationship
- Enter saves, Escape cancels
- New issues appear in graph immediately
- Focus returns to graph after save/cancel

title: Phase 2: Projects & Navigation
description:
## Verification Checklist

This phase implements project management pages and navigation structure in the **Homespun.Web** project (`src/Homespun.Web`).

**Child Issues:**
- [ ] **HeQn06** - Projects List Page - Display all projects with cards
- [ ] **qSPgRV** - Project Creation - Form to create new projects
- [ ] **5sKn2r** - Project Detail Shell - Tab-based layout for project sub-pages

**Agent Verification Instructions:**
When all child issues are complete, verify the following in `src/Homespun.Web`:
1. Confirm `/projects` route displays all projects as cards
2. Verify new project creation form works and projects appear in the list
3. Confirm project detail page loads at `/projects/:projectId`
4. Verify tab navigation within project detail shell works correctly
5. Check that navigation between projects list and detail pages is seamless

Mark each child issue checkbox above as verified, then mark this phase complete.

title: Remove left diff panel and just show changes
description:
In the issue diff page for issues agents there are currently two graph panels to show the issues in the main branch and in the current branch. Delete the left diff panel and just show the issues that have changed in one graph view. 

When an item is selected it should show the details of the changes below the graph.

title: Add 'e' shortcut to open run agent panel
description: When an issue is selected and the edit mode isn't on, pressing the 'e' key should open the run agent panel that allows users to select a prompt and run an agent.

title: Move issue history folder outside of working directory
description: The application saves the issue history in .fleece/history within the main branch of the repository. This is being committed and is causing a lot of merge conflicts. Instead of this, move this folder to the project folder, next to .clones and main, and update the service to use this location instead.

title: Agent queuing on multiple issues
Show all issues that have open PRs regardless of status
description: When an issue is matched to a PR, the issue should always be shown in the graph regardless of its current status.

title: Add Clones tab route and navigation
description:
## Overview Add the new Clones tab to the project page navigation and create the route file. ## Implementation ### 1. Add Tab to Project Page Navigation **File:** `src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/projects.$projectId.tsx` Add new tab entry to the tabs array (between Branches and Prompts): ```typescript const tabs = [ { name: "Issues", path: "/issues" }, { name: "Pull Requests", path: "/pull-requests" }, { name: "Branches", path: "/branches" }, { name: "Clones", path: "/clones" }, // NEW { name: "Prompts", path: "/prompts" }, { name: "Secrets", path: "/secrets" }, { name: "Settings", path: "/settings" }, ] ``` ### 2. Create Clones Route File **File:** `src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/projects.$projectId.clones.tsx` ```typescript import { createFileRoute } from "@tanstack/react-router" import { ClonesTab } from "@/features/clones" export const Route = createFileRoute("/projects/$projectId/clones")({ component: ClonesPage, }) function ClonesPage() { const { projectId } = Route.useParams() return } ``` ### 3. Create Feature Folder Structure Create the following folder structure: ``` src/Homespun.Web/src/features/clones/ ├── components/ │ ├── clones-tab.tsx # Main container (placeholder initially) │ └── index.ts ├── hooks/ │ └── index.ts └── index.ts # Public exports ``` Initial placeholder for `clones-tab.tsx`: ```typescript export interface ClonesTabProps { projectId: string } export function ClonesTab({ projectId }: ClonesTabProps) { return (

Clones

Clone management coming soon. Project ID: {projectId}

) } ``` ## Test Cases 1. Navigation shows Clones tab 2. Clicking Clones tab navigates to `/projects/{id}/clones` 3. ClonesTab component renders with projectId ## Notes - This task sets up the routing infrastructure - The actual ClonesTab content will be implemented in subsequent tasks - Follows existing tab patterns from the project page

title: Control application containers with Komodo
description: Add Komodo for container management with Nerdbank.GitVersioning for automated versioning

title: Integrate Fleece events with workflow triggers
description:
Connect Fleece issue events to workflow trigger system.

## Files to Modify
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Services/FleeceService.cs`

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/FleeceEventPublisher.cs`

## Events to Publish
1. **issue_created**
   - Publish when CreateIssueAsync completes
   - Include: projectId, issueId, type, title

2. **issue_status_changed**
   - Publish when UpdateIssueAsync changes status
   - Include: projectId, issueId, oldStatus, newStatus

3. **issue_assigned**
   - Publish when assignee changes
   - Include: projectId, issueId, assignee

## Implementation
- Use internal event bus pattern
- Fire-and-forget (don't block issue operations)

title: Update application config to launch React web client
description: Review the Tailscale and Docker Compose setup to expose the React web client at the root HTTPS address. Use port 3200 for exposing the original Blazor web application.

title: Add REST API with Swagger for backend testing
description:
Implement a REST API layer to enable programmatic access and API-level testing of Homespun functionality.

## Architecture
- Keep Blazor components using @inject for direct service access (no change)
- Add thin API controllers that call existing services
- Add Swagger/OpenAPI documentation

## API Endpoints

### Projects
- GET /api/projects - List all projects
- GET /api/projects/{id} - Get project by ID
- POST /api/projects - Create project
- PUT /api/projects/{id} - Update project
- DELETE /api/projects/{id} - Delete project

### Pull Requests
- GET /api/projects/{id}/pull-requests - List PRs for project
- GET /api/pull-requests/{id} - Get PR by ID
- POST /api/pull-requests - Create PR
- PUT /api/pull-requests/{id} - Update PR
- POST /api/projects/{id}/sync - Sync PRs from GitHub

### Git Worktrees
- GET /api/worktrees - List worktrees
- POST /api/worktrees - Create worktree
- DELETE /api/worktrees/{path} - Delete worktree

### Claude Sessions
- GET /api/sessions - List active sessions
- GET /api/sessions/{id} - Get session details
- POST /api/sessions - Start new session
- POST /api/sessions/{id}/messages - Send message to session
- DELETE /api/sessions/{id} - Stop session

### Fleece Issues
- GET /api/projects/{id}/issues - List issues for project
- GET /api/issues/{id} - Get issue by ID
- POST /api/issues - Create issue
- PUT /api/issues/{id} - Update issue

### Notifications
- POST /api/notifications - Send notification (testing)

## Files to Create

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| Features/Projects/Controllers/ProjectsController.cs | Projects REST API |
| Features/PullRequests/Controllers/PullRequestsController.cs | PRs REST API |
| Features/Git/Controllers/WorktreesController.cs | Worktrees REST API |
| Features/ClaudeCode/Controllers/SessionsController.cs | Sessions REST API |
| Features/Fleece/Controllers/IssuesController.cs | Issues REST API |

## Files to Modify
- Program.cs - Add Swagger, API explorer

## Implementation Steps
1. Add Swagger/OpenAPI to Program.cs
2. Create ProjectsController (simplest, validates pattern)
3. Implement remaining controllers

## Verification
- Navigate to /swagger and test each endpoint
- Confirm Blazor components still work with @inject

title: Agents page currently broken
description: The agents page currently doesn't work, showing an issue with the real-time updates not working. This appears to be due to a link across the tailscale network.

title: Implement useEnrichedClones and useBulkDeleteClones hooks
Fix flaky issue sync test
Copy unstaged issues when cloning
description:
When issues are added using the Homespun UI, the modifications to the Fleece issues file in the repository is left unstaged and not committed until manually committed with the 'sync' action.

When a new agent is started on an issue, the cloning of the repository to create the branch doesn't copy these issues over as the cloning only includes the issues at the last commit. Update this such that after the cloning occurs the entire contents of the .fleece folder is copied over to replace the newly cloned .fleece folder.

title: Sending messages in chat doesn't update messages
description: Sending messages in the session chat will be sent to the agent to continue work, but the result isn't immediately streamed back into the chat message list. Instead, the page must be refreshed for the chat messages to be updated.

title: Move issue modification prompt to special session type
description:
A new prompt type and session features was added for running an agent whose purpose is to modify the issues directly based on a prompt from the user (e.g. "I want to implement feature X, review the codebase and create Fleece issues for the steps that are required to implement this feature").

This is now showing up in the global prompts and in the "run agent" prompt list. Instead of this, I want to consider these as a separate system from the UI point of view but runs using the same backend as the normal sessions.

Add a button to the toolbar that will run a new "Issues agent". When this button is clicked, a new session with the special "IssueModificationAgent" type should start with an empty chat box. This session should be created in a new branch without a new issue (creating a session on a branch was added in a recent PR #540. A system prompt should provide the instructions that the agent is only to read the codebase and make edits to the Fleece issues using the Fleece CLI. This should still show up in the "Sessions" pages, but have some visual distinction to show that it is a different type of agent.

There should be a link on the session page to a separate page that shows two side-by-side issues graphs representing a diff between the "main" issue graph (the same one that is shown in the projects page) and the issue graph from the branch that was created for this session. On this page there should be an "accept" and "cancel" button. If "accept" is clicked, the issues should be merged into the main branch (without committing) and the session stopped.

title: Remove "Agent idle" and relocate status indicator
description: When no agent is running, a green dot and "Agent idle" text is shown at the top right hand corner of the page. Remove this as there is no point displaying the idle status of the agent. Move the status indicator to the left hand side of the notification bell icon.

title: Review parsing of status when loading diff
Make "agents", "prompts" and "sessions" consistent
description:
The titles "agents", "prompts" and "sessions" are used inconsistently throughout the application.

- Prompts should refer to the actual prompt "build", "plan", "rebase", etc. that are injected into the session when it is started. The label on the AgentControlPanel and in the left side bar should be "Prompt", rather than "Agent".
- Agents refer to a running instance of Claude Code on an issue. This should be used to replace nearly all of the "Sessions" text throughout.
- Sessions refer to a particular conversation thread that is either currently running or has been run on an agent. There will be very little user interaction with individual sessions, as the agents tend to only require one or two sessions to complete their work and this is mostly managed by the instances of Claude Code.

Put together a plan to make this terminology consistent throughout the application.

Be very careful with links between pages - after updating addresses ensure that the correct reference is used (e.g. if the /sessions/ page is changed to /agents/, change all instances of these links.

title: Change mock scripts to run containers
description:
Change the scripts/mock.sh and scripts/mock.ps1 to build and run the application in a container with mock data instead of running the application directly. The application should be able to specify a port to expose the container to so that it can connect to it with the MCP tools.

You may need to modify the scripts/run.sh and scripts/run.ps1 scripts to allow the build to be changed to use mock data instead.

Update the Dockerfile to include the necessary dependencies for the container to run the application as a container. Use DooD unless there is an obvious reason to use DinD.

Try to test this, but if it requires rebuilding the host container that you are currently running in let me know and I can test it then.

title: Switch rendering state for series and parallel issues
description: The rendering of series and parallel types in the issue graph is reversed - series issues look like parallel and parallel issues look like series. Swap this.

title: Session page route incorrect in breadcrumbs
description: The breadcrumbs shown on the session page show the sessions page instead, and there are a handful of similar cases where the breadcrumbs are incorrect. Please review the breadcrumbs generally and put together a plan to correct them. Consider how they can be structured to reduce manual maintenance.

title: Update the README.md
description: The README.md is quite outdated. It refers to when we were previously using OpenCode as the agent harness. Review and update the content of the README to match the current state. The CLAUDE.md is up to date, this can be used as a reference.

title: Keyboard Navigation Service
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement a Vim-like modal keyboard navigation service for the task graph.

**Service: useKeyboardNavigation hook**
Manages keyboard state, edit modes, and navigation within the task graph.

**Edit Modes (enum):**
1. `Viewing` - Normal navigation mode (default)
2. `EditingExisting` - Editing an existing issue title inline
3. `CreatingNew` - Creating a new issue inline
4. `SelectingAgentPrompt` - Navigating prompt selection dropdown
5. `SelectingMoveTarget` - Selecting target for move operation

**Navigation Controls (Viewing Mode):**
- `j` / `↓` - Move selection down one issue
- `k` / `↑` - Move selection up one issue
- `h` / `←` - Navigate to parent (move right in tree)
- `l` / `→` - Navigate to first child (move left in tree)
- `g` - Jump to first issue
- `G` - Jump to last issue

**Issue Creation:**
- `o` - Create issue below current (enter CreatingNew mode)
- `Shift+O` - Create issue above current

**Issue Editing:**
- `i` - Edit title at beginning (enter EditingExisting mode)
- `a` - Edit title at end
- `r` - Replace title (clear and edit)
- `Enter` - Open full edit page

**Issue Movement:**
- `J` (Shift+j) - Move issue down in sibling order
- `K` (Shift+k) - Move issue up in sibling order

**Agent/Prompts:**
- `e` - Enter SelectingAgentPrompt mode
- `j`/`k` in prompt mode - Navigate prompts
- `Enter` - Accept selected prompt

**Depth Controls:**
- `[` - Decrease depth (show fewer levels)
- `]` - Increase depth (show more levels)

**Search:**
- `/` - Focus search input
- `n` - Next search match
- `N` - Previous search match
- `Enter` (in search) - Embed search
- `Escape` - Clear search

**Type Cycling:**
- `Tab` (viewing mode) - Cycle type: Task → Bug → Feature → Chore
- 3-second debounce per issue

**Other:**
- `Space` - Toggle inline expansion
- `Escape` - Clear selection / collapse / cancel edit / exit mode
- `u` - Undo

**Tasks:**
1. Create useKeyboardNavigation hook with state management
2. Implement edit mode state machine
3. Create useIssueSelection hook for selected issue state
4. Handle keyboard event bubbling and capture
5. Implement scroll-into-view when navigating
6. Create focus management for graph container
7. Add accessibility attributes (aria-activedescendant)
8. Type cycling with debounce

**State Structures:**
- `selectedIssueId` - Currently selected issue
- `editMode` - Current keyboard mode
- `inlineEditState` - Current edit with issue ID, title, cursor position
- `pendingNewIssue` - New issue being created with parent/child info

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Vim-like navigation works smoothly
- Mode transitions are clear and correct
- Focus management works reliably
- Scroll follows selection
- All keyboard shortcuts documented
- No conflicts with browser shortcuts

title: Make logs one line
description:
All logs are currently logged to two lines.

Simplify the log implementation to just single line logs. Show the file name, class and line number where the log is called.

title: Scroll down with selected issues
description: When the selected issue scrolls past the bottom of the screen using the keyboard controls, scroll the window down with it. Add the keyboard shortcuts g and shift+G to select the first and last issues as well.

title: Branch preview for issues with custom ID not using correct ID
description: When an issue has a custom branch ID, it shows the "Branch Preview" correctly in the "edit" page but doesn't show the branch ID correctly in the issue detail panel, instead choosing to show the automatically generated branch ID instead. Fix this.

title: Max turns error caused shutdown of container
description:
During agent execution there was an error logged by the container that resulted in the Claude Code process exiting prematurely. Different errors were shown, however only one container had the error passed through to the Homespun front end.

Error 1:
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_max_turns', is_error=true
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_max_turns'
ERROR [logger.js:26] query forwarder error for session 'd80727c7-49bf-44f8-a362-15452f4ea02d': Claude Code process exited with code 1

Error 2:
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_during_execution', is_error=true
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_during_execution'
ERROR [logger.js:26] query forwarder error for session 'b9f5ea1d-7952-4680-be33-8d4ec2bdc694': Claude Code process exited with code 1
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_during_execution', is_error=true
INFO [logger.js:19] result: subtype='error_during_execution'
ERROR [logger.js:26] query forwarder error for session 'f713a37a-83ba-4377-a89a-de3a1611b8a7': Claude Code process exited with code 1

Please review and diagnose the issue. Refer to the Claude Agent SDK documentation at https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/typescript#sdk-result-message. Fail gracefully with an error message in the chat window, and try to allow reconnection to the same session if possible.

title: Add global prompts page
description: Implement a global prompts management page in the React frontend at /prompts. Add support for a 'None' prompt option that allows users to start agent sessions without an initial prompt. Add visual badges to distinguish between global and project-scoped prompts in the UI.

title: Replace ACA session pools with NFS-mounted worker container apps
description:
## Problem

Azure Container Apps session pools do not support volume mounts (confirmed in the 2025-07-01 API). This prevents agent worker containers from sharing a filesystem with the main application container. Currently:

- The Azure Files SMB mount does not support chmod, breaking both Tailscale state and git clone operations
- Agent workers cannot access cloned repositories on the shared filesystem
- Each worker must clone repos independently, duplicating data and bandwidth

The goal is for the main app and all agent containers to mount the same NFS file share and treat repositories as a local filesystem. Agents work in their own git worktrees, minimising file operation conflicts.

## Solution

Replace ACA session pools with regular container apps for agent workers, backed by Azure Files NFS which supports full POSIX permissions. The worker already manages multiple sessions via HTTP API internally (WorkerSessionService), so a single worker container app can handle concurrent sessions without per-session container isolation.

## Work Breakdown

### Phase 1: Infrastructure - VNet + NFS Storage

The ACA environment requires VNet integration at creation time (cannot be added post-creation), and NFS requires the storage account to be accessible from within that VNet.

1. **Add VNet and subnets to Bicep** - Create a new `network.bicep` module:
   - VNet with address space (e.g., 10.0.0.0/16)
   - ACA infrastructure subnet (e.g., 10.0.0.0/23 - ACA requires minimum /23)
   - Storage private endpoint subnet (e.g., 10.0.2.0/24)

2. **Update environment module** (`modules/environment.bicep`):
   - Add `vnetConfiguration` with infrastructure subnet ID
   - Set `internal: true` for internal-only environment (Tailscale handles external access)

3. **Switch storage to NFS** (`modules/storage.bicep`):
   - Change `enabledProtocols` from `'SMB'` to `'NFS'`
   - Set `supportsHttpsTrafficOnly: false` (NFS requires this)
   - Keep `Premium_LRS` / `FileStorage` (already correct tier)

4. **Add private endpoint for storage** - New `storage-endpoint.bicep` module:
   - Create private endpoint for the storage account on the storage subnet
   - Create private DNS zone for `privatelink.file.core.windows.net`
   - Link DNS zone to VNet

5. **Update environment storage mount** (`modules/environment.bicep`):
   - Change from `azureFile` (SMB with account key) to `nfsAzureFile` (NFS, no key needed)
   - NFS uses network-based auth, so `storageAccountKey` parameter can be removed

### Phase 2: Infrastructure - Worker Container App

Replace the session pool module with a regular container app for workers.

6. **Create worker container app module** (`modules/worker-containerapp.bicep`):
   - Regular `Microsoft.App/containerApps` resource (like main app)
   - Mount the same NFS volume from the environment storage
   - Volume mount path: `/data` (same as main app, shared filesystem)
   - Environment variables: GitHub token, Claude OAuth (from Key Vault secrets)
   - Ingress: internal only (only accessible within the ACA environment)
   - Target port: 8080 (worker HTTP API)
   - Scaling: min 1, max based on `maxConcurrentSessions` parameter
   - HTTP scaling rule based on concurrent requests
   - Include `revisionSuffix` with deployment timestamp (same pattern as main app)

7. **Update main.bicep**:
   - Add network module deployment
   - Pass VNet/subnet IDs to environment module
   - Replace session pool module with worker container app module
   - Pass worker container app FQDN as output
   - Remove session pool outputs, add worker app outputs

### Phase 3: Application - Agent Execution Service

The worker already handles multiple sessions via its HTTP API (POST /api/sessions, /api/sessions/{id}/message, etc). The main change is how the main app connects to the worker.

8. **Create new execution service** or update `AzureContainerAppsAgentExecutionService`:
   - Remove all Azure Management API calls for session pool management (no more creating/deleting pool sessions via PUT/DELETE to the sessions API)
   - Configure worker endpoint as a simple base URL (worker container app's internal FQDN)
   - Route all HTTP/SSE calls directly to the worker container app
   - Session lifecycle: worker manages sessions internally (already implemented in WorkerSessionService)
   - StartSession: POST to `http://{worker-fqdn}/api/sessions`
   - SendMessage: POST to `http://{worker-fqdn}/api/sessions/{id}/message`
   - StopSession: DELETE to `http://{worker-fqdn}/api/sessions/{id}`
   - Health check: GET `http://{worker-fqdn}/api/health`

9. **Update configuration** (`AgentExecutionOptions.cs` and `appsettings.json`):
   - Add `WorkerEndpoint` setting (internal FQDN of worker container app)
   - Remove session pool specific settings (SubscriptionId, ResourceGroup, SessionPoolName)
   - Or keep the existing ACA mode and add a new property for the worker endpoint

10. **Update worker to use shared filesystem**:
    - Configure ClaudeAgentSdk working directory to use the NFS-mounted path (e.g., `/data/src/{project}/{worktree}`)
    - Worker already runs in the same filesystem context as main app
    - Verify git operations work on NFS mount (chmod support)

### Phase 4: Cleanup and Testing

11. **Remove session pool resources**:
    - Delete `modules/sessionpool.bicep`
    - Remove session pool references from `main.bicep`
    - Clean up unused parameters

12. **Update deploy script** (`scripts/deploy.ps1`):
    - Update outputs displayed after deployment
    - Add worker container app URL to outputs

13. **Update start.sh**:
    - Revert Tailscale state to `/data/tailscale` (NFS supports chmod)
    - Or keep on local filesystem if persistence isn't needed

14. **Testing**:
    - Deploy to dev environment
    - Verify NFS mount works (chmod, git clone, git worktree)
    - Verify Tailscale connects
    - Start an agent session and confirm it can read/write to shared filesystem
    - Verify concurrent sessions work correctly
    - Test agent worktree isolation

## Key Decisions

- **Single worker container app** vs per-session containers: Single app is simpler, the worker already handles multi-session. Agents are isolated via git worktrees, not containers.
- **ACA environment recreation**: VNet integration requires recreating the environment. This will cause downtime. Plan for a maintenance window.
- **Internal ingress only**: Both main app and worker use internal ingress. Tailscale provides external access to the main app. Workers are only accessible within the environment.

## References

- Azure Files NFS setup for ACA: https://azureossd.github.io/2025/10/17/Setting-up-a-NFS-volume-with-Azure-Container-Apps/
- ACA storage mounts: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-apps/storage-mounts
- ACA VNet integration: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-apps/vnet-custom
- Session pool API limitation (no volumes): https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/resource-manager/containerapps/container-apps-session-pools/create-or-update?view=rest-resource-manager-containerapps-2025-07-01

title: Optimize Docker build times with shared base image
description:
Both Docker images (Dockerfile and src/Homespun.AgentWorker/Dockerfile) install nearly identical runtime tooling independently - Node.js, gh CLI, Claude Code, Playwright MCP + Chromium, Fleece CLI, Docker CLI. These npm global installs (especially Claude Code with node-pty native compilation, and Playwright MCP + Chromium at ~1GB) are the primary bottleneck. Every code change rebuilds all of this from scratch. The CI/CD pipeline (cd.yml) also has zero caching configured.

Approach: Shared Base Image + CI Caching

1. Create Dockerfile.base - shared tooling image with all common runtime tools (.NET 10 SDK, Node.js, gh CLI, Claude Code, Playwright MCP + Chromium, Fleece CLI, Docker CLI). Use BuildKit cache mounts on npm install steps.

2. Modify Dockerfile - change runtime stage FROM to use base image. Keep only main-app-specific items (Tailscale, user setup, published app copy).

3. Modify src/Homespun.AgentWorker/Dockerfile - same pattern, keep only worker-specific items.

4. Update scripts/run.sh - build base image first before app builds. Docker layer cache makes this instant when base unchanged.

5. Update scripts/run.ps1 - same change for PowerShell.

6. Update .github/workflows/cd.yml - add BuildKit (setup-buildx-action), add build-base-image job, use registry cache (cache-from/cache-to), upgrade build-push-action to v6.

Files to modify: Dockerfile.base (new), Dockerfile, src/Homespun.AgentWorker/Dockerfile, scripts/run.sh, scripts/run.ps1, .github/workflows/cd.yml

Expected impact: Local code-only rebuild ~2-5 min (from ~15-20 min). CI builds with cache hit ~5-8 min (from ~20-30 min). Disk savings ~2.5GB shared vs ~5.5GB duplicated.

title: Show issue title in session card
description: The session cards on the /sessions page show the issue ID in the title rather than the issue title itself. Move the issue ID to the lower section with smaller text, and show the issue title in large text (this should be provided by the server).

title: Add plan indicator to agent status page
description:
When a session is in plan ready mode, it should have an indication on the /sessions page and should be pushed to the top of the list. The order should be:

- Plan ready
- Question
- Waiting
- Working

title: Remaining client fixes
description:
Review and fix the following in Homespun.Web:

- The add issue above and add issue below buttons don't work. They should open the inline issue creator above or below the selected issue
- The selector to change the number of levels displayed in the graph was not ported from the Blazor app
- Add an option to the agent run prompts for "No prompt" - this should open a new session in plan mode without inject a prompt and navigate to the session page immediately.
- Add a dark mode switch to the global settings and default to the system mode.
- Change default selected model to Opus.

title: Remove old monolith project and final cleanup
description:
Remove the old `Homespun` monolith project from the solution and perform final cleanup to ensure the new architecture is clean and complete.

## Steps

### 1. Remove old project
- Remove `src/Homespun/` directory entirely
- Remove `Homespun` project entry from `Homespun.sln`
- Verify no other projects still reference the old `Homespun.csproj`

### 2. Verify solution structure
The final solution should contain:
\`\`\`
Homespun.sln
├── src/
│   ├── Homespun.Shared/          (class library - shared DTOs/contracts)
│   ├── Homespun.Server/          (ASP.NET web API - backend)
│   ├── Homespun.Client/          (Blazor WASM - frontend)
│   ├── Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk/  (class library - Claude SDK C# wrapper)
│   └── Homespun.Worker/          (TypeScript/Hono - agent worker using @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk)
├── tests/
│   ├── Homespun.Tests/           (unit tests)
│   ├── Homespun.Api.Tests/       (API integration tests)
│   ├── Homespun.E2E.Tests/       (browser E2E tests)
│   └── Homespun.Worker/          (Vitest tests for TypeScript worker)
└── infra/                        (Azure Bicep deployment)
\`\`\`

### 3. Verify project references
\`\`\`
Homespun.Server → Homespun.Shared, Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk, Homespun.Client
Homespun.Client → Homespun.Shared
Homespun.Worker → (standalone TypeScript project, no .NET references)
Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk → (standalone .NET class library)
Homespun.Tests → Homespun.Server, Homespun.Shared (and optionally Homespun.Client)
Homespun.Api.Tests → Homespun.Server
Homespun.E2E.Tests → (none - browser tests)
\`\`\`

### 4. Clean up unused code
- Remove any backward-compatibility shims added during migration
- Remove any `// TODO: remove after migration` comments
- Remove any dead using statements
- Ensure no duplicate type definitions exist across projects

### 5. Update documentation
- Update `README.md` with new project structure
- Update `CLAUDE.md` with new development workflow
- Document: how to run the server, how to develop the client, how to build/deploy
- Document the Homespun.Worker TypeScript project's role in agent execution

### 6. Full verification
- `dotnet build Homespun.sln` succeeds with no warnings
- `dotnet test` for all .NET test projects passes
- `npm test` in Homespun.Worker test directory passes
- `docker build` succeeds for both Server and Worker images
- Application runs end-to-end:
  - Server starts and serves WASM client
  - Navigation works
  - API calls work
  - SignalR real-time updates work
  - Mock mode works
  - Session streaming works (Server → Worker → Claude SDK → SSE back to Server → SignalR to Client)
  
### 7. Performance check
- Measure initial page load time (WASM download + initialization)
- Verify SignalR reconnection works after page refresh
- Check that the WASM bundle size is reasonable
- Consider enabling ahead-of-time (AOT) compilation for better runtime performance (can be a follow-up)

title: Test container creation
description: Can you check whether there is a ./scripts/mock.sh script that builds and runs development containers?

title: Sessions List Page
description:
Create the sessions management page for the Homespun.Web project.

**Tasks:**
1. Create page at /sessions
2. Create useSessions hook to fetch all sessions
3. List active and recent sessions in a table with:
   - Session ID
   - Associated issue/PR (with link)
   - Project name
   - Status (active, completed, error)
   - Last activity timestamp
   - Message count
4. Add filter controls:
   - Filter by status (Active, Completed, Error)
   - Filter by project
5. Implement click to navigate to session
6. Add archive/delete actions with confirmation
7. Show real-time status updates via SignalR SessionStatusChanged
8. Add empty state
9. Show archived sessions in separate section or tab

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Sessions list displays correctly
- Filtering works
- Status updates in real-time via SignalR
- Navigation to session works
- Archive/delete actions work

title: Visual Editor
description:
Implement the React visual workflow editor using React Flow.

## Deliverables
1. React Flow canvas integration
2. Custom node components for each node type
3. Node configuration panels
4. Edge connection with DAG validation
5. YAML/code editor mode toggle
6. Save/load workflow functionality
7. Live execution visualization

title: Add issue specific issues agent button and prompt
description:
There is currently a prompt named "IssueModify" in the prompt list, and an "issues agent" button in the toolbar.

Do the following:
- The "IssueModify" prompt should be moved to a separate category with the heading "Issue agent prompts", where there is a fixed single prompt used when running the issue modification agent on an issue, and a fixed system prompt that is used for all issue agents. These can be modified - they are fixed in the sense that there cannot be more than one prompt for each.
- Change the behaviour of the issues agent button in the toolbar to be specific for the selected issue. The modal that pops up should have a text box that allows for instructions to be added in before running the agent (e.g. rename this issue), and should show which issue is selected below this. When the Start Agent button is clicked, this should then start an issue agent, use the IssueModify prompt and replace the placeholders with details of the selected issue and the instructions, and send the prompt to the agent similar to running other agents. If no instructions are provided, just open the session page with no prompt, but include the system prompt.

title: Server A2A Consumer + AG-UI Producer
description:
Server receives A2A events from worker, translates to AG-UI for client.

## Tasks
1. Install AG-UI NuGet package: `dotnet add package Microsoft.Agents.AI.Hosting.AGUI.AspNetCore --prerelease`
2. Create A2A Message Parser (new file: src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Data/A2AMessages.cs)
   - Define C# records: A2AMessage, A2ATask, A2AArtifact
   - Define events: A2ATaskStatusUpdateEvent, A2ATaskArtifactUpdateEvent
3. Create AG-UI Event Emitter (new file: src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AGUIEventService.cs)
   - A2A Message → AG-UI TextMessageStart/Content/End
   - A2A TaskStatusUpdateEvent (working) → AG-UI RunStarted
   - A2A TaskStatusUpdateEvent (completed) → AG-UI RunFinished
   - A2A TaskStatusUpdateEvent (input-required for question) → AG-UI Custom event 'QuestionPending'
   - A2A TaskStatusUpdateEvent (input-required for plan) → AG-UI Custom event 'PlanPending'
4. Update DockerAgentExecutionService to parse incoming SSE as A2A events
5. Update ClaudeCodeHub to emit AG-UI events

## Files to Modify
- src/Homespun.Server/Homespun.Server.csproj
- src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Data/A2AMessages.cs (new)
- src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Data/AGUIEvents.cs (new)
- src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/AGUIEventService.cs (new)
- src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/DockerAgentExecutionService.cs
- src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Hubs/ClaudeCodeHub.cs

title: Allow function keys to pass through on projects screen
description: Hitting the function keys while on the task graph doesn't do anything because they are being blocked by the keyboard controls. Allow the function keys to pass through to the browser (e.g. F5 to reload, F12 for browser dev tools, etc.)

title: Tab/Shift+Tab closes inline issue creation input
description:
When creating a new issue using keyboard controls (o/O), pressing Tab or Shift+Tab to change the lane position closes the inline input instead of adjusting the lane.

## Root Cause
In InlineIssueCreateInput.razor, the Tab key is handled but the browser's default Tab behavior is not prevented. This causes focus to move away from the input, triggering HandleBlur which cancels the issue creation.

## Expected Behavior
- Tab should increase the lane offset (making new issue a parent of adjacent issue)
- Shift+Tab should decrease the lane offset (making new issue a child of adjacent issue)
- The inline input should remain focused and visible

## Actual Behavior
- Tab/Shift+Tab causes the input to lose focus
- HandleBlur is triggered, canceling the creation
- No lane adjustment occurs

## Fix Required
Add @onkeydown:preventDefault for Tab keys OR call e.preventDefault() in the JavaScript interop for Tab key handling in InlineIssueCreateInput.razor.

## Related Tests
E2E tests in IssueHierarchyCreationTests.cs document this bug and will fail when fixed.

title: Fix run panel
description:
When I click on the run button in the React client the application a modal panel opens but it doesn't have anything in it for me to select or a button to run. It should have the ability to select the prompt, model and base branch.

This should also be implemented in the edit issue panel, if I choose to save and run the issue - currently the save and run button doesn't do anything.

Use the same component for both for the sake of consistency.

In the edit issue panel, also add the mini prompt button for generating the branch ID, similar to the Blazor client.

title: Remove quick issue creation bar
description: With the creation of the new toolbar buttons and inline issue creation for creating new issues on mobile, remove the quick issue creation bar completely from the repository as it is no longer needed.

title: Fix session message caching
description: Session message caching was added in PR #128. This doesn't work as expected, I believe due to the session messages not being persisted to a location that is accessible to the container. These messages should be stored in /data/sessions/{project id}/{session id}.json. Could you please update this, then run a new test container with ./scripts/mock.sh? Use the API to start a new session with an example prompt and test how the session messages are extracted to a file.

title: Exception is thrown when branches page is open
description: When I open the branches page on a project, the following error is logged to the browser console. Review the root cause of this exception and fix it.

title: Change background colour of tool calls and branch table headings
description:
The background colour of in-session tool calls when the window is in dark mode is the same as the chat window background colour. Change the shade such that it is readable. Also update the rounding of the corners so multiple tool calls stack together nicely and form one large chat bubble.

The branch table headings on the branch page are also white when viewed in dark mode. Make them appropriate to the mode.

title: Clear context when executing  plan
description:
In the Claude Code CLI, after a plan has been created there are some options provided to commence implementation of the plan. One of these is to clear context and start implementing. When the plan is read and displayed in the Homespun application, an option to clear context and start implementation should be shown below the displayed plan. When clicked, this should be performed by in the same session window.

In doing this, the application should take the read plan file and send it as the next message after clearing the session context. Refer to the question UI implementation for how buttons should appear in the chat.

title: PR and issue linking doesn't work
description: Currently PRs and issues are not being linked together. When a PR is created with a branch matching that of an issue. The issue should have the PR added to it's "linked PRs" property. Issues with linked PRs should not be shown in the UI but the information should instead be shown alongside the PR summary when the PR is clicked. This linking should occur on project refresh when new PRs are synced and downloaded.

title: Update all frontend issues
description:
We are in the process of creating a new React based web UI for the Homespun application to replace the Blazor issues. There are a series of issues associated with this one (siblings and parents) that contain instructions, however many of them don't specifically call out the Homespun.Web project. Please update the issues as to include this reference to the overall project. There are also a series of Phase issues that describe the work of the child issues. Instead of this description, I want to use these issues as a final way of checking that everything in the child issues was completed. Update the descriptions to list the child issues and prompt an agent to confirm that all components have been completed and are correctly wired together.

Only make these changes for issues that are still open.

title: Handle post-merge testing
Fix CD action
description: The recent commit to main didn't trigger the CD workflow which should be triggered on all pushes to main to build a new working container. Review this.

title: Create temporary file system and use real files as test fixture
description: Replace in-memory mock services with real file-based services that operate on a temporary folder structure. This will allow E2E tests to exercise the full stack while maintaining test isolation.

title: Update issue service mock implementation with full persistence
Add prompt "code" mode
description:
In the prompts pages (both the local and global pages), add a tab that switches between the normal prompts view (with the prompts listed in cards) and a "code" mode that shows a text box with the prompts serialized as raw JSON. I want to be able to use this to be able to "import" and "export" all prompts quickly.

There should be an apply button at the bottom that checks that the JSON is valid and then updates the prompts, and a revert button that deserializes the prompts again and undoes and changes in the text box.

title: Add file name and PR search
description:
When the user types @ or # into the chat box or description for editing an issue, this should bring up a modal window that allows the user to browse the repository files (with @) or cached PRs (with #).

The repository file search should be a simple text match of the file names, and the PRs should be similar. A list of matches from best to worst with a limit of 20 should be shown. When OK or enter is pressed, this should add "@{file path from repo root}" or "PR #{number}" to the prompt at the location where the key was first pressed.

The list of files and list of PRs should be asynchronously loaded into the client on page load and cached, and the search should happen on the client. The cache should be hashed to check when it should be updated by the server (which should also maintain a hash of the content).

title: Fix issue diff view in issue agents
description: In the sessions page for issue agents there is a button that says "view changes". When clicked, this adds /issue-diff to the route and should take the user to another page that shows the changes to the issues between the graph shown on the main project page and the issues that are in the issue agent cloned branch. However, when clicked there is no redirection and the diff is not shown. Please review and fix this to show the issue page.

description:
The mini prompt service seems to be failing due to rate limiting issues. Please investigate. The logs are below:

title: Restart container on sessions
description: When an agent enters an error state, there should be an option to restart the worker container on the same session. This button should stop the worker container, create a new worker container and start it on the same session (having persisted the Claude Session ID from the container). Provide a /test/error endpoint to the worker containers so that we can force an error and have the server restart the container with the same session.

title: Side panel and tool expansion doesn't work
description: When loading the session page, an unhandled exception occurs in the side panel (Issue/PR/To-do's/Files area): 'InvalidCharacterError: Failed to execute setAttribute on Element: @onclick is not a valid attribute name.' This crashes the Blazor Server circuit, breaking all interactive functionality on the page. The error occurs consistently on every session page load. The @onclick directive is being rendered as a literal HTML attribute instead of being processed by Blazor's render tree, likely in a component that renders raw HTML or uses MarkupString incorrectly.

title: Containers page doesn't show correct session
description: The linked session link shown on the container page does not have the correct ID to allow it to navigate to the current session page. Review the way that a session ID is assigned to a container to ensure that it corresponds with the session IDs that are used to show sessions in the Homespun app. The word "conversation" may also be used to distinguish the difference between a session operating on an issue and a session in the Claude Code sense of the word. I would suggest instead just using the issue ID for sessions as we want to maintain a "one clone and one agent container per issue" policy, with a GUID used only if the agent is not operating on an issue (e.g. it is operating directly on a branch).

title: Fix thinking messages from AG-UI
description:
When thinking messages are streamed in, they tend to be streamed in at the top, just under the chat message. Instead of this, there should be a thinking style message that is appended between tool usages. It should be open when first streamed in and then collapsed when the next tool messages come in.

Fixed by:
1. Sorting content blocks by Index when rendering to ensure correct order
2. Auto-collapsing thinking blocks when a new tool starts streaming
3. Auto-collapsing thinking blocks when a tool block is received

title: Fix graph display
description:
The graph display in the React client has the following issues when compared to the Blazor client:

- The lines from the series and parallel execution modes are swapped: series should come out of the bottom of the node but instead come out of the right side.
- Sometimes complete and closed issues are displayed when they shouldn't be. Carefully review the way that the issues are fetched from the server and how they are processed by the client in comparison between the two clients. When I look at the same issue in the Blazor client and the React client the status and type don't necessarily match. Ensure that the server is being used as the single source of truth.
- The type and status badges should open drop downs when clicked that allow these values to be changed on the fly.

title: Improve logging
description: Logs in the server are currently written in a two line format. Change this so that all log entries are written to one line, including the line number, class that the log is coming from and level of the log.

   at Homespun.Client.Pages.Design.DesignIndex.OnInitializedAsync()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.ComponentBase.RunInitAndSetParametersAsync()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.RenderTree.Renderer.GetErrorHandledTask(Task , ComponentState )

Please review and fix.

title: Mini agent prompts should run in Local Agent Execution mode by default
description: Mini agent prompts should run in the same process as the server and not spin up a new container as the memory usage is likely very small. The only current example of the mini agent prompts is the working branch ID generator, but the overall infrastructure for mini prompts should use this local execution pattern.

title: Make session control consistent
description: The session control differs between the agents page on the project and the global agents page. It should just be a single Blazor component that is used on both pages. The only real difference between the two pages is that one is filtered for just the agents related to a project and the other has all agents but is grouped by project.

title: Plans not available after context cleared
description: Agents are able to write plans successfully to the .claude folder, and tend to write these plans to files at "/home/homespun/.claude/plans/cheeky-stirring-stonebraker.md". They can then successfully read these plans back within the same session. However, if I clear their context and then have them execute these plans again, they fail to find these plans. Is this due to the agent containers holding the plans locally and it being cleared when the agents are being restarted? What are my options to fix this?

title: Add rules for e2e test creation to CLAUDE.md
Render full graph edges when in insert mode
description:
Currently the display of the graph edges when new issues are being created don't match the condition that they're being added - they always show a line coming out the top of the node when (for example) if they are being added between two siblings they should show a line from both the top and bottom.

The graph rendering algorithm should treat these "new" issue nodes that haven't yet been created exactly the same as the other issues, with the exception that an editing control is shown instead of the issue title etc.

Consider refactoring the graph rendering algorithm and Blazor component to treat the new draft issues as being normal issues, and render the edges as they will be after the issue is created.

title: In-session prompts don't inject issue context
description: When selecting a prompt from the list in a session, the issue context isn't injected into the placeholders when it is copied into the chat input. Update this so that it is.

title: Make pull button show background progress
description: When the pull button at the top of the page is clicked it doesn't appear that anything happen. Use a Blazor Blueprint loading symbol while all changes are being pulled.

title: Loading States and Skeletons
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Add comprehensive loading states throughout the application.

**Tasks:**
1. Create reusable skeleton components for:
   - Project cards
   - Issue list rows
   - PR cards
   - Session messages
   - Detail panels
2. Replace all loading spinners with contextual skeletons
3. Add React Suspense boundaries at route level
4. Implement optimistic updates for:
   - Issue creation
   - Issue updates
   - Status changes
5. Add error states with retry mechanisms:
   - Network errors
   - API errors
   - Timeout errors
6. Create error fallback component for error boundaries
7. Add loading indicators for mutations

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- All loading states use appropriate skeletons
- Content doesn't jump/shift on load
- Errors can be retried
- Optimistic updates feel instant
- Failed mutations show clear error feedback

title: Chat window doesn't show messages
description: The chat window in the React Homespun.Web client doesn't show the message text in the responses - it shows up as empty little empty bubbles, with one per message and tool call. Update this such that at least the message text is being rendered to the screen.

title: Combine tool use messages
description: Tool use messages are currently shown one after another as separate messages, and there is a "flat" tool display showing the name of the tool and a clickable tool display showing the results. To consolidate this in the session UI, make all tools that come one after another just stack tool executions into one chat bubble if they are subsequent to one another, and only show one row per tool execution (that can be expanded to show the result).

title: Show timelines from different branches
description: The app currently only shows the issues and timeline from the main branch. Instead of this, it should be able to show the issues and timelines from all branches - as Fleece is used for storing issues and these issues as JSONL in the repository itself, it is possible for different branches to contain different issues. Show a branch symbol next to issues that have associated branches created in the repository, and when clicked the page should change the timeline to show the issues as they are stored in that branch. This should be a new route in the repository. There should be a button at the top of the timeline to then return back to the default branch.

title: Use general model versions
description:
A specific model version is being passed in on SDK initialisation, as can be seen in the worker logs:

```
SDK init: permissionMode='bypassPermissions', model='claude-opus-4-5-20251101'
```

This would seemingly start the agent with an older model, as Opus 4.6 is now available. The model passed in should always just be 'opus', 'sonnet' or 'haiku' to allow Claude Code to select the latest version. There are also some messages that show just 'opus', so it is possible that the correct model is being passed in:

```
POST /sessions - mode=Build, model=opus, workingDirectory=/workdir, resumeSessionId=none
```

However, generally speaking just fix this such that 'opus' is always used without versions to be on the safe side.

title: Implement SignalR client integration for Blazor WASM
Add more issue information to the sessions page
description:
The sessions page for the Homespun.Web React client should be updated to a card based interface rather than a table. This should also include more issue information like the title, agent status and the existence of a PR.

Refer to the implementation in the Blazor client.

title: Fix PR status
description: The PR status is always shown as "Checks running". There was a previous PR that attempted to fix this but it didn't quite work. Please review again and let me know if there are any logs that you think would be worthwhile checking in this process.

title: Add buttons to issue rows for editing issues and starting agents
description:
In the issue rows on the project page, add inline buttons to edit the issue and run agents on the right hand side. These should show up on hover and selection only.

The run agent should open a context panel that contains the agent control panel (i.e. the ability to start an agent with a particular prompt)

title: Allow dev and release containers on the same machine
description: It should be possible to run a dev and a release container on the same machine. Dev containers should have the hostname `homespun-dev` and be run using the latest local build. Production containers should use watchtower to pull from the GitHub container registry and have `homespun` as the hostname. They should both be run from the run.sh and run.ps1 scripts. I believe that this is already possible, it just needs to be able to inject the hostname into the Docker configuration.

title: Set up Grafana MCP
Fix branch name when branches are created
Add execution history and logging
description:
Track workflow execution history for debugging and analytics.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowHistoryService.cs`

## Features
1. **Execution Records**
   - Store in `.workflows/executions_*.jsonl`
   - Record: start time, end time, status, trigger info
   - Node-level timing and status

2. **Execution Logs**
   - Structured logging for each node
   - Store agent session IDs for reference
   - Error details for failed nodes

3. **History Queries**
   - List executions for workflow
   - Filter by status, date range
   - Get detailed execution with node history

4. **Cleanup Policy**
   - Configurable retention period
   - Archive old executions
   - Delete context files after retention

## API Endpoints
- `GET /api/workflows/{id}/executions` - List executions
- `GET /api/executions/{id}` - Get execution detail
- `GET /api/executions/{id}/logs` - Get execution logs

title: Improve e2e testing
description: The children of this issue consist of tasks that were intended to change the mock server (run with ./scripts/mock.sh) to use local files instead of in-memory issues, and to add a mock agent that provides simple answers back to the server. This means that almost everything (except GitHub) could be tested in the e2e tests. Confirm that this has been implemented and is the case by inspecting the files created by the mock.sh and whether they are edited when the e2e tests are run.

title: Show issue and PR details in session window
description:
The session window should show the related issue and PR details in the window. Create a side panel on the right hand side of the window for these details. There should be two tabs in this panel:

1) Issue - show the related issue
2) PR - show the related PR information
3) To-do's - show the to-do's for the current session
4) Files - show the files that have changed in this branch across the commits since it diverged from it's parent.

Use a TDD approach and ask any clarifying questions you may have.

title: Pull and sync button
description:
There is a split button in the Blazor application that allows the user to pull recent changes from the git repository to main and merge the fleece issues, and to optionally commit and push the new fleece issues as a "sync" operation.

This button hasn't been added to the React application, please add it in, in-line with the main project title on the projects page. This should be mostly implemented in the backend already, except for maybe the graph refreshing behaviour.

title: Show PR status in graph where linked
description:
Show the PR status in the issue graph for linked PRs:
- Red if tests have failed
- Grey if no tests have been run
- Orange if there are merge conflicts
- Yellow if tests are running
- Green if all tests pass and PRs are mergeable.

This status should be asynchronously loaded and does not need to be persisted. This status should be polled by the server and sent to the client through SignalR updates (with trace logging as a maximum) along with the polling for merged PRs and new open PRs. Polling should occur only for open PRs.

title: Add single container mode for mini prompts and execution mode
description: Add a worker sidecar container that runs alongside the main Homespun container to handle mini prompts. Route mini prompt requests to the sidecar via HTTP instead of using the ClaudeAgentSdk C# library.

title: Allow differentpermissions setting in session chat window
description: Currently there are no settings to change the permissions provided to Claude when a message is sent. There should be a dropdown box allowing a user to select between 'Bypass permissions', 'Accept edits', 'Plan' and 'Default'.

title: Consolidate UI
description:
The UI is currently scattered within various individual pages, and some components exist as separate files while others are embedded in pages. This is making it difficult to manage UI quality and consistency.

Please review the current pages and styling and put together a plan for consolidating this as much as possible by extracting reusable components.

Add tailwind to the project and use this as a centralised styling system. A guide to using tailwind with Blazor may be found here: https://github.com/tesar-tech/BlazorAndTailwind?tab=readme-ov-file. Keep the existing styles (colours, fonts, etc.) as much as possible.

title: Add default filters
description: The filters shown for the issue graph should default to showing "next" issues and issues that are assigned to the current user. When the 'f' key is pressed, the caret in the filter box should be placed at the end of the text box for adding more filters.

title: Sync button should reload all issues from disk
description: Occasionally the app will keep an old state of fleece issues, particularly when the issues on disk change due to an agent action or pushes from another branch. The sync button on the agents page should always fully reload the fleece issues from disk (merging if necessary) to fully reload the issues state.

title: Tool view changes aren't implemented
description: When a session is being run it still shows the raw tool output as JSON. I was under the understanding that this was largely fixed in PR #84. Please review the PR and the current status of the application, using `./scripts/mock.sh` to run the application and the Playwright MCP tools to navigate the page. Do not kill any processes or containers to run the application. You may need to add additional test data to show the tool behaviour. Create a plan to fix this.

title: PR checks don't show merge status
description: PR checks now correctly show whether checks are passing or not, but doesn't show whether the PR can be merged (i.e. if there are merge conflicts). Update this to be included in the PR status check

title: Add arbitrary environment variables to worker
description: I would like to be able to add additional environment variables to the worker containers when they are started such as license keys etc. Add a secrets management page for each project that allows keys to be stored locally per project (in a secrets.env in the project folder, next to the .clones and main folder). These can then be injected when the container is started.

title: Clean up prompt management system
Inline Issue Details
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement inline detail rows for viewing issue/PR details within the task graph.

**NOTE: This replaces the original 'side panel' concept. The Blazor implementation uses inline expandable rows, not a separate panel.**

**Primary Component: InlineIssueDetailRow**
Expands below the issue row when selected and triggered by Space key or double-click.

**Tasks:**
1. Create InlineIssueDetailRow component showing:
   - Issue ID, Type badge, Status badge, Priority badge
   - Branch name with copy button
   - Parent issue links (clickable navigation)
   - Description (markdown rendered)
   - Linked PR status badges
   - Active session info (if agent running)
   - Session cache history
2. Create InlinePrDetailRow for merged/closed PRs showing:
   - PR number, title, state badge
   - Description (markdown rendered)
   - Commits list
   - Related issues
3. Expand/collapse triggers:
   - Space key when row is selected
   - Double-click on row
4. State management for expanded rows
5. Smooth expand/collapse animation
6. Close on Escape or clicking elsewhere

**IssueRowActions (hover actions):**
- Edit button (navigate to full edit page)
- Open Panel button (for mobile full-width view)
- Run Agent button with dropdown launcher

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Space/double-click expands inline details below selected row
- All issue metadata displayed clearly
- Markdown description renders correctly
- PR status badges show checks/approval state
- Actions (Edit, Run Agent) work correctly
- Escape or click elsewhere closes expansion

title: Add Markdown rendering to PR details
Open sessions at bottom of chat
description: When a session page is opened, it should show the chat screen scrolled all the way to the bottom of the chat. It should auto scroll to the bottom of the chat screen when it is scrolled to the bottom, but if not it should just remain as is and show the "scroll down" button that is currently shown.

title: Create client-side HTTP service layer for Blazor WASM
UI fixes
Show closed PRs as branched off
description: In the 'merged PRs' section at the top of the projects page, the closed PRs should be shown as branched off the main vertical line (i.e. one lane to the right, with e a connector).
