title: Create Homespun.Server ASP.NET minimal API project
description:
Create the new `Homespun.Server` project that will be the ASP.NET backend, hosting all server-side logic, API endpoints, and SignalR hubs. This project replaces the current `Homespun` web project's backend role.

## What to create

### Project setup
- Create `src/Homespun.Server/Homespun.Server.csproj` as a `Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web` project targeting `net10.0`
- Add to `Homespun.sln` under the `src` solution folder
- Add ProjectReference to `Homespun.Shared` and `Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk`
- Add NuGet references: `Fleece.Core`, `Markdig`, `Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Client`, `Microsoft.Data.Sqlite`, `Octokit`, `Swashbuckle.AspNetCore`

### What goes in this project

**Program.cs** - New minimal API startup:
- All DI service registration (from current Program.cs lines 21-260)
- Configure CORS to allow the Blazor WASM client origin
- Map all API controllers
- Map SignalR hubs (`/hubs/notifications`, `/hubs/claudecode`)
- Map health check endpoint
- Serve the Blazor WASM client as static files (via `app.UseBlazorFrameworkFiles()` and `app.MapFallbackToFile("index.html")`)
- Swagger/OpenAPI configuration
- Data protection configuration
- Mock mode support

**Controllers** - Move all 7+ API controllers as-is:
- `SessionsController`
- `ProjectsController`
- `PullRequestsController`
- `WorktreesController`
- `IssuesController`
- `NotificationsController`
- `OrchestrationController`

**SignalR Hubs** - Move both hubs:
- `ClaudeCodeHub` → Implement `IClaudeCodeHubClient` from Shared
- `NotificationHub`

**All Feature Services** - Move complete Features/ directory structure:
- ClaudeCode services (ClaudeSessionService, AgentExecutionService, etc.)
- Git services (GitWorktreeService, MergeStatusCacheService, etc.)
- GitHub services (GitHubService, GitHubClientWrapper, etc.)
- Fleece services (FleeceService, FleeceIssueTransitionService, etc.)
- Gitgraph services (GraphService, GraphCacheService, etc.)
- Projects services (ProjectService)
- PullRequests services (PullRequestWorkflowService, PullRequestDataService)
- Notifications services (NotificationService)
- AgentOrchestration services (MiniPromptService, BranchIdGeneratorService)
- Commands (CommandRunner)
- Testing/Mock services
- Shared services (MarkdownRenderingService)

**Data layer** - Move all data access:
- `JsonDataStore` and `IDataStore`
- `MessageCacheStore`, `SessionMetadataStore`, `ClaudeSessionStore`
- All data file I/O

**Configuration** - Move:
- `appsettings.json`, `appsettings.Mock.json`
- `launchSettings.json`
- `start.sh` (container entrypoint)

## Agent execution and Homespun.Worker integration
- Agent execution services in the server orchestrate work by communicating with the **Homespun.Worker** (TypeScript/Hono) containers via HTTP
- The server does NOT run Claude Code sessions directly — it delegates to Homespun.Worker containers that use the official `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` TypeScript SDK
- `DockerAgentExecutionService` spawns Homespun.Worker containers and communicates via HTTP/SSE
- `AzureContainerAppsAgentExecutionService` manages Homespun.Worker instances on Azure
- The `Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk` C# library provides the SDK client interface used by the server to interact with Workers

## Key decisions
- The server project does NOT contain Blazor components or Razor pages
- No `AddRazorComponents()` call - this is a pure API server
- CORS must be configured to allow the WASM client to make requests
- The server hosts the WASM client's files via `UseBlazorFrameworkFiles()`/`MapFallbackToFile`
- Service interfaces that the client needs (for strongly-typed HTTP clients) should reference types from Homespun.Shared only
- No reference to Homespun.Worker is needed in the .csproj — communication is over HTTP at runtime

## Verification
- Project compiles independently
- All API endpoints are accessible via Swagger
- SignalR hubs accept connections
- Health check endpoint works

title: Phase 6: Settings & Configuration
description:
## Verification Checklist

This phase implements settings pages and configuration management in the **Homespun.Web** project (`src/Homespun.Web`).

**Child Issues:**
- [ ] **s6ex0j** - Global Settings Page - GitHub auth, Git config, app settings
- [ ] **n6hQSe** - Project Prompts Tab - Manage agent prompts
- [ ] **TqnqwI** - Project Secrets Tab - Manage environment variables/secrets

**Agent Verification Instructions:**
When all child issues are complete, verify the following in `src/Homespun.Web`:
1. Confirm the Global Settings page is accessible at `/settings`
2. Verify GitHub authentication status displays correctly
3. Confirm Git configuration shows current user values
4. Verify Project Prompts tab allows CRUD operations on agent prompts
5. Check that prompts appear in agent launcher dropdown
6. Confirm Project Secrets tab allows adding/deleting secrets with masked values
7. Verify secret values are never exposed in the UI after saving

Mark each child issue checkbox above as verified, then mark this phase complete.

title: Update sync box to just a sync button
description:
The sync "box" on the projects page that contains the "sync fleece" and "sync github" should be updated to just be a manual "sync" button that replaces the "refresh" link at the top of the page.

This should just perform the fleece sync as this also syncs the main branch, as well as perform the PR sync for open PRs only. This should all be done asynchronously.

title: When selected issue changes while agent is starting, incorrect message is sent
description: When the 'start' agent button is clicked, it takes a few seconds for the agent to start. If the selected issue is changed during this time, the correct branch is cloned and the agent is started, but the message from the new selected issue is sent to the agent. Fix this such that the correct message is always sent from the issue that the agent is started on.

title: Add WorkflowBranch MCP tool
description:
Add WorkflowBranch MCP tool for conditional branching based on agent decisions.

## Files to Modify
- `src/Homespun.Worker/src/tools/workflow-tools.ts`
- `src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/session-manager.ts`

## Tool Schema
\`\`\`typescript
WorkflowBranch: {
  description: "Signal a conditional branch decision",
  input_schema: {
    type: "object",
    properties: {
      decision: { type: "string", description: "Branch path to take" },
      reason: { type: "string", description: "Explanation for decision" },
      confidence: { type: "number", minimum: 0, maximum: 1 },
      data: { type: "object", description: "Data to pass to branch" }
    },
    required: ["decision", "reason"]
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Server-Side Handling
- Match decision value against edge conditions
- Only activate matching outgoing edges
- Pass data to active branches
- Error if no matching edge found

title: Update application config to launch React web client
description: Review the Tailscale and Docker Compose setup to expose the React web client at the root HTTPS address. Use port 3200 for exposing the original Blazor web application.

title: Different base worktrees
description:
The system currently uses the default branch as the base for all new branches on issues that are created. Instead of this, I would like to be able to use other selected branches as the base and create stacked PRs.

Add a base branch selection control to the new agent start controls. This can be automatically set to the default branch.

title: Plan Approval UI
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement the plan approval interface for when the agent exits plan mode (ExitPlanMode tool).

**Background:**
When the Claude agent uses the ExitPlanMode tool after creating a plan, the UI must display the plan approval interface with three options. This mirrors the functionality in the Blazor client.

**Data Models (from Homespun.Shared):**
```typescript
interface AGUIPlanPendingData {
  planContent: string    // The plan markdown content
  planFilePath?: string  // Optional file path where plan was written
}
```

**SignalR Events:**
- Receive: `AGUICustomEvent` with `name="PlanPending"` containing `AGUIPlanPendingData` payload
- Receive: `SessionStatusChanged` with `hasPendingPlanApproval=true`
- Send: `ApprovePlan(sessionId, approved, keepContext, feedback?)` to respond

**Three Approval Options:**
1. **Clear Context & Start Implementation** - Approve the plan, clear conversation context, start fresh agent session
   - Calls `ApprovePlan(sessionId, true, false, null)`
2. **Continue with Context** - Approve the plan, keep existing conversation context
   - Calls `ApprovePlan(sessionId, true, true, null)`
3. **Reject & Modify** - Reject the plan, provide feedback for revision
   - Shows textarea for feedback
   - Calls `ApprovePlan(sessionId, false, false, feedback)`

**Tasks:**
1. Create PlanApprovalPanel component:
   - Header with "Plan Ready for Implementation" title and icon
   - Description text explaining the options
   - Three action buttons with distinct styling:
     - Primary button: "Clear Context & Start Implementation"
     - Default button: "Continue with Context"
     - Danger/destructive button: "Reject & Modify"
2. Create rejection feedback form:
   - Textarea for user feedback
   - "Send Feedback" button
   - Only visible after clicking "Reject & Modify"
3. Create useApprovePlan mutation hook:
   - Send approval via SignalR `ApprovePlan` method
   - Handle the three different approval modes
   - Handle loading and error states
4. Handle SignalR PlanPending event:
   - Parse AGUIPlanPendingData from AGUICustomEvent
   - Store plan content and file path in session state
   - Update session status to WaitingForPlanExecution
   - Set hasPendingPlanApproval flag
5. Display plan content (optional):
   - Show plan markdown using PromptKit Markdown component
   - Collapsible section to view full plan
6. Handle ContextCleared event:
   - Show notification when context is cleared
   - Reset relevant UI state
7. Display plan approval UI inline in the chat message stream

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Plan approval panel displays when AGUICustomEvent with PlanPending is received
- Three distinct action buttons are available
- "Reject & Modify" reveals feedback textarea
- Approval/rejection is sent via SignalR correctly
- Session status changes appropriately (WaitingForPlanExecution → Running)
- UI clears after successful approval/rejection
- Plan content can optionally be viewed

title: Branch icon doesn't show merge status in PR badge
description: The branch icon next to the PR link in the issue graph is always green regardless of the merge conflicts status. Review this and fix it such that it is red if there are merge conflicts for that PR, information that should be fetched from GitHub rather than determined locally.

title: Migrate backend services from monolith to Homespun.Server
description:
Move all backend service implementations from the current `Homespun` monolith project into `Homespun.Server`. After this task, the monolith's Features/ directory and backend logic should live entirely in the server project.

## Services to migrate (by feature)

### ClaudeCode Feature
- `ClaudeSessionService` / `IClaudeSessionService`
- `ClaudeSessionStore` / `IClaudeSessionStore`
- `ClaudeSessionDiscovery` / `IClaudeSessionDiscovery`
- `SessionMetadataStore` / `ISessionMetadataStore`
- `MessageCacheStore` / `IMessageCacheStore`
- `SessionOptionsFactory`
- `ToolResultParser` / `IToolResultParser`
- `HooksService` / `IHooksService`
- `AgentStartupTracker` / `IAgentStartupTracker`
- `AgentPromptService` / `IAgentPromptService`
- `TodoParser` / `ITodoParser`
- Agent execution services: `DockerAgentExecutionService`, `AzureContainerAppsAgentExecutionService`, `LocalAgentExecutionService`, `IAgentExecutionService`

**Note:** Agent execution services communicate with **Homespun.Worker** (TypeScript/Hono) containers over HTTP/SSE. The server orchestrates agent sessions by spawning Worker containers and polling their REST API. The `Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk` C# library provides the client interface for this communication.

### Git Feature
- `GitWorktreeService` / `IGitWorktreeService`
- `MergeStatusCacheService` / `IMergeStatusCacheService`
- `RebaseAgentService` / `IRebaseAgentService`

### GitHub Feature
- `GitHubService` / `IGitHubService`
- `GitHubClientWrapper` / `IGitHubClientWrapper`
- `GitHubEnvironmentService` / `IGitHubEnvironmentService`
- `IssuePrLinkingService` / `IIssuePrLinkingService`
- `IssuePrStatusService` / `IIssuePrStatusService`
- `GitHubSyncPollingService` (hosted service)

### Projects Feature
- `ProjectService` / `IProjectService`

### PullRequests Feature
- `PullRequestWorkflowService`
- `PullRequestDataService`
- `FeatureService`

### Data layer
- `JsonDataStore` / `IDataStore`

### Fleece Feature
- `FleeceService` / `IFleeceService`
- `FleeceIssueTransitionService` / `IFleeceIssueTransitionService`
- `FleeceIssuesSyncService` / `IFleeceIssuesSyncService`
- `IssueSerializationQueueService` / `IIssueSerializationQueue`

### Gitgraph Feature
- `GraphService` / `IGraphService`
- `GraphCacheService` / `IGraphCacheService`
- `GraphBuilder`, `TimelineSvgRenderer`, `TimelineLaneCalculator`

### Notifications Feature
- `NotificationService` / `INotificationService`

### Navigation Feature
- `BreadcrumbService` / `IBreadcrumbService` (NOTE: this may become client-only)

### AgentOrchestration Feature
- `MiniPromptService` / `IMiniPromptService`
- `BranchIdGeneratorService` / `IBranchIdGeneratorService`

### Commands Feature
- `CommandRunner` / `ICommandRunner`

### Shared Feature
- `MarkdownRenderingService` / `IMarkdownRenderingService`

### SignalR Feature
- `SignalRUrlProvider` / `ISignalRUrlProvider`
- `SignalROptions`

### Testing Feature
- `MockDataStore`, `MockClaudeSessionService`, `MockDataSeederService`
- `JsonlSessionLoader`
- All mock service implementations

## Migration approach
1. Copy the entire Features/ directory to Homespun.Server/Features/
2. Update namespaces from `Homespun.Features.*` to `Homespun.Server.Features.*`
3. Update service interfaces that reference shared types to use `Homespun.Shared.*` namespaces
4. Keep service interfaces that are server-internal (not needed by client) in the Server project
5. Move `Program.cs` DI registration code to the server

## Key decisions
- Service interfaces that the client needs to know about (for HTTP client wrappers) remain in Shared as marker interfaces or are defined fresh there
- Server-only interfaces (ICommandRunner, IClaudeSessionDiscovery, etc.) stay in Server
- The `IBreadcrumbService` may need to be reimplemented client-side since it tracks navigation state
- Agent execution services remain in the server — they orchestrate Homespun.Worker containers but do not need a .csproj reference to Homespun.Worker (communication is HTTP-based at runtime)

## Verification
- Server project builds
- All API endpoints work
- SignalR connections work
- Mock mode still functions
- Existing unit tests can be pointed at the new project and pass

title: Search doesn't work
description: The  leece search tool no longer works, stating Error: Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'source').

title: Review large number of changes shown in diff panel
description: When I start a new issues agent even without any changes, the branch is cloned and a large number of differences are shown (mostly a lot of creations) even though the fleece issues have just been copied and should be identical. Add an integration test that shows this issue occurring.

title: Continue existing sessions
Create and edit shortcut
description:
Currently hitting the ENTER key in the quick issue creation bar will create a new issue, but the "create and edit" button must be clicked on. Please change this such that SHIFT+ENTER will activate the "create and edit" button and open the edit window.

Add small icons to the buttons to describe this.

title: Fix hardcoded colors in NotificationBanner component
description: Replace hardcoded hex colors in NotificationBanner.razor.css with CSS variables. Add new CSS variables for notification hover states: --notification-info-hover, --notification-warning-hover, --notification-action-hover. Update app.css with these new tokens.

title: Make filters bar part of toolbar and restore float
description:
When there is a filter applied to the issues, it shows up as a separate toolbar below the main project toolbar. This was introduced in PR #535 It should instead sit within the same toolbar, just as a separate row that expands and collapses when the filter button is clicked.

The toolbar used to float above the issues as well but no longer does. Fix this. Remove the default filter that is applied when the page is opened - it should be unfiltered. Add a button next to the ? button that applies these default filters with the square-user Lucide icon.

title: New issues created with Tab/Shift+Tab don't establish parent-child relationships
description:
When creating a new issue using keyboard controls with Tab/Shift+Tab lane modifiers, the parent-child relationships are not being established correctly.

## Expected Behavior

### When creating BELOW selected issue:
- Tab (+1 lane): New issue should become PARENT of the selected issue (issue above)
- Shift+Tab (-1 lane): New issue should become CHILD of the selected issue (issue above)

### When creating ABOVE selected issue:
- Tab (+1 lane): New issue should become PARENT of the selected issue (issue below)
- Shift+Tab (-1 lane): New issue should become CHILD of the selected issue (issue below)

## Current Status
This bug cannot be tested until PUgrNi (Tab closes input) is fixed.

## Implementation Notes
- The lane offset is tracked in InlineIssueCreateInput.razor
- The CreateIssueAsync call needs to include the parent/child relationship based on lane offset
- For Series execution mode, sort order should also be considered

## Test Coverage
E2E tests in IssueHierarchyCreationTests.cs include tests for relationship verification that will execute once PUgrNi is fixed.

title: Ensure that stopping sessions kills container
description:
When sessions are stopped the parent container that they were running in is not necessarily killed. This should always happen when the "stop session" button is pressed, both the button in the session and in the sessions list window.

Currently only killing the containers from the container window stops the Docker containers.

title: Migrate standard details tools from PyRevit to Taipan
description:
There are some tools used to manage standard details models in Revit in the repository at https://github.com/Northrop-Digital/nr-standard-details. These have been written as PyRevit scripts. Clone them to a temporary folder that is ignored in the .gitignore (e.g. /.tmp) and migrate them into Revit commands. Use the existing commands in the folder as a guide. 

Create a Taipan configuration file in the %APPDATA%/Northrop/Applications.Taipan as a feature flag for this tool - it should be installed with Taipan but the buttons should only be visible for a certain group of maintenance users.

title: Details not refreshed when issues are edited
description: When an issue is edited it doesn't immediately show the new issue details in the detail panel, and occasionally when issues are updated only a portion of the description is persisted.

title: Update graph rendering
description:
Make the following updates to the graph rendering on the project page.

- Merged and closed PRs are not shown in the task graph. They should be shown at the top, above the "Issues" divider. Only the most recent 5 should be shown, with a button for loading 5 more PRs at the very top of the list.
- Add markers for PR and agent status to each issue in the graph. If a PR is linked to an issue, a PR badge should show up with the PR number, that when clicked takes the user to GitHub. The agent status should show whether the agent is currently working. This has been implemented in previous versions of the graph rendering component but has since disappeared, review the codebase to port this behaviour over and clean up the old graph display.
- Add a marker showing the type of the issue.
- Show all merged PRs and issues with circles. Issues without descriptions should be hollow circles and issues with descriptions should be solid circles. The colour of the circle should be based on the agent status.

title: Add agents for modifying issues
description:
For the Homespun.Web React client, I would like to add an agent button on the project page (next to the push button). This should run an agent that can modify the fleece issues based on instructions from the user.

It should do the following:

- Open a small popup with a prompt input and run button.
- Persist any changes that have been made in the Fleece issues to the main branch without committing them.
- Create a new clone with branch name `hsp/agent+{shortened timestamp}` from main that includes the unstaged fleece issue changes in the main repository folder.
- Start a new agent with system instructions that tell the agent not to write any files in the repository and only to make modifications using the Fleece CLI tool and the user's instruction. If this was run from the toolbar with a selected issue, that issue's ID should be included in the system prompt. This should open a session much like any other agent.
- In a session window that has been created using this method, there should be a tab that shows the issue diff (which should be available in the Fleece.Core library) and two buttons that say "Accept changes" and "Cancel".
- If "Accept changes" is clicked, the issues should be merged with the issues in the main branch and be persisted again (without commit), then the session should be stopped and the browser redirected to the projects page.
- If "Cancel" is clicked the session should be stopped and the changes discarded.

title: Add more issue information to the sessions page
description:
The sessions page for the Homespun.Web React client should be updated to a card based interface rather than a table. This should also include more issue information like the title, agent status and the existence of a PR.

Refer to the implementation in the Blazor client.

title: Test additional permutations of new issue hierarchy creation
description:
I want to add additional tests for the creation of new issues in the graph view to ensure that they are added in the correct location in the hierarchy. Currently, new issues are added correctly when added as parents to existing issues or when they are added between two issues that are in series.

See below for documentation on the expected behaviour and tests to be added. Add this to the docs/ folder as well.

# Task Graph Issue Insertion Behaviour

## Purpose

This document specifies how new issues are inserted into the task graph dependency diagram. It is intended for test writers implementing tests against `KeyboardNavigationService` and `TaskGraphView`.

**Source files:**
- `src/Homespun.Client/Services/IKeyboardNavigationService.cs` — `PendingNewIssue` model (properties below)
- `src/Homespun.Client/Services/KeyboardNavigationService.cs` — `CreateIssueBelow`, `CreateIssueAbove`, `IndentAsChild`, `UnindentAsSibling`, `GetInheritedParentInfo`, `AcceptEditAsync`
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Requests/IssueRequests.cs` — `CreateIssueRequest` fields sent to the API
- `tests/Homespun.Tests/Services/KeyboardNavigationServiceTests.cs` — existing test patterns

## Diagram Legend

All diagrams use a text-based notation to represent the task graph:

| Symbol | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `o` | An issue node |
| `\|` | A vertical edge connecting a child (above) to a sibling or parent (below) |
| `+--o` | A right-angle connector from a child chain to a parent node |
| `x--o` | Same as `+--o` but indicates the new issue is being inserted into this edge |
| blank line | A gap between unrelated issues (no parent-child relationship) |

Children render **above** their parents in the task graph. Lane 0 is the leftmost column (leaf/child nodes); higher lanes are further right (parent/root nodes). "Above" and "below" in this document always refer to **visual position** on screen (render order), not hierarchy level.

## Keyboard Controls

| Key | Effect | Mode Transition |
|-----|--------|-----------------|
| `o` | Create new issue **below** the selected issue | `Viewing` → `CreatingNew` |
| `Shift+O` | Create new issue **above** the selected issue | `Viewing` → `CreatingNew` |
| `TAB` | Make the new issue a **parent** of the reference issue | Stays in `CreatingNew` |
| `Shift+TAB` | Make the new issue a **child** of the reference issue | Stays in `CreatingNew` |
| `Enter` | Accept the new issue (calls `AcceptEditAsync`) | `CreatingNew` → `Viewing` |
| `Escape` | Cancel and discard the pending issue | `CreatingNew` → `Viewing` |

The **reference issue** is the issue that was selected when `o` or `Shift+O` was pressed.

## Code Properties Quick-Reference

When a new issue is created, the `PendingNewIssue` properties determine what gets sent to the API in `CreateIssueRequest`:

| Action | `PendingChildId` | `PendingParentId` | `InheritedParentIssueId` | API: `ChildIssueId` | API: `ParentIssueId` | API: `ParentSortOrder` |
|--------|-------------------|--------------------|--------------------------|-----------------------|-----------------------|------------------------|
| Default (no TAB) | `null` | `null` | From `GetInheritedParentInfo` | `null` | = `InheritedParentIssueId` | = `InheritedParentSortOrder` |
| TAB | = `ReferenceIssueId` | `null` | `null` (cleared) | = `PendingChildId` | `null` | `null` |
| Shift+TAB | `null` | = `ReferenceIssueId` | `null` (cleared) | `null` | = `PendingParentId` | `null` |

**`GetInheritedParentInfo` logic:** Looks up the reference issue's parent. If the reference issue has a parent, the new issue inherits that parent (sibling creation). If the reference issue has no parent, returns `null` for both fields (orphan/standalone creation).

All issues in this document are drawn as "series" issues. For "parallel" issues the same parent/child rules apply — only the sort order computation differs.

---

## 1. Default (Sibling) — no TAB / Shift+TAB

New issue is created at the same hierarchy level as the reference issue. The new issue inherits the reference issue's parent (if any) via `GetInheritedParentInfo`.

### 1.1 Between two children

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
| <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 1, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 2
o Child issue 2
|
+--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

```
o Child issue 1
|
o New issue
|
o Child issue 2
|
+--o Parent issue
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentSortOrder` = midpoint between Child issue 1 and Child issue 2 sort orders

### 1.2 Between a child and parent

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
| <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 2, or `Shift+O` above Parent issue
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
o New issue
|
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentSortOrder` = computed sort order after Child issue 2

### 1.3 Between an orphan and a child hierarchy

This depends on whether the new issue is created below the orphan or above the child.

#### Location

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
  <-- insert here: `Shift+O` above Child issue 1, or `o` below Next issue
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 1

The reference issue is Child issue 1, which has Parent issue as its parent. `GetInheritedParentInfo` returns Parent issue. The new issue becomes a sibling of Child issue 1 under Parent issue.

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)

o New issue
|
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Parent issue"

#### Expected result — `o` below Next issue

The reference issue is Next issue, which has no parent. `GetInheritedParentInfo` returns `null`. The new issue is an orphan.

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)

o New issue (orphan)

o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = `null`
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = `null`

### 1.4 After a parent and before an orphan

The reference issue determines the behaviour. The parent issue has no parent of its own, so whether inserting below it or above the orphan, the new issue is an orphan.

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
      <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue, or `Shift+O` above Next issue
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
```

#### Expected result

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue

o New issue (orphan)

o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = `null` (Parent issue has no parent; Next issue has no parent)
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = `null`

### 1.5 Between two adjacent hierarchies

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1
  <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue 1, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1
o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

#### Expected result — `o` below Parent issue 1

The reference issue is Parent issue 1. Parent issue 1 has no parent, so `GetInheritedParentInfo` returns `null`. The new issue is an orphan.

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1

o New issue (orphan)

o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = `null`
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = `null`

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1

The reference issue is Child issue 2.1. Its parent is Parent issue 2, so `GetInheritedParentInfo` returns Parent issue 2. The new issue becomes a sibling of Child issue 2.1 under Parent issue 2.

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1

o New issue
|
o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.InheritedParentIssueId` = "Parent issue 2"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Parent issue 2"

---

## 2. TAB (Become Parent) — new issue becomes parent of reference

Pressing `TAB` after `o`/`Shift+O` sets `PendingChildId = ReferenceIssueId`. The new issue becomes a **parent** of the reference issue. All previously inherited parent info is cleared.

### 2.1 Between two children

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|     <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 2, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 3
o Child issue 3
|
+--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

The new issue becomes the parent of the reference issue. This breaks the relationship between Parent issue and all children above the insertion point (tree behaviour).

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
+--o New issue

o Child issue 3
|
+--o Parent issue
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.PendingChildId` = reference issue ID (Child issue 2 or Child issue 3)
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = reference issue ID
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = `null`

### 2.2 Between a child and parent

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|     <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 2, or `Shift+O` above Parent issue
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

The new issue becomes parent of the reference issue. Parent issue loses its children; the new issue inherits them.

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
+--o New issue

o Parent issue
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.PendingChildId` = reference issue ID
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = reference issue ID
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = `null`

### 2.3 Between an orphan and a child hierarchy

#### Location

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
  <-- insert here: `Shift+O` above Child issue 1, or `o` below Next issue
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 1 + TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` Creating a parent above a root-level child makes no sense — the new issue would be inserted between two unrelated hierarchies with no children of its own in that context.

#### Expected result — `o` below Next issue + TAB

The new issue becomes parent of Next issue.

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
|
+--o New issue

o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingChildId` = "Next issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = "Next issue"

### 2.4 After a parent and before an orphan

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
      <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue, or `Shift+O` above Next issue
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
```

#### Expected result — `o` below Parent issue + TAB

The new issue becomes parent of Parent issue.

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
   |
   +--o New issue (parent of Parent issue)

o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingChildId` = "Parent issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = "Parent issue"

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Next issue + TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` Creating a parent above a root-level orphan with no children makes no structural sense.

### 2.5 Between two adjacent hierarchies

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1
  <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue 1, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1
o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

#### Expected result — `o` below Parent issue 1 + TAB

The new issue becomes parent of Parent issue 1.

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1
   |
   +--o New issue (parent of Parent issue 1)

o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingChildId` = "Parent issue 1"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = "Parent issue 1"

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1 + TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` The new issue would be inserted between two unrelated hierarchies. Child issue 2.1 already has a parent (Parent issue 2), and the new issue would have no children in this context.

---

## 3. Shift+TAB (Become Child) — new issue becomes child of reference

Pressing `Shift+TAB` after `o`/`Shift+O` sets `PendingParentId = ReferenceIssueId`. The new issue becomes a **child** of the reference issue. All previously inherited parent info is cleared.

### 3.1 Between two children

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|     <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 2, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 3
o Child issue 3
|
+--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

The new issue becomes a child of the reference issue.

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
o New issue
|
+--o Child issue 3
   |
   +--o Parent issue
```

#### Assertions

- `PendingNewIssue.PendingParentId` = reference issue ID (Child issue 2 or Child issue 3)
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = reference issue ID
- `CreateIssueRequest.ChildIssueId` = `null`

### 3.2 Between a child and parent

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|     <-- insert here: `o` below Child issue 2, or `Shift+O` above Parent issue
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` The parent cannot have a child two levels down — the new issue would need to become a grandchild, which is not supported by a single Shift+TAB operation.

### 3.3 Between an orphan and a child hierarchy

#### Location

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
  <-- insert here: `Shift+O` above Child issue 1, or `o` below Next issue
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
```

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 1 + Shift+TAB

The new issue becomes a child of Child issue 1.

```
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)

o    New issue (child of Child issue 1)
|
+--o Child issue 1
   |
   o Child issue 2
   |
   x--o Parent issue
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingParentId` = "Child issue 1"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Child issue 1"

#### Expected result — `o` below Next issue + Shift+TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` It doesn't make sense in context to the orphan Next issue — the new issue would become a child of an orphan, but the visual position (between two unrelated groups) is ambiguous.

### 3.4 After a parent and before an orphan

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue
      <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue, or `Shift+O` above Next issue
o Next issue (orphan, no parent)
```

#### Expected result — `o` below Parent issue + Shift+TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` There could be many levels to travel down from Parent issue.

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Next issue + Shift+TAB

The new issue becomes a child of Next issue.

```
o Child issue 1
|
o Child issue 2
|
x--o Parent issue

o New issue (child of Next issue)
|
+--o Next issue
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingParentId` = "Next issue"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Next issue"

### 3.5 Between two adjacent hierarchies

#### Location

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1
  <-- insert here: `o` below Parent issue 1, or `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1
o Child issue 2.1
|
+--o Parent issue 2
```

#### Expected result — `o` below Parent issue 1 + Shift+TAB

**Blocked (no-op).** `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` There could be many levels to travel down from Parent issue 1 — the system cannot determine the correct depth.

#### Expected result — `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1 + Shift+TAB

The new issue becomes a child of Child issue 2.1.

```
o Child issue 1.1
|
o Child issue 1.2
|
x--o Parent issue 1

o New issue (child of Child issue 2.1)
|
+--o Child issue 2.1
   |
   +--o Parent issue 2
```

**Assertions:**
- `PendingNewIssue.PendingParentId` = "Child issue 2.1"
- `CreateIssueRequest.ParentIssueId` = "Child issue 2.1"

---

## 4. Edge Cases

| Scenario | Expected Behaviour |
|----------|-------------------|
| Empty graph (no issues) | `CreateIssueBelow`/`CreateIssueAbove` returns immediately (`SelectedIndex < 0`) — no issue created |
| Single issue in graph | Normal behaviour — `o` creates at index 1 (end), `Shift+O` at index 0 (start) |
| TAB then Shift+TAB (switching) | Mutually exclusive — Shift+TAB overwrites TAB state. No way to return to default sibling mode without `Escape` + restart |
| Pressing TAB twice | Idempotent — `PendingChildId` is set to the same reference issue ID again |
| Escape during creation | Clears `PendingNewIssue`, returns to `Viewing` mode, no API call |
| Empty title then Enter | `AcceptEditAsync` returns without creating, stays in `CreatingNew` mode |
| Series vs Parallel parents | Same parent/child rules apply; only difference is sort order computation for sibling creation |

---

## Summary of Blocked Scenarios

All "blocked" scenarios mean the key press is a **no-op** — nothing happens. The code does not currently implement blocking validation in `IndentAsChild()` or `UnindentAsSibling()`. These are marked `[NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]` throughout this document. Test writers should write TDD-style failing tests for these scenarios.

| Section | Scenario | Reason |
|---------|----------|--------|
| 2.3 | `Shift+O` above root child + TAB | New issue would have no children |
| 2.4 | `Shift+O` above orphan + TAB | New issue would have no children |
| 2.5 | `Shift+O` above Child issue 2.1 + TAB | New issue would be between unrelated hierarchies with no children |
| 3.2 | Both directions + Shift+TAB | Cannot create grandchild in one operation |
| 3.3 | `o` below orphan + Shift+TAB | Ambiguous context between unrelated groups |
| 3.4 | `o` below parent + Shift+TAB | Many levels to travel down |
| 3.5 | `o` below Parent issue 1 + Shift+TAB | Many levels to travel down |

title: Type change menu doesn't appear when badge is clicked
description: The type change menu (where the issue type can be changed after an issue's type badge is clicked) doesn't open when the type badge is clicked. Add comprehensive test coverage for the type change menu functionality in TaskGraphView component to show that it doesn't appear, then try to fix this.

title: Use cards for sessions page
description:
The sessions page for the Homespun.Web React client should be updated to a card based interface rather than a table. This should also include more issue information like the title, agent status and the existence of a PR.

Refer to the implementation in the Homespun.Client Blazor client and duplicate it in the React client.

title: Fix loading of past pull requests
description: The past pull requests are not being loaded in when the PRs are being refreshed. There should have been some tracking implemented where merged and closed PRs are tracked when the PRs are loaded into the cache - the PRs that were registered as "open" but are no longer in the "open" list that is pulled from GitHub should be checked to determine whether they are "closed" or "merged" and the cache updated as such. However, it seems that this was being done before but has stopped working, as the PRs that are in the cache show the merged PRs only up to a certain date and newer merged PRs are not being shown.

title: Remove worktree references
description: Remove the use of 'worktree' and just refer to branches, as the worktrees have been switch to full clones of the repository in PR #189

title: Agent Launcher and Controls
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement agent spawning and control UI with PromptKit status components.

**PromptKit Components to Use:**
- `Loader` - Loading/spinner indicators for agent status
- `ThinkingBar` - Display "Agent working..." state with stop action
- `TextShimmer` - Animated text for status display

**Install Commands (if not already installed):**
```bash
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/loader.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/thinking-bar.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/text-shimmer.json"
```

**Tasks:**
1. Create AgentLauncher component for issue detail panel:
   - Agent type/prompt selector (dropdown of available prompts)
   - Model selector (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku)
   - Start button with Loader during agent initialization
2. Create useStartAgent mutation hook
3. Create AgentStatusIndicator component using PromptKit:
   - ThinkingBar for "Agent working..." state with `onStop` callback
   - Loader component for different states (starting, running, waiting)
   - Token count display
   - Duration since start
4. Create header status indicator:
   - Badge showing count of active agents
   - TextShimmer effect when agents are actively processing
   - Click to navigate to sessions list
   - Real-time updates via SignalR AgentStatusChanged
5. Implement stop/pause agent actions:
   - Use ThinkingBar's `onStop` prop for inline stop action
   - Separate stop/pause buttons in AgentControlPanel
6. Create AgentControlPanel for detailed agent management:
   - Current task display
   - Stop/Pause/Resume buttons
   - Token usage statistics
   - Loader variants for different states
7. Handle agent start on both issues and PRs

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Agents can be started on issues with selected prompt
- Status displays correctly using PromptKit Loader and ThinkingBar
- ThinkingBar shows during active agent processing with stop action
- Header shows active agent count with TextShimmer effect when active
- Agents can be stopped via ThinkingBar or control panel
- Model selection persists

title: Add ARIA labels to interactive chat and form components
description: Add missing ARIA labels for accessibility: ChatInput.razor (aria-label on textarea and send button), AgentSelector.razor (aria-label and aria-busy), ToolUseBlock.razor (aria-hidden on emoji icon). Ensure screen readers can properly announce these elements.

title: Implement AgentNodeExecutor
description:
Create the agent node executor for running Claude agents within workflows.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/NodeExecutors/INodeExecutor.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/NodeExecutors/AgentNodeExecutor.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/NodeExecutors/NodeExecutorFactory.cs`

## Features
1. **Prompt Templating**
   - Support \`{{variable}}\` substitution from context
   - Include issue context if configured
   - Include file contents if configured (glob patterns)

2. **System Prompt Building**
   - Inject workflow context documentation
   - Document available workflow tools
   - Add custom append/override text

3. **Agent Session Integration**
   - Use existing IAgentExecutionService
   - Set workflow context on session start
   - Handle WorkflowComplete/WorkflowBranch tool results
   - Support Plan/Build/Review modes

4. **Timeout and Retry**
   - Per-node timeout configuration
   - Retry on transient failures

## Pattern Reference
Follow `src/Homespun.Server/Features/ClaudeCode/Services/ClaudeSessionService.cs` for agent session patterns

title: Move branch delete confirmation within branch
description:
On the worktree page, when deleting a branch the confirmation dialog appears at the bottom of the page. Move this such that it appears next to the delete button within the branch itself.

The branches and worktrees are also currently displayed as a series of "cards". Instead of this, make this into tables with columns that display all the relevant information and actions that are in the cards.

title: Add undo feature
description:
Add the ability to undo and redo changes to the issues list. Whenever any changes are made to issues (i.e. adding and editing them), the issues list should be asynchronously persisted in the projects/{project}/history/ folder as timestamped JSONL files using the Fleece library serialization capabilities. An undo and redo button should be added to the project page UI to allow the user to go backwards and forwards in the history, updating the graph view as required. The current timestamp that is being used for the issues should also be saved such that the application is aware where in the history the user is. If the history is at a previous timestamp to the latest one and any changes are made, this should result in all of the history between the "active" timestamp and the latest timestamp being deleted, and a new timestamp added at the end of the history.

Note that merging issues should be considered as an item in the history.

title: Prevent multiple concurrent agents
description: It's currently possible to run multiple concurrent agents on the same issue. This should be prevented by having the agent execution service check whether there are any agents running on an issue. If so the run button should change to an open button to open the relevant session page.

title: Remove linting from CI
description: Linting failures are blocking CI constantly. Remove this from the CI pipeline, and add a new workflow for checking if there were any changes in the last day, then running the automatic lint fixes every day. This should be followed by running the Typescript unit tests and pushing to main if successful.

title: CloneManagementPanel doesn't refresh session status via SignalR
description:
The CloneManagementPanel component displays session status badges in the branch table, but does not subscribe to SignalR events. The session status won't update when a session stops or changes status.

Issues:
- Does NOT implement IAsyncDisposable
- Does NOT have SignalR subscription
- Session status badges in branch rows won't update in real-time

This is the same bug pattern that was fixed in IssueDetailPanel (PR #222).

Fix: Add SignalR subscription to refresh session status when events are received.

title: Split into Blazor WASM and ASP.NET server
description:
There have been ongoing issues with managing the complexity of interactivity with this Blazor SSR Interactive application - a better result will be achieved using Blazor WASM for a front-end and a standard minimal API ASP.NET server application. This will also allow more careful separation of backend and frontend concerns than has currently been done.

The plan is to split the current monolithic Homespun Blazor Server application into:
1. **Homespun.Shared** - Shared DTOs, models, interfaces, and constants used by both client and server
2. **Homespun.Server** - ASP.NET minimal API server with all backend services, SignalR hubs, and data access
3. **Homespun.Client** - Blazor WASM standalone client consuming the server API via HTTP and SignalR

Key principles:
- Keep DTOs and shared contracts in Homespun.Shared (referenced by both projects)
- Server owns all business logic, data access, external integrations (GitHub, Claude SDK, Git, Fleece)
- Client is a pure UI layer using HttpClient and SignalR HubConnection to communicate with server
- Server serves the WASM client as static files
- Existing Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk (C# class library) and Homespun.Worker (TypeScript/Hono agent worker) projects remain unchanged

This parent issue contains child tasks that should be executed in series.

title: Fix session mode button and model selector
description:
Clicking on the session mode button in the session chat window or using the SHIFT+TAB shortcut doesn't show any change in the session mode in the UI, and doesn't appear to pass the message on to the server or worker.

When changing the model selector it changes just below the slab title in the UI, but the server says "Failed to set model on worker for session 25e2c246-b0e9-47bf-b8a5-a5a1f3290a34".

This relates to the UI changes that were merged in PR #508 and #503.

title: Stop agent completions from redirecting browser
description: When agents complete their work they will redirect the page to the session window. Please stop this behaviour and add an agent status window showing the number of working and waiting agents in the top bar of the application. When clicked, this should redirect to the agent sessions page.

title: Remove watchtower
description: Remove watchtower from all build scripts and container build - it's not required anymore as we will be using Azure Container Apps for most of the production deployment.

title: Project Detail Shell
description:
Create the project detail page shell with tabbed navigation.

**Tasks:**
1. Create ProjectLayout component with tabs:
   - Issues (default tab)
   - Branches
   - Prompts
   - Secrets
   - Settings
2. Implement tab routing using TanStack Router nested routes
3. Create project header with:
   - Project name
   - Breadcrumb navigation (Home > Project Name > Tab)
   - Quick actions dropdown (Edit, Delete, etc.)
4. Set up useProject hook for fetching project details
5. Create placeholder Outlet content for each tab
6. Add loading state while project loads
7. Handle project not found (404)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Tabs switch content based on URL
- Project name displays in header
- Breadcrumbs show current location
- Tab state persists in URL (e.g., /projects/123/branches)
- Default tab is Issues when navigating to /projects/123

title: Running agents on open PRs doesn't create worktree
description: Running agents on open PRs switches the main branch worktree to the PR instead of creating a new worktree. Please investigate and fix this - it should either create a new worktree or if one already exists in that location it should use that folder and pull the most recent changes.

title: Change issue rendering to differentiate between series and parallel issues
description:
The graph rendering currently shows the edges coming out of the right side of each node and then turning down to reach the parent node below. This is representative of the "parallel" execution mode (i.e. the parent is rendering in parallel) but shows up regardless of whether the parallel or series execution mode is selected.

Update this such that it only renders this way for parallel execution, and for series execution the edges come out of the bottom of the node. They should go straight down into their sibling or if the next node down is their parent they should go down first then right into the side of their parent.

Note that the execution mode drawing I am talking about is governed by the parent, not the execution mode of the node in question: execution mode signifies whether a node's children should be executed in parallel or series.

title: Fix issue graph scrolling
description: In the Homespun.Web React client, the issue titles in the task graph only have a few characters visible on mobile screens. Instead of this, allow the task graph to scroll horizontally to be able to see the full title. This should be similar to the toolbar, which can current scroll horizontally. The tabs at the top of the project page should also scroll horizontally.

title: Ensure fleece prime hook is running for issues
Allow multi-user usage
Add keyboard navigation
description:
Add basic Vim-like keyboard navigation to the application. The following keys should be used:

- `j` or `up` - down one issue regardless of the hierarchy.
- `k` or `down` - up one issue regardless of the hierarchy.
- `h` or `left` - to a child of the current issue if it exists.
- `l` or `right` - to the parent of the current issue if it exists.

I want to change the new issue creation system from the current UI of the creation bar at the bottom of the screen. Instead of this, creating and editing issue titles should happen in-line in the graph using the normal Vim editing keys:

- `i` - opens the title of the selected issue for editing, starting at the start of the issue.
- `a` - opens the title of the selected issue for editing, starting at the end of the issue.
- `r` - deletes the entire issue name and activates the editing text box. The user should be prevented from saving an empty title.
- `escape` - cancels the creation/edit.
- `enter` - accepts the creation/edit.
- `o` - creates a new issue below the current one. If at the root, just creates a new issue. If there is a parent issue in serial execution mode, places the new issue in the correct sort order (after the `enter` key is pressed)
- `shift+O` - creates a new issue above the current one
- `tab` - when edit mode is enabled (i.e. either when creating an issue or editing an existing one), makes the new/edited issue a parent to the issue above it (if there is one). Can only be pressed a maximum of one time depending on the surrounding issues.
- `shift+TAB` - when edit mode is enabled makes the new/edited issue a child to the issue below it (if there is one). Can only be pressed a maximum of one time depending on the surrounding issues.

The editing can just be a standard text box, it doesn't need to be a modal editor/follow Vim rules. The text box should be dominant - remove the status and type dropdown boxes and default new issues to 'task' and 'open'.

We will continue to add more keyboard commands and update the UI so ensure that the architecture created for keyboard control is flexible. We will also add additional controls for mobile later - keep the creation bar at the bottom of the page for mobile screen sizes.

The application should start with the first 'next' issue "selected".

title: Claude Code sometimes kills session shell when trying to test mock container
description: When Claude Code uses mock.sh to run the application and then tries to close the browser, it inadvertently ends the Claude Code process by killing the wrong shell.

title: Add workflows
description:
Implement an autonomous workflow system for Homespun that enables complex multi-step agent-driven processes with:

- **DAG-based execution** with parallel nodes and conditional branching
- **MCP tool integration** for strongly typed data passing between workflow stages
- **Visual + code editing** for workflow definitions
- **In-repository storage** (JSONL in `.workflows/`)

## Node Types
- **Agent nodes**: Run Claude agents with prompts (plan, build, review modes)
- **Gate/approval nodes**: Wait for human approval before continuing
- **Action nodes**: Direct API calls (create PR, merge, deploy, notify)
- **Transform nodes**: Process data without agents (parse, filter, aggregate)

## Triggers
- Manual start
- Event-driven (issue created, PR merged, webhook)
- Scheduled (cron-like)

## MCP Tools for Data Passing
Agents call workflow-specific MCP tools to pass data back to the orchestrator:
- `WorkflowComplete`: Signal completion with typed outputs
- `WorkflowBranch`: Signal conditional decision
- `WorkflowPassData`: Store intermediate data
- `WorkflowGetContext`: Read data from previous nodes

title: Issue syncing often fails
Add simple issue tree view
description:
I want to try using traditional hierarchical tree view of the issues to see if this is more appropriate. Add a button that switches the view mode to the toolbar. Instead of the current task graph view that shows the next issues at the root and their parents as leaves, flip this to show the parent issues on the left side and their children progressing down and to the right. Still show the graph nodes and edges but matching the new tree structure.

If a node has multiple parents, just show it once when it is first traversed in the tree.

Remove the merged and closed pull requests from the top of the graph in this view mode.

title: Add stop button to session window
description:
The session window should include a stop button that stops the session in the same way that the stop button on the sessions page does. This should be located at the top right.

The title in the session window also gives the full ID of the session: this should be changed to be the name of the issue the session is related to.

title: Fix issue panel on sessions page
description: The tabs in the issue panel don't change when clicked - only the 'Issue' tab is shown (which contains the correct details) but the PR details, to-do's etc. don't show when the tab is clicked. The standard tab UI (as per the projects page) should also be used here for consistency. This is related to PRs #199 and #197, both of which did not fix this issue.

title: Update test projects for new architecture
description:
Update all test projects (Homespun.Tests, Homespun.Api.Tests, Homespun.E2E.Tests) to work with the new multi-project architecture. The Homespun.Worker TypeScript tests are independent and should continue working as-is.

## Homespun.Tests (Unit Tests)

### Project reference changes
- Remove reference to old `Homespun` project
- Add reference to `Homespun.Server` (for testing server-side services)
- Add reference to `Homespun.Shared` (for shared types)
- Update `InternalsVisibleTo` in `Homespun.Server.csproj` to include `Homespun.Tests`

### Namespace updates
- Update all `using Homespun.Features.*` to `using Homespun.Server.Features.*`
- Update all `using Homespun.Components.*` references if any bUnit tests exist

### bUnit component tests
- These currently test Blazor Server components. They need to be updated to test the WASM client components instead
- Change project reference from Server to Client for component tests
- If both server and client tests are needed, consider splitting into separate test projects or using conditional compilation
- Update service mocks: instead of mocking `IProjectService`, mock `HttpProjectApiService` or use a mock `HttpClient` via `HttpMessageHandler`

### Service unit tests
- These should largely work as-is since they test service implementations that moved to `Homespun.Server`
- Update namespaces
- Verify all test fixtures still resolve correctly

## Homespun.Api.Tests (API Integration Tests)

### Project reference changes
- Change `WebApplicationFactory<Program>` to reference the new `Homespun.Server` project
- The `Program` class now lives in `Homespun.Server`
- Update `InternalsVisibleTo` in `Homespun.Server.csproj` to include `Homespun.Api.Tests`

### Test updates
- Update namespace references
- Verify all existing API tests still pass (the endpoints should be identical)
- Add new tests for CORS headers if needed
- Add tests verifying the WASM static file serving works

### WebApplicationFactory configuration
\`\`\`csharp
public class HomespunApiFactory : WebApplicationFactory<Homespun.Server.Program>
{
    protected override void ConfigureWebHost(IWebHostBuilder builder)
    {
        builder.UseEnvironment("Testing");
        // Configure test services
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Homespun.E2E.Tests (End-to-End Tests)

### Changes
- These tests use Playwright to interact with the browser - they should work largely unchanged since the URL structure stays the same
- Update the `HomespunFixture` to start `Homespun.Server` instead of the old `Homespun` project
- The server now serves the WASM client, so page navigation should work the same way
- May need to add waits for WASM initialization (the app takes longer to load initially compared to Blazor Server)
- Consider adding a loading/splash screen check

### Potential issues
- **Initial load time**: Blazor WASM has a longer initial load than Server. E2E tests may need increased timeouts or explicit waits for WASM initialization
- **WebSocket connections**: Playwright may need to wait for SignalR connections to be established before interacting with real-time features

## Homespun.Worker Tests (TypeScript - Vitest)
- The `tests/Homespun.Worker/` test suite (81 Vitest tests) is independent of the .NET project split
- These tests should continue to pass without modification
- Ensure CI continues to run `npm test` in the Worker test directory

## New test considerations

### Client-side integration tests
Consider adding a new test project `Homespun.Client.Tests` for:
- bUnit tests for WASM components
- Testing HTTP service classes with mocked HttpClient
- Testing SignalR service classes with mocked HubConnection

### Test data
- Existing test data in `tests/data/sessions/` should remain compatible
- Mock mode should still work via the Server project

## Verification
- All unit tests pass: `dotnet test tests/Homespun.Tests/`
- All API tests pass: `dotnet test tests/Homespun.Api.Tests/`
- All E2E tests pass: `dotnet test tests/Homespun.E2E.Tests/`
- All Worker tests pass: `npm test` in `tests/Homespun.Worker/`
- Test coverage is maintained or improved

title: Fix page width on mobile
description: The page width on mobile is being stretched to show the full width of the item description, which allows both horizontal and vertical scrolling. Instead of this, the width should be restricted to the width of the screen. Vertical and horizontal scrolling should be in place just for the issue graph, with the top bar and bottom create issue control fixed in place at full width.

title: Custom agents
description:
The agents page should be renamed to "sessions", with "agents" then being used for managing custom agents prompts that can be started.

These custom agents prompts for now should just be the build and plan prompts that are currently available. The prompts themselves can be saved in the homespun-data.json file. These are global settings that affects all projects.

Instead of the "start planning" and "start building" button, there should be a drop-down to select between the various agent prompts, and a start button to start the agent. Also add a "none" option to the button, which just starts an agent in a worktree with no prompt at all.

It should be possible to start agents on PRs as well as on issues. This should check whether the PR branch already has a worktree. If not, it should create a new worktree and start the agent. If it does have a worktree, ensure there are no current agents sessions running in that worktree, then pull recent changes to that worktree and start the agent.

Use a TDD approach in development.

title: Placeholders for past PRs
description: Past PRs don't show up at all until they pop in and move all of the current items. Please update this such that there are placeholders for 5 PRs that are the same size as the PRs will be when they are loaded.

title: Fix inline agent run panel
description: When using the inline agent run panel, selecting a prompt and running it successfully creates a new session but it doesn't inject the first prompt in the same way that running an agent from the issue details panel does. Review this and ensure that the functionality is the same and on the basis that the agent run panel uses Opus as the default model and selects main as the base branch.

title: Create live worker container tests
description: Add a series of tests for the worker containers that sends them queries and expects certain response types. Because this will use live inference, these tests are not run automatically in CI, but instead run manually while developing the worker containers.

title: Question Answering UI
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement the interactive question answering interface for agent questions (AskUserQuestion tool).

**Background:**
When the Claude agent uses the AskUserQuestion tool, the UI must display the questions and allow the user to select options or provide custom answers. This mirrors the functionality in the Blazor client.

**Data Models (from Homespun.Shared):**
```typescript
interface UserQuestion {
  question: string        // The question text
  header: string          // Max 12 chars, displays as chip/tag
  options: QuestionOption[] // 2-4 options
  multiSelect: boolean    // Allow multiple selections
}

interface QuestionOption {
  label: string       // Display text for the option
  description: string // Explanation of the option
}

interface PendingQuestion {
  id: string
  toolUseId: string
  questions: UserQuestion[]
  createdAt: Date
}
```

**SignalR Events:**
- Receive: `AGUICustomEvent` with `name="QuestionPending"` containing `PendingQuestion` payload
- Send: `AnswerQuestion(sessionId, answersJson)` to submit answers

**Tasks:**
1. Create QuestionPanel component to display pending questions:
   - Header with "Claude has a question for you" title
   - Render each UserQuestion with header chip and question text
   - For each question, display QuestionOptions as radio buttons (single-select) or checkboxes (multi-select)
   - Add "Other..." option that reveals a text input for custom answers
2. Create QuestionOption component:
   - Display option label and description
   - Highlight selected state
   - Support both radio and checkbox modes
3. Implement question state management:
   - Track selected answers for each question
   - Track custom "Other" answers separately
   - Validate all required questions have answers before enabling submit
4. Create useAnswerQuestion mutation hook:
   - Send answers via SignalR `AnswerQuestion` method
   - Handle loading and error states
5. Handle SignalR QuestionPending event:
   - Parse PendingQuestion from AGUICustomEvent
   - Update session status to WaitingForQuestionAnswer
   - Clear previous answers and initialize defaults
6. Add submit button with validation:
   - Disabled until all questions have answers
   - Show loading state during submission
7. Display question UI inline in the chat message stream (after the assistant message that triggered it)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Questions display when AGUICustomEvent with QuestionPending is received
- Single-select questions show radio buttons, multi-select show checkboxes
- "Other" option reveals text input for custom answers
- Submit button is disabled until all questions have answers
- Answers are sent via SignalR when submitted
- Session status changes appropriately (WaitingForQuestionAnswer → Running)
- UI clears after successful submission

title: Remove Homespun.Client and associated testing
description: Commit and push to update PR.

title: E2E Tests
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Create end-to-end tests matching existing Playwright coverage.

**Tasks:**
1. Configure Playwright for React application
2. Review existing Blazor E2E tests in tests/ directory
3. Port critical user flow tests:
   - Project creation and navigation
   - Issue CRUD operations
   - Issue tree/graph navigation
   - Agent session start and chat
   - Settings configuration
4. Add visual regression tests (optional)
5. Configure test data setup/teardown
6. Set up E2E in CI pipeline
7. Add E2E test scripts to package.json
8. Document how to run E2E tests locally

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Playwright E2E tests pass
- Coverage matches or exceeds existing Blazor E2E tests
- Tests run in CI pipeline
- Tests can be run locally
- Critical user journeys are covered

title: Manage multiple sessions
description:
Multiple sessions on the same branch should be accessible. The Claude worker should be used as the single source of truth - I believe the management of multiple sessions is possible using the Typescript SDK.

I want to have the "session" page represent all the sessions that have been run on an issue. A session tab should be added to the information panel that shows all past sessions, each of which represents a context window. Clicking on these should open previous sessions as read-only.

When the agent session page is navigated to, show the latest active session only. There should also be a button to clear context and add a new session for this agent worker container.

Clarify the difference between "agent" (a worker container running on a cloned issue branch) and a session ("a single conversation with that "agent" when working.

title: Tidy up client code
Update the Grafana dashboards to filter by issue
description: For worker containers and relevant parts of the server and client, add an IssueId field to logs that contains the issue ID related to the log. The aim of this is to allow the Grafana dashboards to be filtered to only the logs relating to particular issues.

title: Permissions are not being passed to agent per command
description: Changing the permissions in the UI and sending a new message to Claude does not change the permissions of the agent. This should be updated such that new permissions are sent and the Claude Code server should use new permissions per message (if they change). The "build" permission that is used to start agents should refer to "bypass permissions".

title: Fix file search string interpolation
description:
When the file search is run in an input box with the "@" symbol, this opens a small window above the input box that shows the matching results, and when the enter button is pressed the file name is added to the chat window enclosed in curly braces. Change this behaviour such that curly braces are not added - quotation marks should be added only if there is a space in the name (e.g. `@"file name.txt"`). 

The curly braces are used to delimit where the search is run so that the "search mode" only applies when the cursor is within the curly braces. Update this behaviour according to the assumption that file names don't contain spaces unless they are enclosed in quotation marks.

Make the search results window that pops up the full width of the input box so it shows more of the file path.

The PR search doesn't include the curly braces and as a result the search window always pops up after the "#" is added. This should be fixed to assume that there are no spaces within a PR number and the search window should only pop up if the cursor is directly after the "#" without spaces. I am happy to not be able to add spaces in the search term, as this would break the above proposed functionality.

title: Just have "plan" and "build" permissions
description: There are currently four options for permissions settings that match the options in Claude Code. Instead of this, simplify the permissions settings to just two: "Build", which maps to "Bypass Permissions" and "Plan", which maps to "Plan".

title: Task graph loading waits for GitHub PRs to load
description: The graph

title: Update React client logging to Promtail
description: The server container logs to a PLG stack, which is set up using Docker Compose. The Homespun.Web React client should also log to the PLG stack using OpenTelemetry. This should be tagged with Component="Client" such that the client logs can be separated.

title: Remove all logging from tests
description: Many tests log results to the console. Any ILogger that is being used in tests should log to file only in a TestResults folder to improve the test performance. Update the logging behaviour of the test projects to ensure that this is happening.

title: Add activity log
Add buttons to issue rows for editing issues and starting agents.
description:
In the issue rows on the project page, add inline buttons to edit the issue and run agents on the right hand side. These should show up on hover and selection only.

The run agent should open a context panel that contains the agent control panel (i.e. the ability to start an agent with a particular prompt

title: Fleece is not installed on the worker containers
description: Ensure the Fleece CLI tool is installed on the worker containers. This can be done with `dotnet tool install -g Fleece.Cli`. It is installed on the parent application containers, just not on the agent worker containers.

title: Use Blazor Blueprint
description:
Install the Blazor Blueprint MCP on the client workers by executing this command after Claude is installed:

claude mcp add blazorblueprint --transport stdio -- npx -y @blazorblueprint/mcp@latest --scope user

Consider whether this should go in the Dockerfile or the application.

This should install the MCP server and allow it to be used by the workers building Homespun. Also install the library to the client application using the instructions here: https://blazorblueprintui.com/docs/installation

title: Phase 1: Foundation & Infrastructure
description:
## Verification Checklist

This phase establishes the foundational infrastructure for the React frontend port in the **Homespun.Web** project (`src/Homespun.Web`).

**Child Issues:**
- [ ] **rXJGro** - Project Setup and Toolchain (Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, shadcn/ui)
- [ ] **0ApnTK** - OpenAPI Client Generation (hey-api/openapi-ts)
- [ ] **B46NgY** - Core Layout and Routing (TanStack Router)
- [ ] **9S4Xeg** - SignalR Client Setup

**Agent Verification Instructions:**
When all child issues are complete, verify the following integration points in `src/Homespun.Web`:
1. Confirm the Vite dev server starts successfully with `npm run dev`
2. Verify TanStack Router navigation works between routes
3. Confirm OpenAPI client can make API calls to the backend
4. Verify SignalR connection establishes and receives events
5. Check that shadcn/ui components render correctly with Tailwind styling

Mark each child issue checkbox above as verified, then mark this phase complete.

title: Add git identities to agent worker containers
description:
When running the application on Azure Container Apps the agent workers are not able to push changes in Git, stating that they do not have their identity set up. See below for example response. However, when run using the docker agent execution mode (i.e. agents running in docker containers on the same host machine), this issue does not occur. Please diagnose the root issue and come up with a plan to fix this.

### Example agent response

Git doesn't have an identity configured on this machine. I can't update the git config per my safety protocols. Could you configure your git identity first?

git config --global user.email "you@example.com"
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
Once that's set, I can retry the commit and push.

title: Add task queues for issues and PRs
Project Toolbar
description:
Implement the sticky toolbar above the task graph with issue management controls.

**Component: ProjectToolbar**
A horizontally scrollable toolbar positioned below the project title, containing all issue management actions.

**Layout:**
- Sticky positioning (below project title at ~52px)
- Horizontal scroll on mobile
- Button groups separated by visual dividers
- Z-index to stay above graph during scroll

**Button Groups (left to right):**

1. **Creation buttons:**
   - Create above (Shift+O) - arrow-up-from-line icon
   - Create below (O) - arrow-down-from-line icon

2. **Hierarchy buttons:**
   - Make child of another - corner-right-up icon
   - Make parent of another - corner-left-down icon
   - Both enter 'SelectingMoveTarget' mode with crosshair cursor

3. **Undo/Redo buttons:**
   - Undo (u) - undo icon
   - Redo - redo icon
   - Show action descriptions in tooltip
   - Disabled when stack is empty

4. **Edit button:**
   - Open full edit page - pencil icon
   - Disabled when no issue selected

5. **Agent Run button:**
   - Opens AgentLauncher popover
   - Shows prompt selection dropdown
   - Keyboard: `e` to open prompt selection

6. **Depth Controls (right side):**
   - Decrease depth (`[`) - minus icon
   - Current depth display
   - Increase depth (`]`) - plus icon

7. **Search Input (right side):**
   - Slash (/) to focus
   - Shows match count badge
   - `n`/`N` for next/previous match
   - Enter to embed search

**Tasks:**
1. Create ProjectToolbar component with responsive layout
2. Implement button groups with icons (lucide-react)
3. Wire up keyboard shortcuts via KeyboardNavigationService
4. Implement undo/redo integration with IssueHistoryApi
5. Create depth control state and handlers
6. Implement search input with match count
7. Add move target selection mode state
8. Mobile-responsive horizontal scrolling

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Toolbar stays fixed below project title on scroll
- All buttons trigger correct actions
- Keyboard shortcuts work when toolbar focused
- Disabled states show correctly
- Mobile: horizontally scrollable

title: SignalR Client Setup
description:
Create React-compatible SignalR client wrapper for real-time updates.

**Tasks:**
1. Install @microsoft/signalr package
2. Create useSignalR hook for connection management
3. Implement automatic reconnection with exponential backoff
4. Create typed event handlers for:
   - Notifications Hub (/hubs/notifications)
     - IssuesChanged event
   - Claude Code Hub (/hubs/claudecode)
     - ReceiveMessage event
     - SessionStatusChanged event
     - AgentStatusChanged event
5. Create SignalRProvider context component
6. Add connection status indicator component
7. Handle connection lifecycle (connect on mount, disconnect on unmount)
8. Add reconnection UI feedback

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- SignalR connects successfully to both hubs
- Events are received and can trigger React state updates
- Reconnection works after network interruption
- Connection status is visible in the UI
- No memory leaks on unmount

title: Update Grafana dashboard to collect worker logs separately
description: Worker logs are being captured by the PLG logging stack but are not being shown in the dashboard under "Worker logs". Update the worker logs to log these properly.

title: Remove the Git Graph view
description: There is a legacy "git graph" view of the issues (rather than the current default task graph view). Remove it.

title: Add dedicated Tailscale sidecar container to Docker Compose
