description: Replace the embedded Tailscale setup in the homespun container with a dedicated Tailscale sidecar container in docker-compose.yml. Currently Tailscale runs inside the main homespun container (start.sh) and proxies services via tailscale serve. A dedicated sidecar would provide cleaner separation of concerns, allow Tailscale to persist independently of app restarts, and make it easier to expose additional services (e.g. Grafana, Loki) over the tailnet.

title: Install browser in worker container to allow E2E tests to run
description: The worker containers aren't able to run the E2E tests due to an issue with the installation of browsers in the worker container. Run dotnet test to find this issue, then run commands as required to install the browser required. Once the E2E tests can run successfully, update the worker container dockerfile or start.sh as required to fix this problem.

title: Add issue tree context to prompt
description:
Add a new {context} placeholder to prompts so that a prompt can show an issue's children and parents for context.

Only the direct children should be shown for the issue, but all of the parents and ancestors should be shown. This would look something like:

- v9n23k [feature] [open] Parent 1
  - fk28rh [feature] [open] Parent 2
    - pzk048 [task] [open] Parent 3
      - jfh481 [task] [progress] This issue
         - ruw192 [bug] [complete] Child 1
         - bfh293 [task] [complete] Child 2

Each row should include the type and status of the issue.

title: Extract LoadingSpinner component with size variants
description:
Create a reusable LoadingSpinner component to replace inline spinner patterns used in Settings.razor, SessionManagement.razor, and ProjectDetail.razor. Support sm, md, lg size variants and optional label text.

Test with bunit tests and with playwright MCP using a mock container created with mock.sh

title: Session not found page flashes up when opening sessions
description: When opening a session page in the browser, a "session not found" page often flashes up briefly while the session is being loaded. Instead of this, switch straight to the session page with skeleton components and only go to the session not found page when it is confirmed by the server that the session doesn't exist.

title: Add button for issues agent rather than prompt
Make "agents", "prompts" and "sessions" consistent
description:
The titles "agents", "prompts" and "sessions" are used inconsistently throughout the application.

- Prompts should refer to the actual prompt "build", "plan", "rebase", etc. that are injected into the session when it is started. The label on the AgentControlPanel and in the left side bar should be "Prompt", rather than "Agent".
- Agents refer to a running instance of Claude Code on an issue. This should be used to replace nearly all of the "Sessions" text throughout.
- Sessions refer to a particular conversation thread that is either currently running or has been run on an agent. There will be very little user interaction with individual sessions, as the agents tend to only require one or two sessions to complete their work and this is mostly managed by the instances of Claude Code.

Put together a plan to make this terminology consistent throughout the application.

Be very careful with links between pages - after updating addresses ensure that the correct reference is used (e.g. if the /sessions/ page is changed to /agents/, change all instances of these links.

title: Run branch ID mini-prompt in background
description: When a new issue is created the branch ID is generated from the title using a mini prompt. This is done synchronously and blocks opening of the editing page until the ID is generated. Instead of this, it should run in the background, with the "suggest" button in a loading state until the branch ID has been generated.

title: Add full refresh button
description: In the settings tab on the projects page, add an additional button that triggers a full update of the GitHub PRs (i.e. downloads all merged, closed and open PRs and updates the entire cache for the project).

title: Create logging container
description: The client, server and worker containers should all log to a PLG stack of containers running on the host machine. The client should send telemetry back to the server with a new endpoint on the server. Create a Docker Compose file for starting these containers alongside the server container and connecting them. The ./scripts/run.sh and ./scripts/run.ps1 scripts should be adjusted to use Docker Compose, while the ./scripts/mock.sh and ./scripts/mock.ps1 can remain as is, just using dotnet run.

title: Review correct hierarchy
New issues need to be refreshed to show up
Issue sync behaviour broken
description:
The issue synchronisation behaviour is generally broken, with two significant issues:

- It appears to commit changes to the fleece issues first before pulling changes from the origin. This causes divergence and merge conflicts. Instead, it should always pull first before committing such that the fleece issue changes are unstaged and can just sit on top of the pulled changes. Due to the hashing behaviour this should result in two sits of issues files that can then be merged. Tests should be added using temporary git repositories to simulate different git conditions with multiple merged branches containing changes issues files to confirm that if the only local changes that occur in the main branch used by the Homespun application are in the ./fleece folder to the issues_{hash}.jsonl and tombstones_{hash}.jsonl files, that there are never any merge conflicts that prevent syncing.
- The full issues data from disk is not read and used to replace the issues data in memory when the sync button is pressed. As a result, if the file changes at some point the front end does not receive issues information such as the description from the back end. Clicking on an issue in this condition results in no changes to the UI, but clicking on pre-existing issues that haven't changed still works.

title: Default timeline mode
description:
When a project page is opened the old mode of rendering the timeline is shown first before switching to the task graph mode. Only the task graph mode should be shown as a method of rendering the timeline - remove the other mode from the codebase entirely.

Ensure that at the top of the timeline the 5 most recent PRs as well as a button to load previous PRs is shown, similar to how the old system worked.

title: Fix agent status colours
description:
The agent status colours in the agent indicator and in the sessions page should be matched:

Green: running
Yellow: waiting for input
Orange: waiting for plan execution
Purple: waiting for question answers
Red: error

title: Fix worker image build
description:
The worker image build is still failing in CI with the below problem. Please review and fix.

buildx failed with: ERROR: failed to build: failed to solve: process "/bin/sh -c useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash --uid 1000 homespun 2>/dev/null || true     && mkdir -p /home/homespun/.claude/todos /home/homespun/.claude/debug /home/homespun/.claude/projects /home/homespun/.claude/statsig /home/homespun/.claude/plans     && mkdir -p /home/homespun/.local/share /home/homespun/.config /home/homespun/.cache     && mkdir -p /workdir     && cp -r /root/.claude/plugins /home/homespun/.claude/plugins 2>/dev/null || true     && cp -r /root/.mcp.json /home/homespun/.mcp.json 2>/dev/null || true     && chown -R homespun:homespun /home/homespun /workdir /app     && chmod -R 777 /home/homespun/.local /home/homespun/.config /home/homespun/.cache /home/homespun/.claude" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1

title: Add undo feature
description:
Add the ability to undo and redo changes to the issues list. Whenever any changes are made to issues (i.e. adding and editing them), the issues list should be asynchronously persisted in the projects/{project}/history/ folder as timestamped JSONL files using the Fleece library serialization capabilities. An undo and redo button should be added to the project page UI to allow the user to go backwards and forwards in the history, updating the graph view as required. The current timestamp that is being used for the issues should also be saved such that the application is aware where in the history the user is. If the history is at a previous timestamp to the latest one and any changes are made, this should result in all of the history between the "active" timestamp and the latest timestamp being deleted, and a new timestamp added at the end of the history.

Note that merging issues should be considered as an item in the history.

title: Migrate Blazor components to WASM client and rewire to HTTP services
description:
Move all Blazor components (pages, shared components, layout) from the monolith into `Homespun.Client` and update them to use the HTTP client services instead of direct server-side service injection.

## Components to migrate

### Layout Components
- `MainLayout.razor` → `Homespun.Client/Layout/MainLayout.razor`
- `NavMenu.razor` → `Homespun.Client/Layout/NavMenu.razor`
- Remove `ReconnectModal.razor` (SignalR reconnect is not needed in WASM the same way)

### Page Components (12 pages)
Move all from `Components/Pages/` to `Homespun.Client/Pages/`:
- `Home.razor` - Dashboard
- `Projects.razor` - Project list
- `ProjectDetail.razor` - Project detail with sessions, issues, PRs
- `ProjectCreate.razor` - Create new project
- `ProjectEdit.razor` - Edit project settings
- `FeatureCreate.razor` - Create feature/PR
- `IssueEdit.razor` - Edit issue details
- `SessionManagement.razor` - View/manage Claude sessions
- `AgentPrompts.razor` - Manage agent prompts
- `Settings.razor` - Application settings
- `Error.razor` - Error page
- `NotFound.razor` - 404 page

### Shared Components (50+)
Move all from `Components/Shared/` to `Homespun.Client/Components/`:

**Chat UI components:**
- `ChatInput.razor`, `ChatMessage.razor`, `ProcessingIndicator.razor`
- `TextBlock.razor`, `ThinkingBlock.razor`, `ToolUseBlock.razor`, `ToolResultBlock.razor`
- `ContentBlock.razor`

**Panel components:**
- `AgentControlPanel.razor`, `AgentManagementPanel.razor`, `AgentStatusPanel.razor`
- `WorktreeManagementPanel.razor`, `CurrentPullRequestDetailPanel.razor`
- `IssueDetailPanel.razor`

**Utility components:**
- `Modal.razor`, `ConfirmModal.razor`, `LoadingSpinner.razor`
- `NotificationBanner.razor`, `FormMessage.razor`, `AsyncActionButton.razor`

**Session history components:**
- `SessionHistoryList.razor`, `SessionCacheHistory.razor`

### Design showcase (optional - lower priority)
- `Design/DesignShowcase.razor` and all showcase components
- Can be migrated later or dropped

## Key changes per component

### Remove render mode directives
Every component currently has `@rendermode InteractiveServer`. Remove ALL of these - WASM components don't use rendermode.

### Replace service injection
Before (Blazor Server - direct service injection):
```razor
@inject IProjectService ProjectService
@code {
    var projects = await ProjectService.GetAllAsync();
}
```

After (Blazor WASM - HTTP service):
```razor
@inject HttpProjectApiService ProjectApi
@code {
    var projects = await ProjectApi.GetAllProjectsAsync();
}
```

### Common patterns to change:
1. **`@inject IProjectService`** → `@inject HttpProjectApiService`
2. **`@inject IClaudeSessionService`** → `@inject HttpSessionApiService` (for REST calls) + `@inject SignalRSessionService` (for real-time)
3. **`@inject IGitWorktreeService`** → `@inject HttpWorktreeApiService`
4. **`@inject IFleeceService`** → `@inject HttpIssueApiService`
5. **`@inject IGitHubService`** → HTTP service or data passed via API
6. **`@inject INotificationService`** → `@inject HttpNotificationApiService` + SignalR for real-time
7. **`@inject NavigationManager`** → Keep as-is (works in WASM)
8. **`@inject IGraphService`** → `@inject HttpGraphApiService`

### Handle services that don't have API equivalents
Some services currently injected by components may not have corresponding API endpoints. For each:
- If the data is needed: Add a new API endpoint on the server and a client HTTP service
- If it's purely client-side logic: Reimplement in the client project
- Examples:
  - `IBreadcrumbService` → Reimplement client-side (it just tracks navigation)
  - `IMarkdownRenderingService` → Can run client-side in WASM (Markdig works in WASM)
  - `ISignalRUrlProvider` → Not needed; client connects directly

## Static assets
- Move `wwwroot/` contents to `Homespun.Client/wwwroot/`
- Move `package.json` and Tailwind config to client project
- Move `.razor.css` scoped style files alongside their components

## _Imports.razor
Create `Homespun.Client/_Imports.razor` with:
```razor
@using System.Net.Http
@using System.Net.Http.Json
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Forms
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Routing
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebAssembly.Http
@using Homespun.Shared.Models.Sessions
@using Homespun.Shared.Models.Projects
@using Homespun.Shared.Models.PullRequests
@using Homespun.Shared.Models.Git
@using Homespun.Shared.Models.Notifications
@using Homespun.Client.Layout
@using Homespun.Client.Components
@using Homespun.Client.Services
```

## Verification
- All components compile in the WASM project
- Pages render correctly when served via the Server project
- Navigation between pages works
- Data loads correctly from API endpoints
- No remaining references to server-side services

title: Inline issue details panel
description: Issue rows should be able to expand when double clicked, showing all of the information in the current issue detail panel such as the PR status, description and so on. The issue detail panel should be refactored to suit this control usage, as we will not be using the detail panel in it's current position anymore.

title: Clicking session indicator in top bar goes to wrong page
description: Clicking on the session indicator navigates to the agents page rather than the session page. Please fix. Also sort the session page to have the waiting sessions at the top and the working sessions at the bottom.

title: Fix browser exceptions
description: There are quite a number of browser errors and observability exceptions thrown in the front end. Navigate to the running application at http://homespun-dev.tail0d83d3.ts.net:8080/ and look around the various project and branch pages using the Playwright MCP tools to get an understanding of the errors that are being logged in the browser and put together a plan to fix them.

title: Worktree display and refresh
description:
The main project window should be split in two - one side showing the current timeline (on the left) and one side showing the current worktrees and local branches (determined through the Git service). Now that issues are kept within the code the actual issue timeline may be different between different branches. Selecting the worktrees on the right should show the different timelines (for the issues, the PRs will remain the same between branches as these are branch-independent) in the timeline window.

There should also be a refresh button that pulls all of the latest changes for all local branches, and a delete button to delete a local branch and worktree from the project (and from disk).

title: Add 's' shortcut to open status editing panel
description: When in 'select' mode, the 's' key should open a panel that allows the current issue status to be selected (similar to the type selection panel).

title: Add Playwright E2E tests for critical journeys
Close issue edit page and go back to the project page when saving and editing issues
description: When the Save & Run Agent button is clicked on the 'Edit Issue' page, this should immediately return back to the main 'Project' when clicked. The agent startup should happen asynchronously on the server.

title: When starting an agent on a PR, clone the branch of the PR before starting agent
Fix issues agent button
description: When the issues agent button is clicked, a new branch is cloned and a new issues agent session is started and opened. When a prompt is typed in, however, the agent immediately goes into an error state. Review the logs using your logs skill. The worker container appears to have not been started when I use `docker ps` on the host machine.

title: Issues not being written with full description with CTRL+Enter
description: Occasionally issues are not being persisted with their full descriptions. Review the issue updating logic to ensure that the full issue description is written to the Fleece issues JSONL file in the repository. This appears to be the case when CTRL + Enter is used to save the changes instead of clicking the "Save Changes" button, and when the user hasn't lost focus on the description box.

title: Implement workflow JSONL storage service
description:
Create JSONL storage service for workflow definitions in `.workflows/` directory.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/IWorkflowStorageService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowStorageService.cs`

## Interface Methods
- `GetWorkflowAsync(workflowId)` - Get single workflow
- `ListWorkflowsAsync(projectPath)` - List workflows for project
- `CreateWorkflowAsync(workflow)` - Create new workflow
- `UpdateWorkflowAsync(workflow)` - Update existing workflow
- `DeleteWorkflowAsync(workflowId)` - Delete workflow

## Implementation Details
- Store workflows in `.workflows/workflows_*.jsonl` files
- Use write-through caching pattern (like FleeceService)
- Generate hash-based IDs for workflows
- Support versioning for schema migrations

## Pattern Reference
Follow `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Services/FleeceService.cs` for JSONL storage patterns

title: Add GitHub and Fleece CLI tools to all containers
Store chat messages in a cache
description:
In the session chat window, I believe the session messages are streamed straight to the chat and otherwise are lost. I want to be able to review the message history and past sessions for all issues rather than have them be temporary only. I believe this would also make it easier to restart old sessions and verify correct rendering behaviour.

Run a mock container with live agent streaming by using the mock-live.sh script and interact with a test agent briefly. When this is done put together a plan for creating a JSONL message store per session that all messages are logged to. Add a session history interface for issues and PRs, both on the main project page and in the session window itself.

The rendered order for messages is also odd, please verify this by looking at the chat as it is streamed in. Live messages persist longer than they should.

title: Answer and plan tools no longer work correctly
description:
The answer and plan tools for the application no longer work correctly when run in docker agent execution mode. In this mode, when the agent uses the AskUserQuestion tool the agent should stop and wait for the user to respond. It is currently assuming that the first answer provided is the correct one and proceeding anyway. This is required only for the local execution mode as in the local execution mode the instance of Claude Code by default will just continue execution.

However, we are using the Typescript version of the Claude Agent SDK in Docker and Azure Container Apps execution modes, which allows this flow. Refer to https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/typescript#ask-user-question and https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/user-input#handle-clarifying-questions for information on how the SDK handles clarifying questions.

Plans operate somewhat differently. Review the code and documentation and see if you can come up with a plan to fix this behaviour for the Docker and Agent Execution modes. The SDK has recently been changed to V1, so this may be fixed or it may require minor updates to the code.

If it will simplify things, OK to remove specific handling for questions and tools from the Local execution mode. Otherwise, if they are handled separately and don't interact in the different modes then OK to leave it as is.

title: Ensure changes are being read from the correct issues agent branch
description: When the issue agent is run it successfully edits the issues in the correct branch. The issue diff component was updated such that it only shows changes in the issues rather than the full issues tree in PR #558, but the actual issues are seemingly not being read from the correct location for the changes instigated by the agent, as even after the changes are made the issue diff window doesn't show any changes. Add logging that shows when the issues are being read and from which folder they are being read from, and add a refresh button to the issue diff screen to allow manual refreshing of both the main and modified issues. When the "accept" button is clicked, the issues in the main branch are not updated and a notification pops up saying "modified 0 issues", so it would appear that the issue is with where the server is drawing the issues from rather than with the front end.

title: Add controls for editing execution mode
Update Fleece library and graph rendering
description:
Update the Fleece.Core library to the latest v1.0.0. This version changes the Fleece issue model to use Lexorank for issue ordering. Each issue just contains a list of ParentIssues and a SortOrder per parent. Update the methods for the creation of the issue graph as required, adding children to each parent and listing them in order of the SortOrder.

The "Group" field has also been removed, please remove all references to this in the UI and graph creation.

title: Implement ActionNodeExecutor
description:
Create the action node executor for automated actions.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/NodeExecutors/ActionNodeExecutor.cs`

## Supported Actions
1. **create_pr**
   - Use existing GitHub service
   - Template title and body with context variables
   - Configure base branch, labels, reviewers
   - Output: PR number, URL

2. **merge_pr**
   - Merge with configurable strategy (merge/squash/rebase)
   - Optional branch deletion after merge
   - Output: merge commit SHA

3. **create_issue**
   - Create Fleece issue
   - Set parent issue from context
   - Configure type, title, description

4. **notify**
   - Send notifications via configured channels
   - Template message with context
   - Support Slack/email/webhook

5. **http_request**
   - Make HTTP requests to external APIs
   - Configure method, headers, body
   - Output: response body

6. **run_command**
   - Execute shell commands
   - Configure working directory, timeout
   - Output: stdout, exit code

## Error Handling
- Retry on transient failures
- Clear error messages for permanent failures

title: Get worker container status from last message
description: The container statuses are quite flaky. Review the way that statuses are held in the worker containers. They should be determined by the last message - i.e. if the last message is pending plan the status should be "awaiting plan", when the last message is "success" the status should be "awaiting input" and so on. Review the message types in the Claude Agent SDK documentation and put together a plan to fix the status endpoint on the container.

title: Fix the reported port in the ./scripts/mock.ps1 and mock.sh files
description: The ports reported in the mock script files are not correct and are reported before the build takes place. Remove these and rely on the application logging to show the port locations.

title: Render Markdown descriptions
description:
The chat window and issue description should both render text to Markdown by default as this often used by agents in communication and for describing PRs.

This should be possible by just parsing the string using Markdig and emitting HTML strings if valid Markdown is found. If not, fall back to just plain text.

title: Side panel and tool expansion doesn't work
description: When loading the session page, an unhandled exception occurs in the side panel (Issue/PR/To-do's/Files area): 'InvalidCharacterError: Failed to execute setAttribute on Element: @onclick is not a valid attribute name.' This crashes the Blazor Server circuit, breaking all interactive functionality on the page. The error occurs consistently on every session page load. The @onclick directive is being rendered as a literal HTML attribute instead of being processed by Blazor's render tree, likely in a component that renders raw HTML or uses MarkupString incorrectly.

title: Simplify agent worker container management
description:
The agent worker container management system for both the Docker execution mode and Azure Container Apps execution modes should be updated to follow similar behaviour, ideally using the same interface if possible.

The behaviour should be as follows:

- The server should maintain a list of all currently running containers and the clone that they are working on.
- When an agent is started to work on an issue in a clone, the list is checked to determine if there is a container already running for that clone. If there is a container already running, the server should check the state of the agent in that container. The following states are possible:
  - No currently running session: a new session is started
  - Session in "working", "question" or "plan" state: the user is notified that there is already an agent working in that clone.
  - Session in "waiting" state: the user is asked whether they would like to terminate that session.
  - Session in "error" state: a new session is started
- If there is no container running, a new container is started with that clone folder mounted as a volume (this is already implemented).

title: Some branch names are not being when AI generated
Project Setup and Toolchain
description:
Initialize the new React frontend project at src/Homespun.Web with the complete toolchain.

**Tasks:**

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- `npm run build` produces a production build without errors
- TypeScript compilation has no errors with strict mode

title: Automate rebasing
description:
Add a button to rebase any open worktrees onto main to the worktrees page. This button should start a new agent that pulls the most recent main branch and rebases onto that. The agents should fix any merge conflicts, review recently merged PRs descriptions and run all tests to ensure no regressions after rebasing.

Add a special rebase agent prompts to the agent management page that can be started on a worktree.

title: Remove background of top section of side bar
description: The side bar of the main page has a lighter colour than the main page background. Remove this background colour from the section that has the Homespun title - just show it for the section that contains "Projects" and "Global"

title: Link issues to PRs
Clicking "save and run" should take the user back to the projects page without prompting for changes
description:
Currently, on the issue edit page when clicking "save and run agent" the user is left on the edit page. When exiting the edit page to go back to the issues page, a confirmation modal for abandoning edits is shown, even though the issue details haven't changed (the issue edits were presumably just saved, although this may be due to the status being changed by the run command).

Update this such that when the button is clicked the user is just taken straight back to the project page. Review the issue with the confirmation modal, if it is due to the status being changed by the run command perhaps clicking the save and run agent button should just change the value in the combo box to "In progress" pre-emptively.

title: Show only last 5 merged PRs
description: Currently the first 5 merged PRs are shown at the top of the graph. Instead of this, the **most recent** 5 merged PRs should be shown. The sort order is correct.

title: Fix Grafana dashboard
description: Recent changes to the Grafana dashboard were made in the browser and then copied to @config/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/json/homespun-logs.json. There now aren't any dashboards showing up in Grafana at all, where it previously had the homespun dashboard it is just empty. Review the implementation and try to understand the error.

title: Edit page additional properties
description:
The edit page should allow editing of all issue properties, including branch ID and group.

Create a new PR when complete.

title: Phase 3: Issues Management
description:
## Verification Checklist

This phase implements the core issues management functionality in the **Homespun.Web** project (`src/Homespun.Web`) - the primary feature of the application using a Vim-like modal interface.

**Child Issues:**
- [ ] **X3MNPm** - Keyboard Navigation Service - Modal state machine with Vim-like keybindings
- [ ] **s6gkIp** - Project Toolbar - Sticky toolbar with creation, hierarchy, undo/redo, depth, and search controls
- [ ] **jFBW7H** - Task Graph View - Lane-based SVG visualization with parent-child relationships
- [ ] **Oq42fh** - Inline Issue Details - Expandable detail rows (Space/double-click to toggle)
- [ ] **s1cECf** - Inline Issue Creation - In-place editing with o/O commands and Tab/Shift+Tab for hierarchy
- [ ] **c3vNTL** - Issue Edit Page - Full editing interface accessed via Enter
- [ ] **QVgMjT** - Issue History (Undo/Redo) - Undo/redo functionality for issues

**Agent Verification Instructions:**
When all child issues are complete, verify the following in `src/Homespun.Web`:
1. Confirm j/k/h/l navigation works through the issue tree
2. Verify o/O creates issues inline at the correct positions
3. Confirm Tab/Shift+Tab changes hierarchy while creating issues
4. Verify Space expands inline details below the selected row
5. Confirm Enter opens the full edit page
6. Verify undo/redo works with Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z
7. Check that search with / and n/N navigation works
8. Confirm depth filtering with [ / ] keys works
9. Verify the task graph SVG renders correctly with all issue relationships

If any changes are required to fix these or to fix the wiring up of components, complete these fixes, build and test then create a PR. Once all tests pass in CI and there are no merge conflicts, squash merge the PR.

title: Blazor Server interactive mode not hydrating in mock environment
description:
When running the app via ./scripts/mock.sh, Blazor Server interactive rendering does not hydrate the page. All @onclick directives throughout the session page (Session.razor) are rendered as literal HTML attributes (e.g. @onclick="ToggleShowAll") instead of being processed by Blazor into event handlers (blazor:onclick). The WebSocket connection to /_blazor is established and Blazor's JS runtime loads, but zero <!--bl:--> component markers appear in the DOM, indicating components are never upgraded from static SSR to interactive mode.

This does NOT affect:
- Native HTML <details>/<summary> expand/collapse (used by ToolResultRenderer and thinking blocks), which work purely via browser functionality
- Any server-side rendering — the page renders correctly with all content visible

This DOES affect:
- The new +/- toggle button in ToolExecutionGroupDisplay (PR #193) — clicking does nothing
- All other @onclick handlers in Session.razor (StopSession, ClearContext, ExecutePlan, etc.)
- Sending messages via the chat input (relies on Blazor form submission)

Configuration appears correct:
- Routes.razor has @rendermode InteractiveServer (line 1)
- Session.razor has @rendermode InteractiveServer (line 19)  
- Program.cs calls .AddRazorComponents().AddInteractiveServerComponents() and .MapRazorComponents<App>().AddInteractiveServerRenderMode()
- App.razor renders <Routes /> without an explicit @rendermode attribute — this may be the root cause, as .NET 8 Blazor Web Apps may require the render mode to be specified at the call site in App.razor

Discovered during manual UI testing of PR #193 (Show limited tool usage). The PR's implementation is correct — this is a pre-existing environment issue.

title: Fix design page
description:
When I open the /design page it immediately crashes, with the following error message logged:

title: Remove the group text box from the new issue bar
description:
The group text box has been removed from the edit page but is still in the new issue bar at the bottom of the projects page. Remove it now that group is not an field in issues.

The "working branch ID" in the edit issue page doesn't work now, adding text to it doesn't seem to change the "branch preview". The branch preview in the main page does work though. This appears to be a recent issue, please review and update such that the "branch preview" changes.

title: Add 'Enter' shortcut to open selected issues for editing
description: When an issue is selected and not in "edit" mode, hitting "Enter" should open the edit page for that issue.

title: Inline Issue Creation
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement inline issue creation within the task graph using Vim-like keyboard commands.

**NOTE: This replaces the original 'bottom bar' concept. The Blazor implementation uses inline creation triggered by keyboard commands.**

**Primary Component: InlineIssueEditor**
A text input that appears in-place within the task graph when creating/editing an issue.

**Creation Commands (from Viewing mode):**
- `o` - Create issue below current selection
- `Shift+O` - Create issue above current selection

**Editing Commands (while viewing):**
- `i` - Start editing at beginning of title
- `a` - Start editing at end of title
- `r` - Replace title (clear and edit)

**Hierarchy Commands (while editing):**
- `Tab` - Make new issue PARENT of reference (indent)
- `Shift+Tab` - Make new issue SIBLING of reference parent (outdent)

**Editor Actions:**
- `Enter` - Save and return to viewing mode
- `Shift+Enter` - Save and open full edit page (for description)
- `Escape` - Cancel edit, discard changes

**Tasks:**
1. Create InlineIssueEditor component with:
   - Text input for title
   - Action buttons (Save, Save+Edit, Cancel)
   - Lane-based positioning matching selected row
2. Implement cursor position control (start, end, replace)
3. Handle Tab/Shift+Tab for hierarchy changes during creation
4. Create useCreateIssue mutation hook
5. Focus management and keyboard event handling
6. Smooth insertion animation
7. Optimistic updates (show new issue immediately)
8. Type cycling with Tab (in viewing mode): Task → Bug → Feature → Chore

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `o`/`O` opens inline editor at correct position
- Tab/Shift+Tab changes hierarchy relationship
- Enter saves, Escape cancels
- New issues appear in graph immediately
- Focus returns to graph after save/cancel

title: Concurrent agent startup causes crash
description: Starting up concurrent agents causes the following exception to be thrown: {"TimeStamp": "2026-02-08T03:46:35.8144258+00:00", "Log": "F warn: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Server.Circuits.RemoteRenderer[100]"}

title: Pass all messages from server to client
description: Currently messages are only persisted on the client when they are sent over SignalR, and refreshing a page can lose messages. Instead, these messages should be stored on the server (in memory or using the JSONL serialisation if that still exists) and the client should be able to perform a full refresh of all messages from the server. This should only happen if the use enters a page that is currently midway through processing. It should start by showing the messages as they stream in, with a button at the top of the conversation that prompts the user to load all previous messages in from the server.

title: Fix agent status and session chats
Fix issues agent issue merging
description:
I just used the issues agent for the first time to add a description to an issue. It successfully modified the issue details in the cloned branch that was created, however the diff view did not seem to pick up that there were changes in the issues - I assume this is because the diff is only loaded once. The issues from the clone and main branch should be re-read whenever the diff view is opened, and when the issues from the cloned branch are to be merged back in.

In addition to this, in the graph view only show the issues that have changed.

title: Add logging for status changes in worker
description:
There are ongoing issues where the status of the worker container doesn't represent what it is actually doing (e.g. saying it is waiting while it is still streaming back input, saying it has a plan pending after the plan has been executed, etc.). The status should be persisted on the worker and changed when certain messages are sent back to the server or received (e.g. when any agent message or tool invocation is sent, the agent is clearly "working").

Add logging to the worker that indicates whenever a status is changed. It should say "Status changed from 'working' to 'waiting'" or something similar.

title: Update configuration to use Homespun web as client
description: Currently the application is set up in Komodo through use of the resource sync in the config folder. This creates a stack that includes launching or connecting to an existing tailscale sidecar container. The Blazor app is served through the ASP.NET server. I would like to change this Docker Compose and Komodo configuration such that the React frontend is used instead of the Blazor frontend. The React frontend is built into a container as well now and should have the same package labels as the server and worker containers.

title: Scrolling to bottom of issues tab doesn't work
description: The issues tab isn't able to scroll all the way to the bottom of the page, seemingly due to the way that the project toolbar and heading float is set up. Remove the floating of the heading, I am happy for this to just scroll off screen. However, I do need the project toolbar to stick to the top of the screen when scrolling down, and for it to be possible to scroll all the way down to the bottom of the page. I have also noticed a scrolling issue on the edit issue page, where on mobile the page doesn't scroll all the way to the bottom. Review any reasons why this might be occurring.

title: Issues agent run doesn't inject prompt
description:
When I run an issues agent with a selected issue and fill in the prompt box on the modal (as introduced in PR 571) the agent is started and the clone is created, but the prompt is not generated or injected into the agent session. Additionally, on the prompts page, there is only one issue agent prompt "IssueAgentSystem" and no "IssueAgentModification" prompt that should be used to create the first "user" message.

Do the following:
- Add the additional fixed "modification" prompt to the prompts list. This should be as below
- Ensure that this prompt is passed in to the agent session when the issue agent session is started.

## Issue Modification Request

You are an agent designed to modify Fleece issues based on user instructions.

IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS:
- You may ONLY use the Fleece CLI tool to make modifications
- Do NOT write any files in the repository
- Do NOT make any code changes
- Focus solely on issue modifications using fleece commands

{{#if selectedIssueId}}
**Selected Issue:** {{selectedIssueId}}
{{/if}}

Available fleece commands:
- fleece list --oneline - List all issues
- fleece show <id> --json - Show issue details
- fleece edit <id> -t <title> -s <status> -d <description> - Edit issue
- fleece create -t <title> -s <status> -y <type> - Create new issue
- fleece edit <id> --parent-issues <parent>:<order> - Set parent hierarchy
- fleece list --tree - View issue hierarchy
- fleece list --next - View task graph

User request: {{userPrompt}}

title: Include draft status for issues
description:
Fleece.Core version 1.5.0 also included a draft status for issues. See release notes here: https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/releases/tag/v1.5.0

Update the types and UI to suit. Note that the changes for 1.6.0 have already been included.

title: Show agent status rings
description: A previous PR #469 was merged to show the agent status on running agents in the graph view. However, it doesn't appear to have worked, as the ring displayed in the graph view remains just around the "next" issues, not the issues with running agents. Please review and fix this - there should only be one "mode" for what is used to display the rings.

title: Install C# LSP on workers
description:
Add the following commands to the Dockerfile to install the C# LSP and Claude code plugins:

dotnet tool install --global csharp-ls
claude plugin install csharp-lsp@claude-plugins-official

title: Fleece issues sync
description: There should be a button on the project page to synchronise the fleece issues in the default branch. When clicked, this should commit all files in the project's `.fleece/` folder and push them. It should then pull all changes back from main and refresh the graph. Notify the user if there are issues with pulling the changes back from main - if there are, provide an option to the user to stash or discard all changes in the main branch and try pulling again.

title: Remove ClaudeAgentSDK
description:
There is currently a project called ClaudeAgentSdk that implements a local version of the agent SDK used to send prompts to Claude Code.

This should be removed as we are now relying on the Worker containers and executing all prompts in separate workers containers.

Please remove this and the local agent execution mode entirely.

title: Add scroll bar to issue information tab
description: The issue information panel in the session window has a series of tabs that show different information in them. It doesn't all fit in the width of the panel, however. Make these tabs horizontally scrollable so I can see all of them.

title: Task Graph View
description:
Implement the issues tab with TaskGraphView - the primary visualization for issues on a project.

**Primary Component: TaskGraphView**
The TaskGraphView renders issues in a lane-based hierarchical graph with SVG connectors showing parent-child relationships.

**Tasks:**
1. Create useIssues hook with filtering support (GET /api/projects/{projectId}/issues/graph)
2. Port TaskGraphLayoutService algorithm:
   - Convert TaskGraphResponse into render lines
   - Lane assignment for hierarchical depth
   - Handle hidden parent detection when filtered by depth
   - Series execution mode support (vertical chaining)
3. Build TaskGraphRow component showing:
   - Lane-based positioning with indent
   - SVG connectors to parent/child nodes
   - Issue ID, title, type badge, status badge
   - Execution mode indicator (series chain)
   - Agent status with pulse animation
   - Search term highlighting (yellow border)
4. Implement row selection (click or keyboard nav)
5. Add real-time updates via SignalR IssuesChanged event
6. Handle expand/collapse with Space key or double-click for inline details
7. Support move target selection mode (crosshair cursor)

**Render Line Types:**
- TaskGraphIssueRenderLine (issues with lane, parent lane, execution info)
- TaskGraphPrRenderLine (merged/closed PRs)
- TaskGraphSeparatorRenderLine (divider)
- TaskGraphLoadMoreRenderLine (load more PRs)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Issues display in lane-based graph visualization
- Parent-child relationships shown via SVG connectors
- Series vs parallel execution modes are visually distinct
- Clicking a row selects it for keyboard navigation
- Space/double-click expands inline details
- Color coding by type (Bug=red, Task=blue, Feature=green, Chore=gray)
- New/updated issues appear without page refresh

title: Fix branch name when branches are created
Add project list to side bar
description: The side bar currently just contains a link to the projects page. Below this (intended slightly) also show all of the projects that are currently available such that they can be linked to

title: Run bd sync on cloned repositories
Remove model page
description: The models page is not required. Remove it and remove it from the side panel.

title: Add drag and drop for issue rearrangement
Clicking on the issue type badge opens context menu to change it
description: Clicking on the type badge of an issue on the projects page should allow the type of an issue to be changed.

title: Fix issue agent diff issues
description:
When the issue agent diff is run it shows every issue as being modified rather than just the changes that have been implemented. When I start a new agent session without a prompt so there are no changes, I expect to see no differences in the issues when I go to the "review changes" page however there are a large number of changes on this page when I view it.

This appears to be an issue with copying the unstaged changes from the .fleece folder in main to the new clone, which should happen when all clones are created. title: Improve load performance for large projects with many PRs
description:
Improve the load performance, particularly for very large projects by using the PR cache more effectively. When a project page is opened the PR cache in the project folder and the issues JSONL should be read first (if not already read into memory) and should be used to render the graph.

When the PRs are synced, only information on the open PRs should be read in to update the status of PRs and link PRs to issues.

Following this, the open PRs that were cached should be checked against this recently pulled list of open PRs to see which ones are still open. For the ones that were not in the list pulled in, the status of these should then be checked to update them to either closed or merged as appropriate.

This should all happen asynchronously after the graph has been rendered.

title: Project-specific prompts
description: Custom agent prompts are currently global and managed on the "Agents" page. As well as this, project-specific prompts should be enabled. These should be edited in much the same way, with the page accessible from the project page as an additional tab.

title: Pull Requests Display
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement PR status display and integration with issues.

**Tasks:**
1. Create PR section in project dashboard (possibly in Issues tab or separate)
2. Create usePullRequests hook (GET /api/pull-requests)
3. Display open PRs with:
   - PR number and title
   - Status badges (Draft, Approved, Changes Requested, Merged)
   - Review status (reviewers, approvals)
   - CI status (checks passing/failing)
   - Linked issue (if any)
4. Display recently merged PRs section
5. Create OpenPullRequestDetailPanel component
6. Create MergedPullRequestDetailPanel component
7. Add quick actions:
   - View on GitHub (external link)
   - Start agent on PR branch
   - Link/unlink issue
8. Show loading skeletons for PR data
9. Handle GitHub API rate limiting gracefully

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Open and merged PRs display with correct status
- Status badges accurately reflect PR state
- GitHub links work
- PR-issue linkage is visible
- Agent can be started on PR branches

title: Core Layout and Routing
description:
Create the base application layout and routing structure using TanStack Router.

**Tasks:**
1. Create RootLayout component with:
   - Sidebar (projects navigation, settings link)
   - Header (breadcrumbs, agent status indicator placeholder)
   - Main content area with Outlet
2. Set up TanStack Router routes matching Blazor routes:
   - / - Projects list
   - /projects/new - Create project
   - /projects/$projectId - Project detail
   - /projects/$projectId/issues - Issues tab
   - /projects/$projectId/branches - Branches tab
   - /projects/$projectId/prompts - Prompts tab
   - /projects/$projectId/secrets - Secrets tab
   - /projects/$projectId/settings - Settings tab
   - /projects/$projectId/issues/$issueId/edit - Edit issue
   - /sessions - Sessions list
   - /sessions/$sessionId - Session chat
   - /settings - Global settings
3. Implement ErrorBoundary component
4. Create NotFound (404) page
5. Set up breadcrumb context/hook

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- All routes render placeholder components
- Sidebar navigation works correctly
- Breadcrumbs update based on current route
- Error boundary catches and displays errors gracefully
- 404 page shows for unknown routes

title: Define workflow DTOs in Homespun.Shared
description:
Create TypeScript-compatible C# DTOs for workflow definitions.

## Files to Create in `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Workflows/`
- `WorkflowDefinition.cs` - Core workflow model with id, title, nodes, edges, settings
- `WorkflowNode.cs` - Node definitions with type, position, config
- `WorkflowEdge.cs` - Edge definitions with source, target, conditions
- `WorkflowTrigger.cs` - Trigger configurations (manual, event, scheduled)
- `WorkflowExecution.cs` - Execution state tracking
- `WorkflowContext.cs` - Context/variable storage model
- `NodeConfigs/AgentNodeConfig.cs` - Agent node configuration
- `NodeConfigs/GateNodeConfig.cs` - Gate/approval node configuration
- `NodeConfigs/ActionNodeConfig.cs` - Action node configuration
- `NodeConfigs/TransformNodeConfig.cs` - Transform node configuration

## Files to Create in `src/Homespun.Shared/Requests/`
- `WorkflowRequests.cs` - API request models for create/update/execute

## Pattern Reference
Follow existing patterns in `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Fleece/IssueDto.cs`

title: Show limited tool usage
description: When a tool execution group has more than 2 tool calls, only show the most recent 2 by default. Add a subtle +/- toggle button to the group header to expand/collapse the full list.

title: Toolbar should stick to top of page
description: When scrolling down the main project page, the toolbar sticks to the current location relative to the top of the unscrolled page. This places it below significantly below the top of the page when it is scrolling. Fix this such that the toolbar scrolls up with the tabs and the heading and only sticks when it is at the top of the page (with the same gap that it has with the sides of the page.

title: Fix agent run buttons
description:
The agent run buttons on the Homespun.Web client's project page (positioned at the right hand side of each issue and in the toolbar) doesn't run the agent. Review the behaviour in the Homespun.Client Blazor application - it should open a popover with some controls to select the prompt to run, then run a new agent session.

There should also be a "save and run agent" panel on the issue edit page.

title: Fix issues agent button
description: When the issues agent button is clicked, a new branch is cloned and a new issues agent session is started and opened. When a prompt is typed in, however, the agent immediately goes into an error state. Review the logs using your logs skill. The worker container appears to have not been started when I use `docker ps` on the host machine.

title: Sort the orphaned issues by group
description: Orphaned issues should be sorted by group. They are currently just shown in one branch, but they should be split into one branch per group.

title: Add keyboard shortcuts
Render issues as one large SVG
description: The task graph is currently rendered as row-by-row SVGs with text descriptions and controls adjacent to each row. This adds significant difficulties in rendering edges that bypass rows. Instead of this I want to use D3.js to calculate the layout of the nodesn and edges, and just rendering the entire task graph in one large SVG, including the issue descriptions and controls. The issue descriptions and controls should be added as <foreignObject> elements so that they can maintain their current complex behaviour.

title: Fix agent run in client
description:
When an agent is run in the Homespun.Web client is successfully starts the new agent session but it doesn't inject the prompt into the session. I'm not sure if it sets the correct session mode either.

Please review both and fix as required.

title: Dont show issue ID on mobile
description: The issue row is already a little crowded on mobile - remove the issue ID on mobile display to leave more room for the issue title.

title: Move floating toolbar position
description: The floating toolbar on the project issues page currently has a position set to 68px from the top. I have tested this in the browser and have found that this should be set to 4px. Update this.

title: Triggers and Polish
description:
Add trigger system, execution history, and polish.

## Deliverables
1. Manual trigger endpoint
2. Event trigger system with Fleece integration
3. Scheduled triggers
4. Execution history and logging
5. Workflow templates/presets
6. Documentation and examples

title: Simplify new issue creation
description: Simplify the creation of new beads issues: issues should be created with just an optional group, type and a required title. Move creation to a box at the bottom of the main project page.

title: Project Secrets Tab
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement the secrets/environment variables tab for projects.

**Tasks:**
1. Create secrets tab content in project detail
2. Create useSecrets hook (GET /api/secrets)
3. List secrets (name only, values always masked):
   - Secret key name
   - Last updated timestamp
   - Delete action
4. Add new secret form:
   - Key name (required, validated for env var format)
   - Value (password input, never displayed after save)
5. Edit secret value (replace only, cannot view existing)
6. Delete secret with confirmation
7. Security considerations:
   - NEVER display full secret values in UI
   - Clear value from memory after save
   - Validate key names
8. Handle loading and empty states

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Secrets list displays with masked values (e.g., ********)
- Add/edit/delete operations work via API
- Values are never exposed in UI after save
- Key names are validated
- Appropriate security warnings are shown

title: Re-render graph when issues are changed
description: SignalR events are not triggering the graph to be re rendered when things like agent status changes or issues get added.

title: Show full plan in session
description:
In the session window when exiting plan mode the full plan is not shown to the user. Ensure this event is captured and show the entire plan to the chat window.

It appears that the plan is also not written to the .claude folder as expected due it not being allowed sufficient permission. Instead, it is often written to a PLAN.md file in the repository. Check this using a mock container with live chat (i.e. mock-live.sh) and create a plan to fix this.

title: Distinguish different issue types and statuses in graph view
description:
The issue symbol in the graph view should have the following colours based on the content of the issue:

Tasks, chores and features:
- Open and with description: filled and blue
- Open and without description: outline only and blue

Bugs:
- Open and with description: filled and red
- Open and without description: outline only and red

Any issue with working agent:
- Agent working: Green and filled
- Agent waiting: Yellow and filled
- Agent question: Orange and filled
- Agent error: Red and filled with cross at centre

title: Messages out of order
description:
The way that messages are shown in a chat has changed with streaming message disabled and this has generally resulted in a cleaner output. However, some messages (like summaries and implementation plans) are shown at the top of the message stack (i.e. directly under the last input) rather than at the very bottom of the conversation, in order of how they were sent by the agent.

Review the message ordering system - they should generally be shown in order that they were sent back from the app. Also, change the display of thinking messages so they appear to be of lower visual priority when compared to direct messages from the agent. Update the design system with a thinking message type that is lighter.

title: When a plan approval is pending, sending a message should cancel the plan
description: When a plan approval is pending and the user instead sends a prompt using the normal chat box, this should be treated the same as the user using the reject button with the text in the chat box used as the description of the changes requested by the user.

title: Show issue and PR details in the session window
Fix issue copying during clone creation
description:
The unstaged issues should be copied to the cloned folder when a new agent is run. This is failing with the following logs:

2026-03-21 13:07:50.414 [Homespun.Features.Git.GitCloneService] : Failed to copy .fleece changes to clone: An error occurred trying to start process 'rsync' with working directory '/data/projects/Northrop/main'. No such file or directory

2026-03-21 13:07:50.414 [Homespun.Features.Git.GitCloneService] : Copying unstaged .fleece changes from /data/projects/Northrop/main to clone at /data/projects/Northrop/.clones/issues-agent-20260321-020836/workdir

title: Show all worker containers and their status
description: I would like an additional page in the side bar for worker containers. This should page should show a list of all current worker containers and their status that have been started by the application, and include information about the project and issue they were started on, the state that the agents are in and a button to manually kill them.

title: Create Homespun.Client Blazor WASM project
description:
Create the new `Homespun.Client` Blazor WebAssembly standalone project that will serve as the frontend UI. This project replaces the current Blazor Server interactive components. ## What to create ### Project setup - Create `src/Homespun.Client/Homespun.Client.csproj` using `Microsoft.NET.Sdk.BlazorWebAssembly` - Target `net10.0` - Add to `Homespun.sln` under the `src` solution folder - Add ProjectReference to `Homespun.Shared` - Add NuGet references: - `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebAssembly` - `Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Client` (for SignalR connections from WASM) - `Markdig` (if markdown rendering happens client-side, otherwise call server API) ### Project structure ``` src/Homespun.Client/ ├── Program.cs # WASM entry point, DI setup ├── wwwroot/ │ ├── index.html # HTML host page (replaces App.razor) │ ├── css/ │ │ ├── tailwind.css # Tailwind input │ │ └── tailwind.min.css # Built Tailwind CSS │ ├── app.css # Custom styles │ └── favicon.png ├── Layout/ │ ├── MainLayout.razor # Main layout │ └── NavMenu.razor # Navigation ├── Pages/ # All page components │ ├── Home.razor │ ├── Projects.razor │ ├── ProjectDetail.razor │ ├── ProjectCreate.razor │ ├── ProjectEdit.razor │ ├── FeatureCreate.razor │ ├── IssueEdit.razor │ ├── SessionManagement.razor │ ├── AgentPrompts.razor │ ├── Settings.razor │ └── ... ├── Components/ # Shared components │ ├── ChatInput.razor │ ├── ChatMessage.razor │ ├── Modal.razor │ ├── LoadingSpinner.razor │ └── ... (all 50+ shared components) ├── Services/ # Client-side service implementations │ ├── HttpProjectService.cs # IProjectService via HttpClient │ ├── HttpSessionService.cs # Session operations via HttpClient │ ├── HttpIssueService.cs # Issue operations via HttpClient │ ├── SignalRSessionService.cs # Real-time session updates via SignalR │ └── ... ├── _Imports.razor # Global using statements ├── App.razor # Root router component ├── package.json # Tailwind CSS build └── tailwind.config.js ``` ### Program.cs entry point ```csharp var builder = WebAssemblyHostBuilder.CreateDefault(args); builder.RootComponents.Add("#app"); // Configure HttpClient to point to server API builder.Services.AddScoped(sp => new HttpClient { BaseAddress = new Uri(builder.HostEnvironment.BaseAddress) }); // Register client-side services (HTTP-backed implementations) builder.Services.AddScoped(); // ... more service registrations // SignalR connections builder.Services.AddScoped(); await builder.Build().RunAsync(); ``` ### Key differences from current Blazor Server 1. **No direct service access** - Components cannot inject server-side services directly. They must use HttpClient or SignalR 2. **No `@rendermode InteractiveServer`** - Remove all rendermode directives; WASM is the default 3. **HttpClient for data fetching** - Replace direct service calls with HTTP API calls 4. **SignalR client connections** - Use `HubConnectionBuilder` from the client instead of being part of the hub server 5. **No server-side state** - All UI state lives in the browser; use local storage or session storage if needed 6. **Tailwind CSS build** - Move the npm/tailwind build into this project since it's the UI project ### wwwroot/index.html Create the HTML host page (replaces App.razor server rendering): ```html Homespun
Loading...
``` ## Verification - Project compiles with `dotnet build` - `dotnet run` on the Server project serves the WASM client - Basic routing works (navigating between pages) - Initial page loads show the layout with nav menu

title: Add logging of message content to worker and improve message logging
description:
We have recently status change logging to the workers to determine when the statuses are changing in the workers.

However, it is difficult to do this without the message content as context. Add "debug" logging that includes tool uses (just the tool information) and the message content so that we can understand what is occurring. This should be configurable when the agent containers are started - update the server code to always set this "debug" flag to true (for now) when the containers are started in the DockerAgentExecutionService.

Also, review the Grafana dashboard in config/grafana to improve the logging outputs - currently it shows the timestamp and level, followed by the "Parsed fields" in badges and then the "Log line" as text. The log line is just `[IssueID] LogLevel [logger.js]`. I would like this to instead be `Timestamp LogLevel [SourceContext] :  Message` for the worker logs, and for the "Parsed fields" to be removed from the log output on the dashboard.

title: Update chat issues considering PromptKit
description: As stated in the CLAUDE.md, I want to use PromptKit for the chat UI components. Update the fleece issues related to implementation of the chat UI in the React Web Client after researching what is available in the PromptKit components. Include the ability to answer questions and approve plans, which is currently included in the Blazor client but not in the fleece issues.

title: Decouple client, server and worker with AG-UI
description:
The AG-UI message protocol should be used instead of the custom/translated message formats that are used in the application. This should be done as follows:

- The worker containers should translate the messages and events emitted by the Claude Agent SDK to the messages and events from the A2A library available with `npm install @a2a-js/sdk`.
- These should be received and handled by the server, and then translated into the AG-UI  protocol and passed on to the client.
- The client should then render these messages.
- The Microsoft Agent Framework contains an implementation of AG-UI in the Nuget package Microsoft.Agents.AI.Hosting.AGUI.AspNetCore. Review the documentation at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/agent-framework/integrations/ag-ui/?pivots=programming-language-csharp as well as other Microsoft Agent Framework tools to consider whether the entire worker architecture could be abstracted as an implementation of IChatClient, or if it should remain as is.

Review the online documentation for these libraries to understand what needs to be done here.

title: Implement workflow context store
description:
Create the workflow context store for managing accumulated state between nodes.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/IWorkflowContextStore.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowContextStore.cs`

## Interface Methods
- `GetContextAsync(executionId)` - Get full context for execution
- `SetValueAsync(executionId, key, value)` - Set a context variable
- `GetValueAsync(executionId, key)` - Get a specific value
- `MergeNodeOutputAsync(executionId, nodeId, output)` - Store node output

## Context Model
\`\`\`csharp
public record WorkflowContext {
    string ExecutionId;
    string WorkflowId;
    string WorkingDirectory;
    JsonElement TriggerData;
    Dictionary<string, NodeOutput> NodeOutputs;  // Completed node outputs
    Dictionary<string, JsonElement> Variables;   // User-defined variables
    List<WorkflowArtifact> Artifacts;            // Produced artifacts
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation
- Store context as JSON file: `.workflows/context_{executionId}.json`
- In-memory cache with ConcurrentDictionary
- Persist on every write for crash recovery

title: Add filter panel
description:
Next to the search box in the toolbar, add a filter button that allows the issue graph to be filtered. 'f' should be the keyboard shortcut for this button (similar to how '/' is the shortcut for searching).

This should expand the toolbar to be two rows deep, with the second row showing the filter query (similar to how GitHub filtering works). The queries should be in the same format as introduced in these Fleece PRs:

- https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/pull/72
- https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/pull/73

Filtering should happen on "enter" being pressed. Add a '?' button next to the text box that shows a popup with some instructions on how to use the query language.

title: Extract Modal and ConfirmModal components
description:
Create reusable Modal and ConfirmModal components to replace duplicate modal patterns in AgentPrompts.razor (Add/Edit and Delete modals) and ProjectDetail.razor (conflict modal). Include proper backdrop, header, body, and footer slots with consistent styling.

Test with bunit tests and with playwright MCP using a mock container created with mock.sh

title: Don't auto open issues panel on mobile - add a button
description:
When an issue is tapped on mobile the side bar opens immediately, costing the screen. This shouldn't happen - add an extra open button next to the edit button that only shows up on mobile. This is the button that will need to be tapped for the panel to open. Otherwise, the panel should remain closed.

Also, remove the exit button from the issue details side panel and put a open/close button in the title bar. This can replace the dark mode button, which can be moved to the settings.

This button should always be there, just disabled on the pages that don't have a side bar.

title: Allow stacked PRs
Create service mocks
description:
Create mocks for all services with fake data sets. The aim is to provide an environment switch for the application that, when enabled, swaps out the live services for the mocks. This will allow a test application to be run that does not persist and affect any production data.

Create a test data store that has in memory persistence to allow adding and deleting data.

Much of this probably already exists in the test libraries. Do not recreate it - migrate it to the main project into a test folder and refactor the tests to use this data.

title: Fix issue agent merging
description: The FleeceChangeDetectionService and merging of issues into main appears to adopt the changes from the issue agent branch entirely rather than merge the issues from the main branch and the issues from the issue agent branch using the timestamp sensitive merging algorithm provided by Fleece.Core. Review this and update it to use the merging algorithm for both the diff calculation and the merging of accepted.

title: Test
Unit and Integration Tests
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Set up testing infrastructure and write comprehensive tests.

**Tasks:**
1. Configure Vitest for unit testing
2. Set up React Testing Library

title: Get plan files straight from the .claude/plans folder
description: The plans tab in the session window is intended to show all of the plans that exist for the current Claude Code agent. It currently doesn't work, often showing errors or old plans from unrelated agents. The plans shown should be read directly from the projects/{project}/.clones/{branch-id}/.claude/plans folder, update this so it just looks in that folder directly for plan files.

title: Fix client and worker logging
description: Update the Grafana dashboards to show the client and worker logs. Currently the worker logs and client telemetry panels are empty. Review the overall Grafana dashboard setup and suggest improvements that can be made. The current dashboard mostly shows logs in JSON.

title: Reduce streaming logs for GitHub
description:
The GitHub PR sync feature floods the application logs. Please remove these logs as the feature is working correctly. Example of logs below:

```
info: Homespun.Features.GitHub.GitHubService[0]
      PR sync completed: 0 imported, 3 updated, 0 removed, 0 errors
info: Homespun.Features.GitHub.GitHubSyncPollingService[0]
      PR sync for nick-boey/Fleece: 0 imported, 3 updated, 0 removed

title: Fix auto branch ID issue creation
description: When issues are created or the title is changed, the working branch ID should be automatically generated from the title using the mini prompt. This used to work, but has since stopped - I must go into the issue edit panel and click the generate button manually. Review the issue and fix it.

title: Stop streaming messages
description: In the docker agent execution mode, messages are streamed word-by-word from the agent container all the way to the UI. This is causing some issues with the ordering of some messages, and tool usages and thinking messages are getting confused. Instead of this, just show complete messages in the UI and attempt to do this at the source by either disabling the streaming of partial messages in the worker container's use of the agent SDK.

title: Switch rendering state for series and parallel issues
description: The rendering of series and parallel types in the issue graph is reversed - series issues look like parallel and parallel issues look like series. Swap this.

title: Update graph rendering
description:
Make the following updates to the graph rendering on the project page.

- Merged and closed PRs are not shown in the task graph. They should be shown at the top, above the "Issues" divider. Only the most recent 5 should be shown, with a button for loading 5 more PRs at the very top of the list.
- Add markers for PR and agent status to each issue in the graph. If a PR is linked to an issue, a PR badge should show up with the PR number, that when clicked takes the user to GitHub. The agent status should show whether the agent is currently working. This has been implemented in previous versions of the graph rendering component but has since disappeared, review the codebase to port this behaviour over and clean up the old graph display.
- Add a marker showing the type of the issue.
- Show all merged PRs and issues with circles. Issues without descriptions should be hollow circles and issues with descriptions should be solid circles. The colour of the circle should be based on the agent status.

title: Fix issues with worktree and branch naming
description:
When a new worktree is created for an issue, it currently creates the new worktree with a default automatically generated branch name. It appears that this is being persisted independent to the {group}/{type}/{branch-id} pattern, so when these details are changed for an issue they are not recalculated and updated prior to creating the branch and worktree.

Create an integration test to confirm that this does occur, then fix it - the branch name and worktree folder name should always match and should always be recalculated just before creating the branch and worktree.

Use the Playwright MCP and a mock build of the website to confirm this issue as well. The mock issue and branch data may need to be extended to show this behaviour.

This appears to be related to the issue X4LlBY - the issue that is displayed in the

title: Rearrange project view
description:
The project timeline view should be rearranged as follows.

Add a dividing element between the merged PRs and current PRs. This can be the same height as the normal issue items - it should just be a horizontal line and some small text saying "CURRENT PRs" (implying that the current PRs are below the line).

Under this line, keep the branched structure that the current PRs have.

Add another line with the text "ISSUES" under that. Issues with parent issues that connect them to the tree or the status "next" should be displayed under the current PRs. If they have the status "next", the issues should just attach to the most recently merged PR.

Then, below this instead of having the "orphaned" issues grouped by their "groups" and shown as branching off the most recently merged PR, just put these all in a flat list below that do not have lines connecting them to the PRs above but show the issue symbol in the first lane.

title: Use flat worktree structure
description:
Use a flat worktree structure instead of the nested directory structure that is currently being used.

Currently, branches are named as `{group}/{type}/{branch-id}+{issue-id}`, and the worktree structure is similar. Instead of this, branches should be changed to just be `{type}/{branch-id}+{issue-id}` and the worktree structure should be flattened by changing the worktree path to:

```
- Project folder
  - /main (default branch)
  - /.worktrees
    - /{type}+{branch-id}+{issue-id}
````

Backwards compatibility is not required - we will recreate the folder structure as required for this change.

title: Fix bug with issue diff calculation failing
Create workflow templates
description:
Create built-in workflow templates for common use cases.

- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Templates/WorkflowTemplates.cs`

title: Add web client build and test to CI and CD
description: A React web client was recently added to the repository under src/Homespun.Web. However, building and testing of this client has not been added to the CI and CD GitHub action files. Please add this.

title: Make agent starting fully asynchronous
description: When the start agent button is clicked the UI pauses on the modal while the clone is created and the session is started. This should be updated such that the starting of the agent happens in the background (it is already executed on the server, the client shouldn't need to wait). If there is an error, a notification should pop up.

title: Fix display of edges with new issue creation
Add bUnit component tests
description:
Add bUnit testing for Blazor components to test rendering and component logic without a browser.

## Overview
bUnit enables testing Blazor components in isolation - fast execution, no browser needed, mocks services and verifies rendering.

## Setup

Add bUnit package to existing test project:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="bunit" Version="1.35.9" />
```

## Test Structure

```
tests/Homespun.Tests/
├── Components/
│   ├── ProjectDetailTests.cs
│   ├── PullRequestTreeTests.cs
│   ├── SessionChatTests.cs
│   └── ...
```

## Test Pattern

```csharp
public class ProjectDetailTests : TestContext
{
    [Test]
    public void ProjectDetail_DisplaysProjectName()
    {
        var mockProjectService = new Mock<ProjectService>();
        mockProjectService.Setup(s => s.GetByIdAsync(It.IsAny<string>()))
            .ReturnsAsync(new Project { Id = "1", Name = "Test Project" });

        Services.AddSingleton(mockProjectService.Object);

        var cut = RenderComponent<ProjectDetail>(parameters =>
            parameters.Add(p => p.Id, "1"));

        cut.WaitForAssertion(() =>
            cut.Find("h1").TextContent.Should().Contain("Test Project"));
    }
}
```

## Components to Test
- ProjectDetail - Project display and actions
- PullRequestTree - PR hierarchy rendering
- SessionChat - Claude session messaging UI
- AgentManagementPanel - Agent status display
- IssueList - Fleece issue display

## Files to Modify
- tests/Homespun.Tests/Homespun.Tests.csproj - Add bUnit package

## Files to Create
- tests/Homespun.Tests/Components/ProjectDetailTests.cs
- tests/Homespun.Tests/Components/PullRequestTreeTests.cs
- tests/Homespun.Tests/Components/SessionChatTests.cs
- (additional component tests as needed)

## Verification
- Run: dotnet test tests/Homespun.Tests
- All bUnit tests should pass

title: Create node configuration panels
description:
Create configuration panels for editing node settings.

## Files to Create
\`\`\`
src/Homespun.Web/src/features/workflows/components/panels/
├── node-config-panel.tsx
├── agent-config.tsx
├── gate-config.tsx
├── action-config.tsx
├── transform-config.tsx
├── workflow-settings-panel.tsx
└── index.ts
\`\`\`

## Panel Features
1. **Node Config Panel**
   - Slide-in panel on node selection
   - Dynamic form based on node type
   - Live validation

2. **Agent Config**
   - Mode selector (plan/build/review)
   - Model selector
   - Prompt template editor with variable hints
   - System prompt customization

3. **Gate Config**

   - Timeout settings
   - Notification channel configuration

4. **Action Config**
   - Action type selector
   - Type-specific form fields
