5. **Transform Config**
   - Operation type selector
   - Expression/script editor
   - Input/output preview

## Pattern Reference
Follow existing panel patterns in `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/issues/components/`

title: Switch to running status after question answer or plan execution starts
description: When the worker receives a notification that a plan is to be executed or that a question has been answered, it should switch the status to "working". The status should only change to "waiting" when the query has returned all messages and is not awaiting plan input or question responses.

title: Fix version numbering
description: The top bar of the web app always shows the same version (0.1.0) regardless of the release version of the container that is currently being used. Ensure that in the container build step in the GitHub actions that the version of the container is passed in to the application such that the correct version of the container is displayed in the top bar of the application.

title: Fix secret injection
description: The secrets page on project is allowing me to successfully save secrets to environments, but they don't appear to be injected into worker containers as environment variables. Please review the container creation code to confirm they are being included.

title: Update mode, prompt and model selection in session window
description:
The controls in the session window above the input box have the following issues:

- The mode selector when clicked says: "Failed to invoke 'SetSessionMode' due to an error on the server" and fails to set the new session mode.
- The model selector doesn't show the model name in text when on mobile.
- There should be a prompt selector that shows the available global and project prompts (defaulting to "No prompt"). When one is selected, it should append the text from the prompt into the end of the chat input after filling in the placeholders.

title: Show issue and PR information on session page
description: There is a series of tabs containing issue, PR information, plans and to do's on the session page in the Homespun.Client Blazor application. Port this over to the React client in Homespun.Web.

title: Cache PRs to file instead of in memory
description: PRs appear to be cached in memory, such that refreshes of the graph are quick if the server hasn't restarted but are slow on first open. Instead of this, cache the PR data to a file in data/src/{project}/pull_requests.jsonl such that it is always fast to load, and PR update are loaded in the background

title: Task Graph Tree View
description:
**ARCHIVED: Merged with Task Graph View (jFBW7H)**

The original plan had separate 'List View' and 'Tree View' components with a toggle between them. The Blazor implementation uses a single unified TaskGraphView that combines both concepts:
- Lane-based positioning provides tree hierarchy visualization
- List-style vertical layout with rows
- No toggle needed - single unified view

The TaskGraphView in jFBW7H now contains all tree visualization functionality.

title: Check agent and main dependencies
description: Confirm that the agent container dependencies more or less match the dependencies that are installed in the main container. At the very least 'fleece' should be installed in the agent container.

title: Set worker mode from prompt setting
description: The agent prompts have a "mode" setting that can be either "Build" or "Plan". It appears for the current Claude implementation that the "BypassPermissions" mode (that maps to "Plan") is always used when the agent is started. The "Plan" mode should be passed in when the prompt mode is "Plan". Please review and fix.

title: Implement YAML/code editor mode
description:
Add YAML/code editor mode as alternative to visual editor.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/workflows/components/workflow-editor/workflow-code-editor.tsx`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/workflows/services/workflow-parser.ts`

## Features
1. **Code Editor**
   - Monaco Editor with YAML syntax highlighting
   - Schema validation with error markers
   - Auto-completion for node types and properties

2. **Mode Toggle**
   - Switch between visual and code modes
   - Sync changes bidirectionally
   - Preserve unsaved changes

3. **Parser Service**
   - `workflowToYaml(workflow)` - Convert to YAML
   - `yamlToWorkflow(yaml)` - Parse YAML to workflow
   - Schema validation

## YAML Schema Example
\`\`\`yaml
title: "My Workflow"
triggers:
  - type: manual
nodes:
  - id: plan
    type: agent
    config:
      mode: plan
      prompt: "Create a plan for {{issue.title}}"
edges:
  - source: plan
    target: review
\`\`\`

title: Improve tool output view
description:
Currently the tool usage and tool output view don't show any of the internal details of the input and output of the tool usage, and are presented as two separate chat bubbles. I believe that these results are sent through from the server and this is mostly a UI issue.

Tool usage and tool results should be contained within a single message bubble, and multiple subsequent uses of tools (without interrupting chat messages) should be combined into a single bubble that show the number of tool usages. It should show the latest two usages that can be expanded to show all of the grouped tool usages. The tool usages should just show up on one line with a basic output, and be expandable to show the full details of the tool usage. Refer to the Homespun.Client Blazor application for an example of this being implemented reasonably well, then port this over to the Homespun.Web React application.

title: Use session log as session test data
description: We had recently set up the mock container to use the JSONL files in tests/data/sessions/demo-project/ and the JsonlSessionLoader to use real session message data for mock session rendering. This had previously worked OK, but is now not being displayed correctly. Could you please update this and then check it using the Playwright MCP tools?

title: Create workflow API endpoints
description:
Create REST API endpoints for workflow CRUD and execution.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Controllers/WorkflowsController.cs`

## Endpoints
\`\`\`
POST   /api/workflows                     # Create workflow
GET    /api/workflows                     # List workflows for project
GET    /api/workflows/{id}                # Get workflow definition
PUT    /api/workflows/{id}                # Update workflow
DELETE /api/workflows/{id}                # Delete workflow

POST   /api/workflows/{id}/execute        # Start execution (manual trigger)
GET    /api/workflows/{id}/executions     # List executions
GET    /api/executions/{id}               # Get execution state
POST   /api/executions/{id}/cancel        # Cancel execution
GET    /api/executions/{id}/context       # Get execution context
\`\`\`

## Implementation
- Use attribute routing with [ApiController]
- Return typed DTOs from Homespun.Shared
- Add proper error handling and validation
- Include projectId query parameter for project-scoped operations

## Pattern Reference
Follow `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Controllers/IssuesController.cs`

title: Update graph rendering for task graph
description: The recent release of Fleece.Core contains a task graph rendering. I would like to update the graph display in this app to default to this rendering mode, where the next tasks are rendered on the left side and the following tasks are rendered on the right side. This behaves kind of like a flattened gannt chart with an abstract display of time. Update the package dependency to the latest version v1.2.0 and plan to update the task rendering using the graph service from that library. See https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/blob/main/src/Fleece.Core/Services/TaskGraphService.cs, https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/blob/main/src/Fleece.Core/Services/Interfaces/ITaskGraphService.cs and https://github.com/nick-boey/Fleece/blob/main/src/Fleece.Cli/Output/TaskGraphRenderer.cs for a text-based example renderer.

title: Thinking messages are out of order with tool usages and displayed as normal chat
description: Thinking messages are streamed in from the chat while an agent is operating. They sometimes overwrite old messages and often appear out of order with the tool use messages (i.e. they always appear just below the last user chat while tool messages accumulate below. Put together a plan for joining and persisting thinking messages that show messages in order and stop appending to messages when interrupted by a tool use message.

title: Add "create above" and "create below" to toolbar
description: Add buttons to the toolbar for creating issues above and below the selected issue. This is usually triggered by the 'o' and 'shift+o' keys on a desktop application, but should be able to be used on mobile with buttons. Choose appropriate icons for the buttons.

title: Add homespun agent orchestration capabilities
description:
I would like to use the Claude Agent SDK to access the various Homespun API endpoints and orchestrate various aspects of the issues, worktrees and branches. There should be two "types" of orchestrating agent - one that simply uses the application endpoints for a particular project and one is started in the repositories' root folder /data/src/{repository} and can have wider impact on the code.

To start with, implement a feature that uses the Haiku model to generate suitable "working branch ID" names for issues when they are first created. It should read the title and create a working branch ID that is 2-4 simple words describing the issue. These should be lowercase and hyphen separated, e.g. "add-agent-orchestration" for this issue. Plan for adding additional prompts and features and for adding buttons throughout the application that can trigger these mini prompts.

Use a TDD approach and ask any clarifying questions as you develop this plan.

title: Change notifications to Blazor Blueprint alerts
description: There is a notifications control in the client. Delete this and use the Blazor Blueprint alerts component instead throughout the application. Refer to the Blazor Blueprint documentation for more information.

title: Fix selection after new issue creation
description: When a new issue is created using the inline editor, the selection goes all the way back to the top. Instead of this, the newly created issue should be selected. Additionally, the IssueDetailPanel doesn't show up when the item is selected - the issue details should be reloaded by the client such that new issues are treated similar to the previous issues immediately upon creation.

title: Add stop button to interrupt agents
description: A stop button should be added to the session window to allow agents to be stopped mid-execution. Prompts should also be allowed to be added mid-execution, similar to how Claude Code CLI works.

title: Fix model types
Task Graph View
description:
Implement the issues tab with TaskGraphView - the primary visualization for issues on a project.

**Primary Component: TaskGraphView**
The TaskGraphView renders issues in a lane-based hierarchical graph with SVG connectors showing parent-child relationships.

**Tasks:**
1. Create useIssues hook with filtering support (GET /api/projects/{projectId}/issues/graph)
2. Port TaskGraphLayoutService algorithm:
   - Convert TaskGraphResponse into render lines
   - Lane assignment for hierarchical depth
   - Handle hidden parent detection when filtered by depth
   - Series execution mode support (vertical chaining)
3. Build TaskGraphRow component showing:
   - Lane-based positioning with indent
   - SVG connectors to parent/child nodes
   - Issue ID, title, type badge, status badge
   - Execution mode indicator (series chain)
   - Agent status with pulse animation
   - Search term highlighting (yellow border)
4. Implement row selection (click or keyboard nav)
5. Add real-time updates via SignalR IssuesChanged event
6. Handle expand/collapse with Space key or double-click for inline details
7. Support move target selection mode (crosshair cursor)

**Render Line Types:**
- TaskGraphIssueRenderLine (issues with lane, parent lane, execution info)
- TaskGraphPrRenderLine (merged/closed PRs)
- TaskGraphSeparatorRenderLine (divider)
- TaskGraphLoadMoreRenderLine (load more PRs)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Issues display in lane-based graph visualization
- Parent-child relationships shown via SVG connectors
- Series vs parallel execution modes are visually distinct
- Clicking a row selects it for keyboard navigation
- Space/double-click expands inline details
- Color coding by type (Bug=red, Task=blue, Feature=green, Chore=gray)
- New/updated issues appear without page refresh

title: Add 'move to parent' and 'move to child' feature
description: Add controls that allow moving an issue to be a child of another issue or to be a parent of another issue. This should move the entire issue tree (which should be accomplished simply by changing the parent of either the first or second selected element). Add these as buttons in the toolbar that has been recently added above the task graph view.

title: Ensure Playwright is installed for testing
JSON message streaming length caused failure
description:
While streaming a session a particular streamed JSON message exceeded the maximum length and caused the session to shut down. Please review whether this issue is still relevant given the changes to message streaming that were recently merged in. This issue seems to happen most often when a session is using the Playwright MCP. If it is still an issue, please put together a plan to fix this issue.

title: Add web client build and test to CI and CD
description: A React web client was recently added to the repository under src/Homespun.Web. However, building and testing of this client has not been added to the CI and CD GitHub action files. Please add this.

title: Add prompt "code" mode
description:
In the prompts pages (both the local and global pages), add a tab that switches between the normal prompts view (with the prompts listed in cards) and a "code" mode that shows a text box with the prompts serialized as raw JSON. I want to be able to use this to be able to "import" and "export" all prompts quickly.

There should be an apply button at the bottom that checks that the JSON is valid and then updates the prompts, and a revert button that deserializes the prompts again and undoes and changes in the text box.

title: Add WorkflowPassData and WorkflowGetContext MCP tools
Update agent status on task graph with SignalR
description:
The agent status in the task graph on the /projects/{project} page doesn't update like the agent status at the top right of the page. Use SignalR to ensure that the agent status is updated on the task graph.

This seems to be particularly the case when an agent is started.

Also make it such that clicking on the agent status badge takes the user to the appropriate session window.

title: Fix search behaviour for search bar
Commit and push changes
description: It should be possible to add --commit and --push flags to the  leece create command to commit and push changes to the issues in the current branch. A  leece commit --push command should also be available to do the same thing.

title: Extract AsyncActionButton component from duplicate loading button patterns
description:
Create a reusable AsyncActionButton component that consolidates the loading button pattern used 8+ times across ProjectDetail.razor, IssueEdit.razor, AgentPrompts.razor, ProjectCreate.razor, FeatureCreate.razor, and Settings.razor. The component should handle loading state, disabled state, and customizable button text/styling.

Test with bunit tests and with playwright MCP using a mock container created with mock.sh

title: Use orange for questions and red for errors
description: Where question and error states are shown, use orange for questions and red for errors. Code should also be monospaced and white rather than the current red colour.

title: Fix session streaming
description: The session window doesn't stream incoming messages from the server - instead, I need to continually refresh the window to show new messages. Fix this such that messages are always streamed to the window when open.

title: Add logging of message content to worker and improve message logging
description:
We have recently status change logging to the workers to determine when the statuses are changing in the workers.

However, it is difficult to do this without the message content as context. Add "debug" logging that includes tool uses (just the tool information) and the message content so that we can understand what is occurring. This should be configurable when the agent containers are started - update the server code to always set this "debug" flag to true (for now) when the containers are started in the DockerAgentExecutionService.

Also, review the Grafana dashboard in config/grafana to improve the logging outputs - currently it shows the timestamp and level, followed by the "Parsed fields" in badges and then the "Log line" as text. The log line is just `[IssueID] LogLevel [logger.js]`. I would like this to instead be `Timestamp LogLevel [SourceContext] :  Message` for the worker logs, and for the "Parsed fields" to be removed from the log output on the dashboard.

title: AgentManagementPanel doesn't refresh session list via SignalR
description:
The AgentManagementPanel component (displayed on /sessions page) shows a list of active sessions with status badges and uptime, but does not subscribe to SignalR events. The session list won't show live status updates - users must refresh the page to see new agent statuses or when sessions stop.

Issues:
- Does NOT implement IAsyncDisposable
- Does NOT have SignalR subscription
- Status indicators and uptime calculations become stale
- New sessions won't appear without refresh

This is the same bug pattern that was fixed in IssueDetailPanel (PR #222).

Fix: Add SignalR subscription and refresh the sessions list when events are received.

title: Add scroll and float to project title and toolbar
description: The project title and toolbar control on the project issues tab should start in its current position at the top of the page but float down when the page is scrolled such that it is always at the top of the screen and visible. On mobile screens when the toolbar is too small to fit all of the controls on one row it should maintain one row and scroll horizontally.

title: Combine global and project prompts
description:
There is a lot of duplication between the global prompts and the project prompts, and it looks like they are copied in by default. Instead of this, consider the project prompts to be in addition to the global prompts. Use the prompt names as keys - if the project prompts contains a new prompt with the same key, the project prompts should override the global prompt.

Add a control to the project prompts page that allows the user to create a new prompt based on an existing global prompt - this should create a new project prompt that copies the global prompt in for editing.

title: Persist permissions per-session and allow them to change mid-session
description:
The permissions associated with a session (i.e. the "session mode" which can be either "Build" or "Plan") is a property of the agent/worker container, and can actually be changed mid-prompt (i.e. is not necessarily sent with every prompt). Currently these seem to be persisted at the client level, and when changed in one session page changes when another session page is navigated to.

Instead of this, I would like the session mode to be pulled as part of the session information. When this session mode is changed, I would like this to be passed down to the server and then to the worker container immediately, without requiring a new prompt to be sent. There should be server and worker endpoints available to perform this function.

title: Mount correct .claude folder
description:
Currently a .claude folder is created per issue next to the workdir folder. However the .Claude folder that is mounted for the agent container is one level up, in the project folder.

Change the mounted folder to the one created per issue to avoid contention over session storage.

title: Add error symbols to agent status
description: When agent containers are in an error state they don't have an icon in the agent status in the top bar. Add a red dot for agents that are in error state.

title: Allow type to be changed in inline issue creator
CurrentPullRequestDetailPanel doesn't refresh session status via SignalR
description:
The CurrentPullRequestDetailPanel component displays session status but does not subscribe to SignalR events (SessionStarted, SessionStatusChanged, SessionStopped). The session status badge will show stale data after session state changes on the server. Users must navigate away and back to see the current state.

This is the same bug pattern that was fixed in IssueDetailPanel (PR #222).

Fix: Add SignalR subscription following the IssueDetailPanel.razor pattern:
1. Add @implements IAsyncDisposable
2. Declare private HubConnection? _hubConnection
3. Call SetupSignalRConnectionAsync() from OnInitializedAsync()
4. Subscribe to SessionStarted, SessionStatusChanged, SessionStopped events
5. Implement proper DisposeAsync() cleanup

title: Sort issues by age
description:
Update the sorting logic for the main issue graph to group the "next" issues at the root of the tree by priority, then sort by oldest at the top to newest at the bottom. Status and type can be ignored in the sorting other than filtering out complete, closed and archived issues. Whether an issue has a description can be ignored.

Let me know if this is embedded within the Fleece.Core library so I can edit that if need be.

title: Object reference issue when editing status
description: When running a command like  leece edit gsVYAO --status complete the issue is updated but an 'Error: Object reference not set to an instance of an object' is also returned

title: Show PR status in details and timeline
description: A PR should show whether it is ready to merge and if all checks pass, fail or are running in the issue detail. This should be used to drive agent options such as auto rebasing or asking an agent to fix all checks.

title: AskUserQuestion tool fails in local agent execution mode - CLI handles it internally via stdin
description: When Claude calls AskUserQuestion in a session using LocalAgentExecutionService, the CLI handles it as an internal stdin-based tool rather than delegating through the streaming protocol. The CLI prompts 'Answer questions?' on stdin, times out after ~19 seconds with no response, and returns an error tool result. The TCS-based interception in LocalAgentExecutionService (lines 264-277) never fires because either TryParseAskUserQuestion returns null or the CLI handles the tool before content_block_stop arrives. SendToolResultAsync cannot work because the CLI doesn't give the caller a chance to provide the tool result. Need to investigate how to configure the CLI to delegate AskUserQuestion to the caller in streaming mode, or use a different mechanism to intercept and answer questions.

title: Migration Completion
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Complete the migration with documentation and deployment updates.

**Tasks:**
1. Update documentation:
   - README.md with React frontend instructions
   - Development setup guide
   - Architecture documentation
   - API integration guide
2. Update CI/CD pipeline:
   - Build React app in pipeline
   - Deploy React app to appropriate environment
   - Update environment configurations
3. Create deprecation plan for Blazor client:
   - Timeline for removal
   - Communication plan
   - Fallback strategy
4. Performance benchmarks:
   - Initial load time comparison
   - Runtime performance comparison
   - Bundle size analysis
5. Create final PR with migration summary:
   - What changed
   - How to use new frontend
   - Known issues/limitations
6. Update docker-compose for production

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Documentation is updated and accurate
- CI/CD deploys React frontend
- Performance meets or exceeds Blazor
- Deprecation plan is documented
- Final PR approved and merged

title: Pull changes from main before creating a new branch
description: When running a new agent a new branch is created from the default branch. When this is done, the recent changes to the default branch should be pulled from main with the fleece issues merged, using the same action that the "pull" button does on the main project page.

title: Add go to next agent button
description: Next to the back button in the agent session page at the top left hand corner of the page there should be a next button that takes the user to the next agent session that is not in a "running" state.

title: Update the agent container to have .NET SDK
description: The agents container uses ASP.NET image as the runtime, it should use the .NET SDK image similar to the main container so that it can build projects.

title: Install Playwright MCP plugin by default
Distinguish stopped sessions from closed sessions
Review parsing of status when loading diff
Fix issues agent issue merging
description:
I just used the issues agent for the first time to add a description to an issue. It successfully modified the issue details in the cloned branch that was created, however the diff view did not seem to pick up that there were changes in the issues - I assume this is because the diff is only loaded once. The issues from the clone and main branch should be re-read whenever the diff view is opened, and when the issues from the cloned branch are to be merged back in.

In addition to this, in the graph view only show the issues that have changed.

title: Create workflow node components
description:
Create custom React Flow node components for each workflow node type.

## Files to Create
\`\`\`
src/Homespun.Web/src/features/workflows/components/nodes/
├── agent-node.tsx
├── gate-node.tsx
├── action-node.tsx
├── transform-node.tsx
├── node-base.tsx           # Shared base styling
├── node-handles.tsx        # Custom connection handles
└── index.ts
\`\`\`

## Node Component Features
1. **Visual Styling**
   - Distinct colors/icons per node type
   - Status indicators (pending, running, completed, failed)
   - Selection highlighting

2. **Handles**
   - Input handles (top)
   - Output handles (bottom)
   - Multiple outputs for branching nodes

3. **Content**
   - Node title
   - Brief configuration summary
   - Status badge

## Pattern Reference
Follow existing component patterns in `src/Homespun.Web/src/components/ui/`

title: Fix tab key for issue levels
Show command
description: Create a new  leece show <ID> command that shows all of the details related to an issue.

title: Issue History (Undo/Redo)
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement undo/redo functionality for issues.

**Tasks:**
1. Create useIssueHistory hook wrapping:
   - GET /api/projects/{projectId}/issues/history/state
   - POST /api/projects/{projectId}/issues/history/undo
   - POST /api/projects/{projectId}/issues/history/redo
2. Add undo/redo buttons to issues toolbar
3. Display history state (canUndo, canRedo)
4. Implement keyboard shortcuts:
   - Ctrl+Z (Cmd+Z on Mac): Undo
   - Ctrl+Shift+Z (Cmd+Shift+Z on Mac): Redo
5. Update UI immediately after undo/redo
6. Invalidate issue queries after history change

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Undo reverts last issue change
- Redo restores undone change
- Keyboard shortcuts work globally on issues page
- Buttons show disabled state when unavailable
- UI updates reflect history changes

title: Add CTRL+C shortcut to stop sessions and go back to session page
description:
When in a session page, ctrl+c should stop the agent and go back to the session list page.

Clicking the stop button should also take the user back to the session list page.

title: Update CLAUDE.md with instructions for developing React client and add vitest
description:
Update the CLAUDE.md with instructions for developing the React client. Remove the references to the Blazor front end as we will just be focusing on the front end. In these instructions, ensure that shadcn/ui with Base UI and default Tailwind are used throughout as much as possible, with the default theme of shadcn/ui (which we will style ourselves later). For the chat components, I want to use the prompt-kit library pre-built components: https://www.prompt-kit.com/docs/installation.

Add notes about using a Vertical Slice Architecture with features organised at the top level if this doesn't already exist in the CLAUDE.md.

I want to test the application using vitest. Please add this in and add instructions to the CLAUDE.md if this doesn't already exist.

title: Fix PR badge link from sessions to session
description: The PR agent status badge links to "/sessions/{id}" rather than "/session/{id}", which is the correct link. Fix this.

title: DAG Execution
description:
Add full DAG execution with parallel nodes, conditional branching, and real-time updates.

## Deliverables
1. DAG builder and cycle detection
2. Parallel node execution with Task.WhenAny
3. Join synchronization for converging branches
4. WorkflowBranch MCP tool for conditional paths
5. Conditional edge evaluation
6. SignalR WorkflowHub for real-time updates
7. Integration tests for parallel execution

title: Create sessions on branches
description:
I want to be able to create new sessions on branches rather than on issues. Add a new button to the branches tab that opens a modal. The modal should prompt the user for a new branch name, with OK and Cancel. When a valid name is entered and OK is clicked, the latest changes to main should be pulled, then a clone with that branch name should be created. This should also be pulled in case there is a branch already with that name on the remote.

Following this, a new agent session should be started on that branch.

title: Fix display of status
description: It appears that the agent status values for closed, complete, review and in progress are mixed up in the React client when compared to the enums in C#. Please review and check whether it is possible to use stronger typing in the Open API conversion of types from server to client. When I view the same issue in the React and Blazor clients they show different statuses. I am reasonably sure that the Blazor client is correct.

title: Create scripts for running dev and prod containers
Always show agent status in nav bar
description: The nav bar should always show the agent status on the right hand side even on small screens. The breadcrumbs should have a horizontal scroll to suit, and the main app title should be removed on small screens.

title: Branch source not mounted to agent worker containers
description: When a new agent container is created to work on an issue, it should have the branch folder of the parent container (/data/src/{project}/.clones/{branch}) mounted to the path , and the Claude Code Executable should be started in this directory. Currently the volume does not appear to be mounted, and the agent has difficulty in finding the correct source files to read and edit.

title: Fix working status rings and prevent multiple concurrent agents
description: The issues graph used to contain rings around each of the nodes that had an agent working on them, with the colour of the ring representing the status. This is no longer shown, likely due to the changes in PR #526. Please review and fix the issue.

title: Consolidate project and src folder
description:
Currently when a session is started in Docker Agent Execution mode, a .claude/ folder and a workdir/ folder is mounted to the container as volumes, allowing the container to persist it's memory. This is logged as such:

```
info: Homespun.Features.ClaudeCode.Services.DockerAgentExecutionService[0]
      Mounting working directory: /home/azureuser/.homespun-container/data/src/Homespun/.clones/bug+fix-status-update+vHU2gY/workdir -> /workdir
info: Homespun.Features.ClaudeCode.Services.DockerAgentExecutionService[0]
      Mounting .claude directory: /home/azureuser/.homespun-container/data/projects/default/issues/vHU2gY/.claude -> /home/homespun/.claude
```

While the mount targed on the worker container is correct, there is some confusion in where these folders are being created. They should be consolidate d in the projects folder and under the same project title. This means that the above should read:

```
info: Homespun.Features.ClaudeCode.Services.DockerAgentExecutionService[0]
      Mounting working directory: /home/azureuser/.homespun-container/data/projects/Homespun/.clones/bug+fix-status-update+vHU2gY/workdir -> /workdir
info: Homespun.Features.ClaudeCode.Services.DockerAgentExecutionService[0]
      Mounting .claude directory: /home/azureuser/.homespun-container/data/projects/Homespun/.clones/bug+fix-status-update+vHU2gY/.claude -> /home/homespun/.claude
```

The changes are:
- Repository working directory is created in the `projects` folder, not the `src` folder. Otherwise this is OK
- .claude folder is created in the `projects` folder but with the project repository name included in the path. The .claude folder should sit beside the workdir folder on the application server file system, so should include the full branch name similar to the working directory.

title: Projects List Page
description:
Implement the projects list page as the application home page.

**Tasks:**
1. Create useProjects TanStack Query hook wrapping GET /api/projects
2. Build projects list with cards showing:
   - Project name
   - Repository path
   - Active issues count (if available)
   - Recent activity timestamp
3. Add "Create Project" button linking to /projects/new
4. Implement project deletion with confirmation dialog
5. Add loading skeleton states during fetch
6. Handle empty state (no projects) with call-to-action
7. Add error state with retry button

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Projects are fetched and displayed on page load
- Create button navigates to create page
- Delete removes project after confirmation
- Loading skeletons show during initial fetch
- Empty state encourages creating first project

title: Stop auto-scrolling
description: Currently the session chat window is auto-scrolled down at all times. Please change this such that the auto-scroll is only active when the window is already at the bottom of the chat, and otherwise just stop the auto-scrolling (there is already a button to pull the scroll to the bottom, which is sufficient).

title: Fix agent run button
description: The agent run button on each issue row doesn't do anything when clicked. It should open a small context pane that contains the normal agent run control panel for running an agent. Please review and fix.

title: Create new issues in correct positions
description: When I have a parent with multiple children and I create a new issue between two of them, I expect it to inherit the parent of the issue that was selected when the 'create issue below' was clicked. This behaviour was successfully implemented in the Blazor app in PRs 293, 299 and 326.

title: Branches Tab
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement the branches/worktrees management tab.

**Tasks:**
1. Create branches tab content in project detail
2. Create useClones hook for fetching clone/worktree data (GET /api/clones)
3. List local worktrees with:
   - Branch name
   - Status (clean, dirty, conflicts)
   - Associated issue/PR (with link)
   - Last commit info (message, date, author)
4. List remote branches not yet pulled (separate section)
5. Add actions per worktree:
   - Pull latest changes
   - Start agent (with prompt selector)
   - Delete worktree (with confirmation, warn if not merged)
6. Create new worktree from remote branch action
7. Add "Refresh All" button to pull all worktrees
8. Show real-time status updates if available
9. Handle loading and empty states

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Local worktrees display with status
- Remote branches show separately
- Pull/delete actions work via API
- New worktrees can be created from remote branches
- Warnings show for unmerged deletions

title: Manual update to main branch
description: The main branch should have the ability to have all changes manually pulled to bring the branch up to date. This should also merge any fleece issues. A button should be available on the branches page next to "run agent" if the main branch is behind the remote.

title: Update issues creation items
description: The fleece issues that are siblings to this one are for the creation of an issues page in the React web client. It appears that they are not up to date with the current implementation in the Blazor client, which is to have a toolbar above the issues, keyboard controls for navigating the issue graph and inline creation of issues. Review the current structure of the project issues tab in the Blazor client and update the Fleece issues to suit.

title: Implement EnrichedCloneCard component
description:
## Overview Create the EnrichedCloneCard component to display individual clone information with linked issue/PR data. ## Implementation ### 1. Create EnrichedCloneCard Component **File:** `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/clones/components/enriched-clone-card.tsx` ```typescript import { GitBranch, Trash2, ExternalLink, AlertCircle } from "lucide-react" import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button" import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge" import { Card, CardContent } from "@/components/ui/card" import { Link } from "@tanstack/react-router" import { formatDistanceToNow } from "date-fns" import type { EnrichedCloneInfo } from "@/api/generated/types.gen" import { AlertDialog, AlertDialogAction, AlertDialogCancel, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogTrigger, } from "@/components/ui/alert-dialog" export interface EnrichedCloneCardProps { clone: EnrichedCloneInfo projectId: string onDelete: () => void isDeleting?: boolean } export function EnrichedCloneCard({ clone, projectId, onDelete, isDeleting, }: EnrichedCloneCardProps) { const branchName = clone.clone.expectedBranch ?? clone.clone.branch?.replace("refs/heads/", "") ?? "unknown" const folderName = clone.clone.folderName const commitSha = clone.clone.headCommit?.slice(0, 7) ?? "unknown" return (
{/* Left side: Clone info */}
{/* Branch name and folder */}
{branchName} {folderName !== branchName && ( ({folderName}) )}
{/* Commit info */}
Commit: {commitSha}
{/* Linked Issue */} {clone.linkedIssue && (
Issue: {clone.linkedIssue.title}
)} {/* Issue not found warning */} {clone.linkedIssueId && !clone.linkedIssue && (
Issue {clone.linkedIssueId} not found (deleted)
)} {/* Linked PR */} {clone.linkedPr && (
PR: #{clone.linkedPr.number}: {clone.linkedPr.title}
)} {/* Deletion reason */} {clone.isDeletable && clone.deletionReason && (
{clone.deletionReason}
)}
{/* Right side: Actions */}
{clone.isDeletable && ( Delete Clone Are you sure you want to delete the clone for branch "{branchName}"? This will permanently remove the clone folder. Cancel Delete )}
) } function IssueStatusBadge({ status }: { status: string }) { const variant = { complete: "secondary", archived: "secondary", closed: "secondary", open: "default", progress: "default", review: "default", }[status.toLowerCase()] ?? "outline" return {status} } function PrStatusBadge({ status }: { status: string }) { const variant = { Merged: "secondary", Closed: "destructive", Open: "default", }[status] ?? "outline" return {status} } ``` ## Test Cases **File:** `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/clones/components/enriched-clone-card.test.tsx` 1. `renders clone branch name and folder` 2. `renders linked issue with status badge` 3. `renders linked PR with external link` 4. `shows delete button when isDeletable is true` 5. `hides delete button when isDeletable is false` 6. `shows deletion reason when provided` 7. `shows warning when issue not found` 8. `opens confirmation dialog on delete click` 9. `calls onDelete when confirmed` ## Dependencies - Requires EnrichedCloneInfo type from generated API - Uses existing Badge and Card components - Uses existing AlertDialog component

title: Implement GateNodeExecutor
description:
Create the gate node executor for human approval workflows.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/NodeExecutors/GateNodeExecutor.cs`

## Features
1. **Approval Flow**
   - Pause execution at gate
   - Broadcast GatePending event via SignalR
   - Wait for approval via API endpoint
   - Resume with approval result

2. **Approval Types**
   - Single approver (one approval needed)
   - Multi-approver (configurable count)
   - Request changes (with feedback loop)

3. **Timeout Handling**
   - Optional timeout configuration
   - Auto-approve, auto-reject, or escalate options

4. **Notification**
   - Integrate with notification service
   - Support Slack/email/webhook channels

5. **Context Display**
   - Show configurable context keys to approver
   - Display summary from previous nodes

## API Endpoint
Add to WorkflowsController:
\`POST /api/executions/{id}/gates/{nodeId}/approve\`

title: Test issue
description: Example issue

title: Put "next" issues with descriptions at the top
description: The Fleece library was just updated to version 1.4.0 to use a new sorting logic for the graph layout service. Update the library dependency to update the graph layout in this application. Review the PRs references in the release notes on GitHub for this new library version. Run build and fix all tests as required.

title: Issue details side panel should squash the main view on desktop, not overlay it
description: The issue details side panel currently overlays the main view when it is open, hiding the PR and edit buttons on desktop. It should instead reduce the width of the display on desktop and only overlay the main view on mobile screens.

title: Update CLAUDE.md with instructions for developing React client and add vitest
description:
Update the CLAUDE.md with instructions for developing the React client. Remove the references to the Blazor front end as we will just be focusing on the front end. In these instructions, ensure that shadcn/ui with Base UI and default Tailwind are used throughout as much as possible, with the default theme of shadcn/ui (which we will style ourselves later). For the chat components, I want to use the prompt-kit library pre-built components: https://www.prompt-kit.com/docs/installation.

Add notes about using a Vertical Slice Architecture with features organised at the top level if this doesn't already exist in the CLAUDE.md.

I want to test the application using vitest. Please add this in and add instructions to the CLAUDE.md if this doesn't already exist.

title: Clearing context and executing a plan still results in a container entering "waiting" status
Node Executors
description:
Implement all node type executors with their specific behaviors.

## Deliverables
1. AgentNodeExecutor - Run Claude agents with prompt templating
2. GateNodeExecutor - Human approval workflow
3. ActionNodeExecutor - Automated actions (PR, merge, notify)
4. TransformNodeExecutor - Data transformation
5. WorkflowPassData and WorkflowGetContext MCP tools

title: Deleting worktrees and branches doesn't check whether remotes exist
description: When deleting a worktree it seems to be assumed that a remote branch exists. When I select the 'delete remote as well' option, it throws an exception as the remote branch doesn't exist. Check whether this exists prior to attempting to delete the remote.

title: Extract EmptyState component for empty list placeholders
description:
Create a reusable EmptyState component to replace empty list placeholder patterns in Projects.razor and AgentPrompts.razor. Support icon, title, description, and optional action button.

Test with bunit tests and with playwright MCP using a mock container created with mock.sh

title: Project links on left hand side don't work
description:
The project links in the left side bar don't work - clicking them changes the address in the address bar but the page does not navigate to the new page. Test this on a mock instance of the app started with ./scripts/mock.sh and use the Playwright tools to investigate. The indentation is also not correct, it should be pushed further to the right so it is past the other links (which have some padding due to the presence of icons.

Add an icon for "Sessions", something like a chat bubble would work.

title: Fix Dockerfile to specify version of Fleece CLI to install
description: Old versions of the Fleece CLI are being cached in the build process. This is installed in @{Dockerfile.base}. Change this command so that it installs a specific version of the Fleece CLI, and update the CLAUDE.md with a note that whenever the Fleece.Core version is changed in the Homespun project that the Fleece CLI version in the Dockerfile must be updated to match it.

title: Complete implementation of issue agent
description: There is a fleece issue related to creating agents that operate on a clone of the main branch, change the fleece issues and merge in the issues without using Git. The backend has been implemented for this, please put together a plan for completing the remaining tasks required for this feature.

title: Remove Azure Container Apps execution mode
description: Due to the slow performance issues with running development workloads on Azure Container Apps, just get rid of Azure Container Apps and keep the Docker execution mode. Remove the infra folder and it's content as well as this was set up to work with ACA.

title: Select on search
description: The highlighting of issues that match the search query happens when "ENTER" is pressed. Instead of this, they should be highlighted when the input text changes, and the selection of the first element should happen when "ENTER" is pressed. Currently pressing ENTER doesn't select any issues at all, and focus on the main task graph view is lost.

title: Question tool
description:
The application can't currently ask the user questions - a tool is shown with the JSON output but there is no way for the user to provide input in the expected manner.

The documentation (in Python and Typescript) is here: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/user-input#handle-clarifying-questions
Please note, however, that the Claude Agent SDK being used by the application is a C# port - add additional functionality as required to get this to work. You can also ask an example question to understand the format, however do not expect this question to be answered as the user cannot interact with the question.

Being asked a question should put the session in a waiting state, and the user should be able to navigate to the page and answer the questions by clicking on option buttons or typing in alternate answers into the chat. I am happy for this to occur in the chat stream itself, it does not require modification to the input box.

title: Add all remaining buttons to toolbar
description:
The toolbar in the Homespun.Web React frontend application is missing the following buttons that are otherwise in the Homespun.Client Blazor frontend:

- Controls for number of visible levels
- Controls for moving an issue to be parent or child of another issue

Furthermore, the task graph was not entirely updated to allow scrolling all the way horizontally to see the entire issue title. The titles are truncated after a number of characters. Fix this, and to aid visibility also remove the issue hash from the issue row information. The relevant information (type and status) should be on the far left, followed by the title.

title: Inline issue details panel
description: Issue rows should be able to expand when double clicked, showing all of the information in the current issue detail panel such as the PR status, description and so on. The issue detail panel should be refactored to suit this control usage, as we will not be using the detail panel in it's current position anymore.

title: Agents aren't being started with issue template variables replaced
description: When an agent is staretd on an issue, the template comes in with {{title}} text rather than the actual injected titles. Ensure that these are injected with template variables replaced when the prompt is being sent to the agent worker.

title: Fix display of plan controls
description:
The "execute plan" controls are often shown whether or not there is actually an active plan for execution. Review the underlying code that shows and hides the plan controls. It should be updated to be based on the agent worker container status, which checks whether the last message was a plan pending message. When a new message comes through from the agent this will update the agent status to "working" or something else, and the UI should remove the plan execution control.

Confirm that this will work to remove the plan controls without excessively polling the agent containers for their status.

title: Implement DAG builder with cycle detection
description:
Create DAG building and validation utilities for workflow graphs.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowDagBuilder.cs`

## Features
1. **Build DAG from workflow definition**
   - Parse nodes and edges into graph structure
   - Identify entry nodes (no incoming edges)
   - Identify exit nodes (no outgoing edges)

2. **Cycle Detection**
   - Use DFS/BFS to detect cycles
   - Return clear error message with cycle path
   - Validate before workflow save/execution

3. **Topological Sort**
   - Return execution order for linear mode
   - Handle multiple valid orderings

4. **Utilities**
   - `GetReadyNodes(completedNodes)` - Nodes with all dependencies satisfied
   - `GetDependencies(nodeId)` - Get upstream dependencies
   - `GetDependents(nodeId)` - Get downstream dependents

## Pattern Reference
Similar to cycle detection in `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Fleece/Services/FleeceService.cs:WouldCreateCycleAsync`

title: Update the sessions page using SignalR
description:
The sessions page shows the active agent sessions, but the status is often out of sync with the agent status display at the top right hand corner of the screen.

I believe this is due to a SignalR wiring issue, where the live results are not being pulled in. Review this and update it such that they are kept up to date.

title: Split sync into pulling main and committing issues
description:
Split the sync button into "sync" and "push". Sync should stash the issues changes, pull the latest changes from the origin, merge the issues and serialise them but do not commit and push the issues.

The push button should first perform the sync functions and then commit and push to main.

title: Fix plan status
description:
Occasionally an agent will emit additional "plan pending" messages **after** a plan has been approved and is already being executed. Logs are below showing this, although the agent was clearly working in the UI while the agent was executing tools and sending back thought messages.

Please add additional logs around this behaviour to diagnose further. Include additional logs on the worker container when messages are being sent to the server. Ideally the latest message should be used to set the worker status (i.e. if it is a tool use or a thinking message, it should be 'working', if it is a plan pending or question it is set appropriately and 'waiting' is only set when the "subtype='success'" message appears.

Add logs for when the worker changes it status, i.e. "Worker status changed from 'waiting' to 'working'" so I can also check that the client is successfully pulling the statuses live from the worker rather than storing them.

title: Check log access works
Fix status issues for plan execution and waiting
description:
Review the worker logs for issues n8kTk6 and YfCSMn.

For issue n8kTk6, the status was left at "awaiting plan execution" while the agent was working, and for issue YfCSMn, the status was set to "waiting" but the agent kept emitting prompts.

I believe the worker status updates were improved in a recent PR, but this was evidently insufficient. Identify the issue from the logs and fix it.

title: Test run E2E tests
description:
I have recently updated the version of Chrome that is installed in this browser for the purpose of being able to run E2E tests. Run the E2E tests, then run the development version of the app with `npm run dev` and inspect it with the Playwright MCP tools to confirm that the correct browser versions are installed.

Let me know whether this was successful or if further changes are required to the installed browsers.

title: Store and re-render messages
Combine global and project prompts
description:
There is a lot of duplication between the global prompts and the project prompts, and it looks like they are copied in by default. Instead of this, consider the project prompts to be in addition to the global prompts. Use the prompt names as keys - if the project prompts contains a new prompt with the same key, the project prompts should override the global prompt.

Add a control to the project prompts page that allows the user to create a new prompt based on an existing global prompt - this should create a new project prompt that copies the global prompt in for editing.

title: Fix session mode button and model selector
description:
Clicking on the session mode button in the session chat window or using the SHIFT+TAB shortcut doesn't show any change in the session mode in the UI, and doesn't appear to pass the message on to the server or worker.

When changing the model selector it changes just below the slab title in the UI, but the server says "Failed to set model on worker for session 25e2c246-b0e9-47bf-b8a5-a5a1f3290a34".

This relates to the UI changes that were merged in PR #508 and #503.

title: Fix Grafana dashboards to include messages in simplified log lines
Keyboard controls don't place in correct hierarchy
description:
When creating a new issue using the keyboard controls, the TAB and Shift+TAB keys should allow the new issue to move up and down lanes (if available, a node can only move up or down one lane). If created up a lane, it should become the parent of the issue directly above it. If created down a lane, it should become a child to the issue below it.

Think through the other permutations of issues above and below the "new" issue and how the hierarchy should be modified. Ask me if there are any questions. Consider both the parallel and series execution modes.

Add E2E tests for all permutations, modifying the mock data if required to fit these in.

title: Improve tool output
description: Currently the tool UI output just shows raw JSON. Different tools should instead display correctly. Review the Claude Agent SDK documentation for the JSON schema of tool responses and show only a brief summary that can be expanded to show a more detailed response. For now, the Explore, Read and Write tools are the most common but male this generally extendable to all tools. You may run some tools as a test to understand the JSON output

title: Chat window doesn't show messages
description: The chat window in the React Homespun.Web client doesn't show the message text in the responses - it shows up as empty little empty bubbles, with one per message and tool call. Update this such that at least the message text is being rendered to the screen.

title: Improve tool output UI
description: Currently the tool UI output just shows raw JSON. Different tools should instead display correctly. Review the Claude Agent SDK documentation for the JSON schema of tool responses.

title: Chat Message List
description:
Create the chat message display component using PromptKit components with streaming support.

**PromptKit Components to Use:**
- `ChatContainer` - Root container with auto-scroll functionality
- `ChatContainerContent` - Content wrapper for messages
- `ChatContainerScrollAnchor` - Auto-scroll anchor
- `ScrollButton` - Scroll-to-bottom button
- `Message`, `MessageAvatar`, `MessageContent`, `MessageActions`, `MessageAction` - Message display
- `Markdown` - Markdown rendering with syntax highlighting
- `Loader` - Loading state indicators

**Install Commands:**
```bash
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/chat-container.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/message.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/markdown.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/scroll-button.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/loader.json"
```

**Tasks:**
1. Create session page at /sessions/$sessionId
2. Install and configure PromptKit ChatContainer, Message, and Markdown components
3. Build message list using PromptKit Message components:
   - User messages with MessageAvatar and MessageContent (right-aligned styling)
   - Assistant messages with MessageAvatar and MessageContent (left-aligned styling)
   - System messages (centered, muted styling)
4. Create useSession hook to fetch session data
5. Implement auto-scroll using ChatContainer and ScrollButton components
6. Handle message streaming via SignalR:
   - Subscribe to ReceiveMessage event
   - Update message content incrementally
7. Use PromptKit Markdown component for assistant message rendering
8. Add MessageActions with copy, regenerate actions
9. Show timestamps on hover via tooltips
10. Add Loader component for historical message loading state
11. Handle session not found state

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Messages display correctly using PromptKit Message components
- Auto-scroll follows new messages via ChatContainer
- ScrollButton appears when scrolled up
- Streaming messages update in real-time
- Historical sessions load properly with Loader indicator
- Markdown renders correctly with syntax highlighting

title: Show agent status on PRs in the timeline
description: When an agent is running on an issue it correctly displays in the PR/issue graph. However, when it is running on a worktree displayed as a PR it does not display. The linked issues seems to work OK, so please just ensure that the agent status badge is showing on the PR graph.

title: Change planning indicator colour to orange on sessions page
description: When a "plan ready" is shown on the sessions page the dot at the top right corner is the same colour as a "working" session. It should be the same colour (orange) as the working agent in the top right hand corner of the screen. Review the CSS structure of the application to ensure that these are all using the same global style.

title: Session doesn't load when card is clicked
description: When the session card is clicked on the /sessions page, the page redirects to the correct session page route but the chat messages and issue title doesn't load. They do load when the page is refreshed, however. Ensure that this information is pulled correctly when the page is routed to.

title: Update Claude CLI flags
description: Currently the default CLI flags for Claude Code include --system-prompt. Change this to --append-system-prompt to ensure that the default Claude Code system prompts are being used.

title: Clicking on the issue type badge opens context menu to change it
description: Clicking on the type badge of an issue on the projects page should allow the type of an issue to be changed.

title: Timeline caching is very slow
description: In PR #118 we had implemented caching to keep a store of the PR data used for rendering the graph in a window. There appears to be an even longer loading time now than before, however. Please investigate why this is. The behaviour should be (if the PR store exists for a project), to load the PR data from the store and render the graph immediately once the page is opened (assuming that the issue loading is very fast which it should be as it is just parsing JSONL files), then to update this from GitHub data after the page is loaded. Review this behaviour and come up with a plan to improve the performance.

title: Fix the session chat UI
description:
The session chat window has the bypass permissions drop-down in an odd location. Move this to above the chat box so the chat box takes up the full width of the screen.

Change the text box behaviour so that enter creates a new line rather than submitting chat. Ctrl enter or clicking the button should be required for submitting chat.

Add drop-down to switch models between opus, sonnet and haiku.

Add a drop-down to select between pre-written prompts from the agents page. When one is selected, clear the chat input and put it in there with the placeholders replaced.

Auto scroll to bottom when new chat messages arrive, and add a button to go straight to the bottom when the text window is scrolled up a bit.

Add a button for clearing context and restarting chat. Keep old messages in the chat but add a separator.

Use TDD for implementation.

title: Review bugs where issues are being deleted
Write unit tests for Phase 1 components
description:
Write unit tests for the Phase 1 workflow components.

## Test Files to Create in `tests/Homespun.Tests/`
- `Features/Workflows/WorkflowStorageServiceTests.cs`
- `Features/Workflows/WorkflowExecutionServiceTests.cs`
- `Features/Workflows/WorkflowContextStoreTests.cs`

## Test Coverage
1. **Storage Service Tests**
   - Create/Read/Update/Delete workflows
   - List workflows by project
   - Handle missing workflow gracefully
   - Concurrent access safety

2. **Execution Service Tests**
   - Start execution from workflow definition
   - Execute nodes in order (linear)
   - Handle node success/failure
   - Pause/resume execution
   - Cancel execution

3. **Context Store Tests**
   - Set/get context values
   - Merge node outputs
   - Persist to disk
   - Load from disk on restart

## Framework
Use NUnit + Moq following existing test patterns in `tests/Homespun.Tests/`

title: Add PR status to PR badge
description: Add visual indicators for PR status (merge conflicts and CI checks) to the PR badge in the main task graph view. The badge should show: Git branch icon for merge conflicts (red = conflicts, green = no conflicts), Check/X/Spinner icon for CI status (green check = passing, red X = failing, spinner = running)

title: Add scroll and float to project title and toolbar
description:
The project title and toolbar control on the project issues tab should start in it's current position at the top of the  page but float down when the page is scrolled such that it is always at the top of the screen and visible.

On mobile screens when the toolbar is too small to fit all of the controls on one row it should maintain one row and scroll horizontally.

title: Type change menu doesn't appear when badge is clicked
description: The type change menu (where the issue type can be changed after an issue's type badge is clicked) doesn't open when the type badge is clicked. Add comprehensive test coverage for the type change menu functionality in TaskGraphView component to show that it doesn't appear, then try to fix this.

title: Migrate template tools from PyRevit to Taipan
description: There are some tools used to manage standard details models in Revit in the repository at https://github.com/Northrop-Digital/nr-template. These have been written as PyRevit scripts. Clone them to a temporary folder that is ignored in the .gitignore (e.g. /.tmp) and migrate them into Revit commands. Use the existing commands in the folder as a guide.

title: Fix session chat input
description:
Sending session messages doesn't work in React client but works in Blazor client.

Number of session modes incorrect, there should be 2 (build and plan) that are converted to the Claude modes by the server.

title: Assign current user when issue that is edited has no user assigned
description: When an issue is edited and there is no user assigned, assign the user that is editing the issue to the issue that is being edited.

title: OpenAPI Client Generation
description:
Set up OpenAPI-based type generation using hey-api/openapi-ts to create a fully typed API client.

**Tasks:**
1. Verify ASP.NET backend exports OpenAPI JSON at /swagger/v1/swagger.json
2. Install @hey-api/openapi-ts as a dev dependency
3. Create openapi-ts.config.ts configuration file
4. Add `generate:api` npm script to fetch OpenAPI spec and generate client
5. Generate TypeScript types for all API endpoints
6. Create base API client with:
   - Fetch or Axios wrapper
   - Error handling interceptors
   - Authentication header injection (for future use)
7. Verify all 19 API services have generated types

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Running `npm run generate:api` generates TypeScript types in src/api/generated/
- All API endpoints have typed request/response interfaces
- API client includes proper error handling
- Types match the C# DTOs in Homespun.Shared

title: Allow agents to be resumed
description:
I would like to be able to resume agent sessions. Use the Claude Agent SDK to find all previous sessions related to worktree folders when an issue or PR is selected. Then, show these sessions in a list. When clicked this should open a new session window with the previous chat in it and allow the user to continue the session.

Use a TDD approach.

title: Fix colours for tool use and settings page
description:
The outline colour for tool use in the chat sessions doesn't show up in dark mode because it is the same colour as the background. Make it lighter so it provides a distinct border around the tool use chat windows. The same applies for most text input and combo boxes, and the borders around tool windows. Ensure Tailwind and reusable components are being used throughout to ensure UI consistency and DRY principles.

Some backgrounds on the settings page are also white in dark mode, please fix this. A previous similar fix was required on the branches page - this is now being shown correctly.

title: Add agent run buttons to edit page
description: On the edit page after editing the description, in addition to saving changes I would like to also be able to save and immediately run an agent on the issue. Please add an agent run control. Ensure that all changes are saved to the issues before the agent is run.

title: Add user assignment to issues
description:
There is an assigned user field in Fleece issues. I would like to be able to add a user email address to the application settings that is required (i.e. all routes redirect to the settings page if there is not a user set up). This setting should be persisted in the homespun-data.json file.

When it is set, all new issues that are created in the homespun application should have the assigned user set to the user's email. Changes should have the assigned user also set using that email address.

title: Add logging for status changes in worker
description:
There are ongoing issues where the status of the worker container doesn't represent what it is actually doing (e.g. saying it is waiting while it is still streaming back input, saying it has a plan pending after the plan has been executed, etc.). The status should be persisted on the worker and changed when certain messages are sent back to the server or received (e.g. when any agent message or tool invocation is sent, the agent is clearly "working").

Add logging to the worker that indicates whenever a status is changed. It should say "Status changed from 'working' to 'waiting'" or something similar.

title: Add toolbar to top of page and move edit and run controls
description:
Add a toolbar to the top of the project page, just below the tabs. The project title and toolbar should be sticky when scrolled down, but the tabs can be scrolled off screen.

This toolbar should have small icon buttons on it for the main actions. Hovering over the buttons should show a tooltip with an explanation and keyboard shortcuts.

For now, just move the inline edit and run controls to the toolbar. These should still be displayed inline with the issue on desktop but should be hidden on mobile to leave more room for the issue title.

title: Float menu bar at top of page over contents
description: The menu bar should float at the top of the page over the page contents, and remain in place as the user scrolls.

title: Remove project information panel
description: The project information panel is not very useful. Migrate the issue sync button elsewhere and just remove the panel.

title: Inline Issue Details
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Implement inline detail rows for viewing issue/PR details within the task graph.

**NOTE: This replaces the original 'side panel' concept. The Blazor implementation uses inline expandable rows, not a separate panel.**

**Primary Component: InlineIssueDetailRow**
Expands below the issue row when selected and triggered by Space key or double-click.

**Tasks:**
1. Create InlineIssueDetailRow component showing:
   - Issue ID, Type badge, Status badge, Priority badge
   - Branch name with copy button
   - Parent issue links (clickable navigation)
   - Description (markdown rendered)
   - Linked PR status badges
   - Active session info (if agent running)
   - Session cache history
2. Create InlinePrDetailRow for merged/closed PRs showing:
   - PR number, title, state badge
   - Description (markdown rendered)
   - Commits list
   - Related issues
3. Expand/collapse triggers:
   - Space key when row is selected
   - Double-click on row
4. State management for expanded rows
5. Smooth expand/collapse animation
6. Close on Escape or clicking elsewhere

**IssueRowActions (hover actions):**
- Edit button (navigate to full edit page)
- Open Panel button (for mobile full-width view)
- Run Agent button with dropdown launcher

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Space/double-click expands inline details below selected row
- All issue metadata displayed clearly
- Markdown description renders correctly
- PR status badges show checks/approval state
- Actions (Edit, Run Agent) work correctly
- Escape or click elsewhere closes expansion

title: Private repositories aren't being added
description: Private repositories that the user are authenticated to access aren't being added in. Check whether this is a PAT issue or whether Octokit is not receiving the GitHub authentication token at all.

title: Fix graph display of merged PRs
description:
Merged PRs are displayed in order of newest at top and oldest at the bottom in the projects page of the UI. They should instead be shown with the oldest at the top and the newest at the bottom, so that the issues appear like they are being "pulled" up into the PRs. There should also be a vertical line coming down from the PRs and connecting to the left side of the "next" nodes that are in the left-most lane of the graph. Push all issue nodes over +1 lane to the right to allow the left-most lane to hold the edges between the main PR and the nodes.

The merged PR nodes also only have a line extending from the bottom of the nodes. They should have lines extending out of the top and bottom as they are always connecting to another merged PR (except for the very oldest one at the top after all PRs are expanded.

title: Fix order of worker Dockerfile to install dotnet before building app
description: The worker container Dockerfile appears to build the server app before installing Dotnet, which doesn't allow caching of the Dotnet app. Review and optimise the worker container Dockerflie.

title: Adding a new issue at the bottom of the issues shows two inputs
description: When I select the very last issue in the graph and create a new issue below using 'o', two inline issue editors appear and one is focused. Create tests to replicate this and then create a plan to fix it.

title: Move from in-memory store to Postgres database
Check that stopping sessions kills container
Update React client logging to Promtail
description: The server container logs to a PLG stack, which is set up using Docker Compose. The Homespun.Web React client should also log to the PLG stack using OpenTelemetry. This should be tagged with Component="Client" such that the client logs can be separated.

title: Dotnet is not installed on the worker containers
description: Ensure that the dotnet CLI is installed on the worker containers, including .NET SDKs 8, 9 and 10. This used to be done by having the image based on the dotnet SDK base image (similar to the main container image), but now we are working off the node image due to the Typescript SDK.

title: Pull session mode and model from agent container
description:
Investigate the way that session statuses are pulled from the agent worker container. They currently appear to be storing this when the agent is first run, resulting in the "mode" being either Plan or Build and staying like that regardless of the prompts that come afterwards (which can change the permissions mode).

The model also always just shows the same model that the agent was launched with.

Finally, the status often stays as "Planning" or "Waiting" after plans are sent for execution. The agent container should be aware that the agent is working and sending back responses, so should be able to store and send back status updates (along with the current model and permissions mode) through a "/status" endpoint.

title: Fix log level parsing for server logs
description: In Grafana the client and worker logs show the log level clearly (e.g. Info, Error, etc.). The server logs, however, do not parse this correctly and they log level is not shown even though it is being captured in the Parsed Field as LogLevel. The lines in the All Logs are also difficult to read - they should be colour coded to show which are coming from the server and client, and have the Message text more visible. Please try to unify the logging output between the client, server and workers as they all look quite different.

title: PR status is always 'Checks running'
description: When looking at any PR status in the client the status is always 'checks running'. The PullRequestWorkflowService was not populating ChecksPassing, IsApproved, ApprovalCount, and ChangesRequestedCount on PullRequestInfo objects.

title: Workflow Foundation (MVP)
description:
Foundation layer for workflow system. Creates the basic storage, execution, and MCP tool infrastructure.

## Deliverables
1. Workflow JSONL storage service (`.workflows/` directory)
2. Basic linear workflow execution engine
3. WorkflowComplete MCP tool in worker
4. Workflow context store for accumulated state
5. Basic REST API endpoints for workflow CRUD
6. Unit tests for storage and execution

title: Test mock container
Worktree management page
description:
I would like to add a branch/worktree page for each project at the route /projects/id/branches. This should list all of the currently started worktrees in the project, allowing me to start and stop agents in the worktree, pull recent changes and delete worktrees and branches (both local and remote). It should also show which worktrees have PRs associated with them, and the status of the worktrees. Only branches and worktrees that have been merged should be allowed to be deleted without warning. Otherwise, a warning modal should be shown.

It should also show all remote branches that have not been pulled. I should be able to create new local worktrees from these branches.

This page should behave like a tab in the projects page, allowing me to switch between the issues view and the worktrees view with links at the top of the page, similar to how GitHub works.

Create a new PR when complete.

title: Add CTRL+C shortcut to stop sessions and go back to session page
description:
When in a session page, ctrl+c should stop the agent and go back to the session list page.

Clicking the stop button should also take the user back to the session list page.

title: Show dotted extension line only for hidden issues
description: When the limited task graph depth was introduced in PR #352, dashed lines were added below all issues that had parent issues hidden by the depth limit. However, this ended up being added for all issues that had any ancestor hidden and not just the direct children of the hidden issues. Fix this.

title: AgentControlPanel doesn't refresh session status via SignalR
description:
The AgentControlPanel component displays a session status badge but does not subscribe to SignalR events. If a session stops on the server, the badge won't update unless the user clicks another control.

Issues:
- Does NOT implement IAsyncDisposable
- Does NOT have SignalR subscription
- Session status badge shows stale data

This is the same bug pattern that was fixed in IssueDetailPanel (PR #222).

Fix: Add SignalR subscription following the IssueDetailPanel.razor pattern.

title: Check changes to issue details before showing "discard changes" dialog
description: When exiting the issue edit page the a modal dialog pops up asking me whether I want to discard changes. This is regardless of whether there actually are any changes - check whether any differences exist before asking this question.

title: Fix Beads setup for new repositories
description: When a new repository is cloned in, the bd init command seems to cause a failure on windows due to LF characters being added to the .beads/ files.

title: Fix worker container permissions
Don't hide session mode and model selectors on mobile
description:
Currently the mode and model text selectors in the session chat don't show the selected text on mobile screens. Update this such as follows:

- They should always show the selected value text on all screens
- Change the mode select to be a toggle button as there are only two options
- Make Shift+Tab the shortcut for toggling the mode.
- Move the controls to above the chat text input.

title: Move all Homespun related items to tags in the Fleece issuesa
Fix container restarts from Playwright
description: Occasionally when a mock container is started with ./scripts/mock.sh or ./scripts/mock-live.sh this will fail and eventually the agent will attempt to restart the docker container that the current application is running in, terminating all progress. Could you please check whether it is possible for an agent to stop the container it is running in (typically the `homespun` container) through the Docker socket, and come up with some guardrails that could prevent this from happening (both deterministic and prompt based).

title: Add issue specific issues agent button and prompt
description:
There is currently a prompt named "IssueModify" in the prompt list, and an "issues agent" button in the toolbar.

Do the following:
- The "IssueModify" prompt should be moved to a separate category with the heading "Issue agent prompts", where there is a fixed single prompt used when running the issue modification agent on an issue, and a fixed system prompt that is used for all issue agents. These can be modified - they are fixed in the sense that there cannot be more than one prompt for each.
- Change the behaviour of the issues agent button in the toolbar to be specific for the selected issue. The modal that pops up should have a text box that allows for instructions to be added in before running the agent (e.g. rename this issue), and should show which issue is selected below this.
