When the Start Agent button is clicked, this should then start an issue agent, use the IssueModify prompt and replace the placeholders with details of the selected issue and the instructions, and send the prompt to the agent similar to running other agents. If no instructions are provided, just open the session page with no prompt, but include the system prompt.

title: Feature Parity Verification
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Verify all Blazor features are implemented in the React frontend.

**Tasks:**
1. Create comprehensive checklist of all Blazor features:
   - Pages (10+)
   - Components (50+)
   - API integrations (19 services)
   - SignalR functionality (2 hubs)
   - Keyboard shortcuts
   - Mobile responsiveness
2. Manually test each feature against checklist
3. Compare UI/UX between Blazor and React implementations
4. Document any intentional differences
5. Fix any missing functionality discovered
6. Verify real-time features work identically
7. Test edge cases and error scenarios
8. Get stakeholder sign-off on parity

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- All Blazor features verified in React
- Differences documented and approved
- No critical regressions
- Stakeholder approval obtained
- Checklist completed and archived

If features are missing, create new Fleece issues under the overall porting issue for creating the remaining features, including a plan to complete them.

title: Install Chromium on worker containers to enable Playwright MCP tools
description: Chromium isn't installed on the worker containers so the Playwright MCP tools cannot be used. Update the Dockerfile to install Chromium in the container image.

title: Add parallel execution to WorkflowExecutionService
Show timelines from different worktrees
description: The app currently only shows the issues and timeline from the main branch. Instead of this, it should be able to show the issues and timelines from all branches - as Fleece is used for storing issues and these issues as JSONL in the repository itself, it is possible for different branches to contain different issues. Show a branch symbol next to issues that have associated branches created in the repository, and when clicked the page should change the timeline to show the issues as they are stored in that branch. This should be a new route in the repository. There should be a button at the top of the timeline to then return back to the default branch.

title: Port frontend to React+Vite+Tailwind+TanStack Router+TanStack Query+Zustand
description:
The current frontend client application is written using Blazor with the Blazor Blueprint front end library. I would like to instead create a new front end using a Typescript stack, as I am having some performance issues with Blazor.

This port will be quite significant, so I will need to implement it in a number of stages.

**Target Stack:**
- Language: TypeScript
- Frontend Framework: React
- Build Tool: Vite
- UI Components: shadcn/ui
- Styling: Tailwind CSS
- Routing: TanStack Router
- Data Fetching: TanStack Query
- Client State: Zustand
- API Client: OpenAPI-TS (hey-api/openapi-ts)

**Location:** src/Homespun.Web
**Hosting:** Standalone Vite dev server with API proxy to ASP.NET backend
**Migration Strategy:** Coexist with Blazor during development

See .claude/plans/react-frontend-port-plan.md for detailed technical plan.

title: Add E2E tests for Clones tab
Implement ClonesTab component with sections and Delete All
Persist permissions per-session and allow them to change mid-session
description:
The permissions associated with a session (i.e. the "session mode" which can be either "Build" or "Plan") is a property of the agent/worker container, and can actually be changed mid-prompt (i.e. is not necessarily sent with every prompt). Currently these seem to be persisted at the client level, and when changed in one session page changes when another session page is navigated to.

Instead of this, I would like the session mode to be pulled as part of the session information. When this session mode is changed, I would like this to be passed down to the server and then to the worker container immediately, without requiring a new prompt to be sent. There should be server and worker endpoints available to perform this function.

title: Fix status issues
description: Use the logs skill to review the recent worker container logs from Loki. I am still having some difficulty with the agent status not being updated as expected (Awaiting plan execution status after the plan has been executed, awaiting input status while the agent is still responding, etc.). Please try to identify these issue and work out how to fix them.

title: Add agents for modifying issues
description:
For the Homespun.Web React client, I would like to add an agent button on the project page (next to the push button). This should run an agent that can modify the fleece issues based on instructions from the user.

It should do the following:

- Open a small popup with a prompt input and run button.
- Persist any changes that have been made in the Fleece issues to the main branch without committing them.
- Create a new clone with branch name `hsp/agent+{shortened timestamp}` from main that includes the unstaged fleece issue changes in the main repository folder.
- Start a new agent with system instructions that tell the agent not to write any files in the repository and only to make modifications using the Fleece CLI tool and the user's instruction. If this was run from the toolbar with a selected issue, that issue's ID should be included in the system prompt. This should open a session much like any other agent.
- In a session window that has been created using this method, there should be a tab that shows the issue diff (which should be available in the Fleece.Core library) and two buttons that say "Accept changes" and "Cancel".
- If "Accept changes" is clicked, the issues should be merged with the issues in the main branch and be persisted again (without commit), then the session should be stopped and the browser redirected to the projects page.
- If "Cancel" is clicked the session should be stopped and the changes discarded.

title: Fix dashboards
Set type and status button width to 56px
description:
The type and status button widths in the issues graph are currently variable in width. Set them to exactly 56px in width such that they always fit the text within them and always remain the same width for alignment purposes.

Also add an outline for the execution mode button and set the height to match the type and status buttons.

title: Phase 5: Branches & Pull Requests
description:
## Verification Checklist

This phase implements branch/worktree management and pull request integration in the **Homespun.Web** project (`src/Homespun.Web`).

**Child Issues:**
- [x] **2DmkMw** - Branches Tab - Manage git worktrees
- [x] **oFPOQ2** - Pull Requests Display - Show PR status and integration with issues

**Agent Verification Instructions:**
When all child issues are complete, verify the following in `src/Homespun.Web`:
1. ✅ Confirm the Branches tab displays local worktrees with status (clean/dirty/conflicts) - Verified: shows ahead/behind/up-to-date badges
2. ✅ Verify remote branches show separately from local worktrees - Component implemented, filters correctly
3. ✅ Confirm pull/delete worktree actions work via API - Pull returns 204, delete has confirmation dialog
4. ✅ Verify Pull Requests display shows open and merged PRs with correct status badges - Component has PrStatusBadge, CiStatusBadge, ReviewStatusBadge
5. ✅ Check that PR-issue linkage is visible in the UI - Link2 icon and View Linked Issue button implemented
6. ⚠️ Confirm agents can be started on PR branches - Button present, handler is TODO (agent orchestration separate feature)
7. ✅ Verify GitHub links open correctly - ExternalLink icons with target="_blank"

All UI components verified complete. Start agent functionality has UI but handler is TODO (agent orchestration is a separate feature).

title: Issue Edit Page
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Create the full issue edit page for detailed editing of all issue fields.

**Route:** /projects/$projectId/issues/$issueId/edit

This is accessed via:
- Enter key when an issue is selected
- Edit button in IssueRowActions
- Shift+Enter after creating a new issue

**Tasks:**
1. Create edit page route at /projects/$projectId/issues/$issueId/edit
2. Build full form with all fields:
   - Title (text input)
   - Description (Markdown editor with live preview)
   - Status (dropdown: Open, InProgress, Review, Complete, Closed, Archived)
   - Type (dropdown: Task, Feature, Bug, Chore)
   - Priority (1-5 selector)
   - Execution mode (Series/Parallel toggle)
   - Working branch ID (text input with validation)
   - Parent issues (multi-select with search)
   - Tags (tag input)
3. Fetch issue data with useIssue hook
4. Implement save action (PUT /api/issues/{issueId})
5. Add cancel action returning to previous page
6. Add unsaved changes warning on navigation
7. Validate required fields (title)
8. Handle loading and error states
9. Keyboard shortcut: Cmd/Ctrl+S to save

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- All issue fields are editable
- Changes save correctly via API
- Cancel returns without saving
- Unsaved changes prompt on navigation away
- Markdown preview works in real-time
- Accessible from task graph via Enter or Edit button

title: Playwright doesn't render CSS
description:
When the Playwright MCP tools are run on a site when it is built with dotnet run in a container it doesn't render the CSS properly, likely due to either the Tailwind dependencies not running or some Blazor issues. Could you please run the app, and take a screenshot with Blazor then review the browser and app logs to diagnose and fix this issue?

In previous attempts you appear to have killed the Homespun process, dotnet process or the container that you are running in. Do not kill any containers that you are running on under any circumstances - instead, run a container with a new name using the ./scripts/run.sh command if you are testing something.

title: Distinguish interrupted sessions from stopped sessions
description:
Stopping a session with the button or escape key completely stops the session and agent (similar to clicking the stop session button at the top right). Instead of doing this, please update it such that the agent is interrupted instead, and the next message just continues with the previous conversation. Use the word "interrupt" and "resume" to describe this particular behaviour and to differentiate it from "stop". The same "waiting" status can be used for this state.

Ensure that this works for both the local and docker agent execution modes.

title: Add EnrichedCloneInfo model and CloneEnrichmentService
Add Clones tab to project page with cleanup automation
description:
## Overview

Add a new "Clones" tab to the project page that displays all clones found in the `.clones` folder. The tab will show clone metadata, link to related PRs and issues, and provide deletion capabilities for stale clones.

## Key Features

### 1. Clones Tab Display
- Add "Clones" tab to project page navigation (between Branches and Prompts tabs)
- Show two separate lists:
  - **Feature Clones**: Clones linked to issues/PRs (branch name contains issue ID via `+{issueId}`)
  - **Issues Agent Clones**: Clones with branch names starting with `issues-agent-`

### 2. Clone Information Display
Each clone card should display:
- Clone folder name and branch name
- Current commit SHA with relative time
- Linked Issue (if extractable from branch name via `BranchNameParser.ExtractIssueId()`)
  - Issue title and status from Fleece
- Linked PR (if branch matches a cached PR via `IGraphCacheService`)
  - PR number, title, status (Open/Merged/Closed)
- Clone status indicators:
  - On correct branch vs detached/wrong branch
  - Has active session indicator

### 3. Delete Button Logic
Show a delete button on clones that meet ANY of these criteria:

**PR-based deletion criteria:**
- Linked PR has status `Merged`
- Linked PR has status `Closed`

**Issue-based deletion criteria:**
- Linked issue has status `complete`
- Linked issue has status `archived`
- Linked issue has status `closed`
- Linked issue cannot be found (deleted)

### 4. Delete All Button
- Button at top of the page to "Delete All Stale Clones"
- Only visible when there are deletable clones
- Shows count of clones that will be deleted
- Requires confirmation dialog before proceeding
- Deletes all clones matching the deletion criteria

## Technical Implementation

### Backend Changes

1. **New API Endpoint: `GET /api/clones/enriched`**
   Create a new endpoint that returns enriched clone data:
   ```csharp
   public class EnrichedCloneInfo
   {
       public CloneInfo Clone { get; set; }
       public string? LinkedIssueId { get; set; }
       public FleeceIssue? LinkedIssue { get; set; }  // Include issue details
       public PullRequestInfo? LinkedPr { get; set; }  // Include PR details
       public bool IsDeletable { get; set; }  // Pre-calculated deletion eligibility
       public string? DeletionReason { get; set; }  // Why it is deletable
       public bool IsIssuesAgentClone { get; set; }  // Branch starts with "issues-agent-"
   }
   ```

2. **New API Endpoint: `DELETE /api/clones/bulk`**
   Bulk delete endpoint for "Delete All" functionality:
   - Accept list of clone paths
   - Return success/failure for each
   - Invalidate relevant caches

3. **Service: `CloneEnrichmentService`**
   New service to enrich clone data:
   - Extract issue ID from branch name using `BranchNameParser.ExtractIssueId()`
   - Look up issue details via `IFleeceService`
   - Look up PR via `IGraphCacheService.GetCachedPRData()` matching branch name
   - Calculate `IsDeletable` based on PR/issue status

### Frontend Changes

1. **Route: `/projects/:projectId/clones`**
   - Create new route file: `projects.$projectId.clones.tsx`
   - Add tab to project page navigation

2. **Component: `ClonesTab`**
   - Main container component
   - Two sections: "Feature Clones" and "Issues Agent Clones"
   - Header with "Delete All Stale" button

3. **Component: `EnrichedCloneCard`**
   - Display enriched clone information
   - Show linked issue with status badge (links to issue detail)
   - Show linked PR with status badge (links to PR URL)
   - Conditional delete button based on `isDeletable`
   - Loading state while checking PR/issue status

4. **Hooks:**
   - `useEnrichedClones(projectId)` - Fetch enriched clone data
   - `useBulkDeleteClones()` - Mutation for bulk deletion

5. **UI Patterns:**
   - Follow existing BranchCard styling patterns
   - Use existing badge components for status
   - Use existing confirmation dialog for delete actions
   - Empty state when no clones exist

## Test Plan

### Backend Tests
- Test `CloneEnrichmentService` issue ID extraction
- Test PR matching via branch name
- Test deletion eligibility calculation for each status type
- Test bulk delete endpoint

### Frontend Tests
- Test `EnrichedCloneCard` renders correctly with all data states
- Test delete button visibility based on `isDeletable`
- Test "Delete All" button shows correct count
- Test confirmation dialog flow

### E2E Tests
- Navigate to Clones tab
- Verify clones display with correct linked data
- Verify Issues Agent clones are separated
- Test single clone deletion
- Test bulk deletion

## Files to Create/Modify

### Backend
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Git/Controllers/ClonesController.cs` - Add enriched and bulk delete endpoints
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Git/CloneEnrichmentService.cs` - New service
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Git/EnrichedCloneInfo.cs` - New model
- `src/Homespun.Shared/Requests/CloneRequests.cs` - Add bulk delete request

### Frontend
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/projects.$projectId.clones.tsx` - New route
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/routes/projects.$projectId.tsx` - Add tab navigation
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/clones/` - New feature folder
  - `components/clones-tab.tsx`
  - `components/enriched-clone-card.tsx`
  - `hooks/use-enriched-clones.ts`
  - `hooks/use-bulk-delete-clones.ts`
  - `index.ts`

### Tests
- `tests/Homespun.Tests/Features/Git/CloneEnrichmentServiceTests.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/clones/components/clones-tab.test.tsx`
- `src/Homespun.Web/src/features/clones/components/enriched-clone-card.test.tsx`
- `src/Homespun.Web/e2e/clones-tab.spec.ts`

title: Limit number of levels shown in task graph
description:
When rendering the task graph, only show the tasks up to a depth of three. If there are additional parent tasks, show a little faded continuation indicator on the bottom or side of the node (depending on whether it is leading to a series or parallel parent).

Add buttons and [ ] keyboard shortcuts to increase and decrease the number of levels shown on the graph.

title: Add session information to the session page
description: On the session page, there is a tab in the Homespun.Client Blazor application that is not present in the Homespun.Web React application. Review the implementation in Blazor and port it over to the React application. It should show relevant information about the issue that the session is related to, the PR (if one has been linked to the issue), the to-dos and the relevant plans.

title: Fix chat display
description:
The Homespun.Web chat display has the following problems:

- The tool result messages aren't joined to the tool call messages and end up on the user side rather than the agent side.
- There is no button to go to the bottom of the chat window. I believe this is a feature of the PromptKit UI component but if not implement it.
- The messages can exceed the screen width and have a horizontal scroll. They should be constrained to the screen width.
- Headings do not render properly in the user messages (they show up the same size) or in the agent messages (no markdown is rendered at all). All messages should be rendered assuming they are markdown and styled appropriately.

title: Add enriched clones and bulk delete API endpoints
Change default git email to homespun-bot@homespun.com
description: Change the default email address for git to homespun-bot@localhost.com to avoid sign in issues with Claude Code UI - it validates email addresses for sign ins.

title: Review fleece prime hook
description: There should be a hook that runs `fleece prime` and injects it into the system prompt of the agent whenever a new agent is started. This provides the agent with basic instructions on how to use the fleece CLI tool. Review whether this is still implemented by checking that it is being injected into your prompt, then look at the codebase to ensure that this happens for both the normal agent invocation and the issues agent invocation.

title: Complete UI update
description:
There were recently some updates to main from PR #88. This contains some unfinished work as described in the PR description and a markdown file in .plans/

Please complete this work.

title: Set up workflows feature folder and React Flow
description:
Create the frontend feature folder structure and integrate React Flow.

## Dependencies to Add
\`\`\`bash
npm install reactflow
\`\`\`

## Files to Create
\`\`\`
src/Homespun.Web/src/features/workflows/
├── components/
│   ├── workflow-editor/
│   │   ├── workflow-canvas.tsx
│   │   ├── workflow-toolbar.tsx
│   │   └── index.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── hooks/
│   ├── use-workflow.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── stores/
│   └── workflow-editor-store.ts
├── types/
│   └── workflow.ts
└── index.ts
\`\`\`

## Initial WorkflowCanvas Implementation
- Set up React Flow with background and controls
- Configure node types (will be implemented separately)
- Set up basic canvas interactions (pan, zoom)

title: Ensure issues are synchronised
description:
When issues are edited in the Homespun.Web application in the edit page, changing the status and type doesn't update the status and type of the issue. Review all items other than the description and title to ensure that they are being persisted.

Also add no priority as an option and default to it.

title: Starting another agent mid-start causes exception
Search issues
description:
Create a simple text search feature that highlights and selects issues similar to Vim.

The '/' key should open the search bar and focus on it. This should be at the top of the page. When text is typed into this bar, the relevant issues should be highlighted with a border and the text that matches should be highlighted with a yellow highlight.

Hitting enter should then "embed" the search term into the viewer and hide the search bar. The first match should be selected. Typing 'n' or 'SHIFT + n' should then scroll forwards and backwards through the search results. Escape should remove the search term for the viewer and reset to the normal state. When in the search state, all other keyboard controls should act as normal - the user should still be able to use the j/k controls to go up and down the un-matched issues.

title: Get branch name from linked PR if one exists
description: If there is a linked PR that contains an issue ID for an issue, always use the branch name in the PR to find the clone and launch the agent in the appropriate location. If there is no PR, also check the folders in the /.clones folder to see if there are any folders with the same issue ID as the issue. Occasionally if the branch name has not been persisted and a PR is created, creating a new session for the issue can result in a new branch being created with no reference to the already created branch/PR (which has the same issue ID but different branch ID and therefore different branch name).

title: Fix flaky E2E tests
description: Fix all Typescript linting and formatting issues in this PR

title: Save and run agent doesn't run agent
description:
On the edit issues page, there should be prompt, model and branch selection controls above the save and run button. This should then launch the agent in the same way that the run button in the toolbar launches the agent.

Currently, clicking on the button doesn't do anything, it just seemingly saves the changes.

title: Get worker container status from last message
description: The container statuses are quite flaky. Review the way that statuses are held in the worker containers. They should be determined by the last message - i.e. if the last message is pending plan the status should be "awaiting plan", when the last message is "success" the status should be "awaiting input" and so on. Review the message types in the Claude Agent SDK documentation and put together a plan to fix the status endpoint on the container.

title: Remove legacy pre-A2A and AGUI messaging
description: The application was migrated from using a proprietary messaging format between the client, server and worker containers to using A2A and AG-UI. Remove all legacy code relating to this messaging as it is no longer required for backwards compatibility.

title: Create Homespun.Shared project with shared DTOs and contracts
description:
Create a new class library project `Homespun.Shared` that will contain all types shared between the Blazor WASM client and the ASP.NET server. This is the foundation that both projects will reference.

## What to create

### Project setup
- Create `src/Homespun.Shared/Homespun.Shared.csproj` targeting `net10.0` as a class library
- Add to `Homespun.sln` under the `src` solution folder
- Add NuGet reference to `Fleece.Core` (1.0.0) since Issue types from this library are used in DTOs

### Models/DTOs to move into Homespun.Shared

**From Features/ClaudeCode/Data/ (session models):**
- `ClaudeSession` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Sessions.ClaudeSession`
- `ClaudeMessage` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Sessions.ClaudeMessage`
- `ClaudeMessageContent` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Sessions.ClaudeMessageContent`
- `ClaudeSessionStatus` enum
- `SessionMode` enum
- `ClaudeContentType` enum
- `ClaudeMessageRole` enum
- `PendingQuestion`, `SessionTodoItem`, `TodoStatus`
- `ToolResultData` and all subtypes (ReadToolData, WriteToolData, BashToolData, etc.)
- `SessionSummary` (returned by API)
- `SessionCacheSummary`, `ResumableSession`, `RunningSessionInfo`

**From Features/PullRequests/Data/Entities/ (domain entities used as DTOs):**
- `Project` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Projects.Project`
- `PullRequest` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.PullRequests.PullRequest`
- `PullRequestInfo` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.PullRequests.PullRequestInfo`
- `OpenPullRequestStatus`, `PullRequestStatus` enums
- `PullRequestWithStatus`, `PullRequestWithTime` records

**From Features/Git/ (git models):**
- `WorktreeInfo` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Git.WorktreeInfo`
- `WorktreeStatus` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Git.WorktreeStatus`
- `BranchInfo` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Git.BranchInfo`
- `FileChangeInfo`

**From Features/Gitgraph/ (graph visualization models):**
- `IGraphNode` interface and all implementations (IssueNode, PullRequestNode, SectionDividerNode)
- `Graph`, `GraphBranch`, `GraphNodeType`, `GraphNodeStatus` enums
- `TimelineLaneLayout`

**From Features/Notifications/:**
- `NotificationDto` → `Homespun.Shared.Models.Notifications.NotificationDto`
- `NotificationType` enum

**From Features/GitHub/:**
- `IssuePullRequestStatus`
- `GitHubAuthStatus`, `GitHubAuthMethod` enum

**API Request/Response DTOs (from controllers):**
- `CreateSessionRequest`, `SendMessageRequest`
- `CreateProjectRequest`, `UpdateProjectRequest`, `CreateProjectResult`
- `CreateIssueRequest`, `UpdateIssueRequest`
- `CreatePullRequestRequest`, `UpdatePullRequestRequest`
- `CreateWorktreeRequest`, `CreateWorktreeResponse`, `WorktreeExistsResponse`
- `CreateNotificationRequest`
- `GenerateBranchIdRequest`, `GenerateBranchIdResponse`
- `SyncResult`

**From Features/Fleece/:**
- `FleeceIssueSyncResult`, `PullResult`, `BranchStatusResult`

### SignalR hub contracts
- Create `Homespun.Shared.Hubs.IClaudeCodeHubClient` interface defining all server-to-client SignalR messages (BroadcastSessionStarted, BroadcastMessageReceived, BroadcastStreamingContentDelta, etc.)
- Create `Homespun.Shared.Hubs.IClaudeCodeHub` interface defining all client-to-server SignalR messages (JoinSession, SendMessage, StopSession, etc.)
- Create `Homespun.Shared.Hubs.HubConstants` class with hub URL constants ("/hubs/notifications", "/hubs/claudecode")

### Constants
- API route constants (e.g., `ApiRoutes.Sessions`, `ApiRoutes.Projects`, etc.)

## Key principles
- Only move types that need to be referenced by BOTH client and server
- Types that are internal to the server (e.g., service implementations, EF Core entities if any) stay in the server project
- Keep the `Notification` type (with `Action` callback) server-side; only `NotificationDto` goes to Shared
- The `Homespun` project should still compile after this - update its references to point to the Shared project via a ProjectReference
- Update all `using` statements in the monolith to reference the new namespaces

## Verification
- Solution builds with no errors
- All existing tests pass
- No functionality changes - this is purely a code organization refactor

title: Add Komodo install script
Status doesn't update while plan is executing
description:
When I commence work on a plan by clicking on the "Clear Context & Start Implementation", the status often remains as "Plan ready" in all of the various status indicators. Please review whether it is the front end, server or worker container that is causing this to happen. The container should generally be polled directly, and is apparently regularly polled by the front end to ensure that the containers are up to date, so it is most likely an issue with the agent container or server passing messages through.

The session window also often has the buttons for executing the plan remain in place while the agent is working. Once one of these buttons is clicked this should trigger the plan mode to be removed **and** the status to be updated. Review in detail.

title: Fix working status rings and prevent multiple concurrent agents
description: The issues graph used to contain rings around each of the nodes that had an agent working on them, with the colour of the ring representing the status. This is no longer shown, likely due to the changes in PR #526. Please review and fix the issue.

title: Show current branch commit in agent session panel and on branches page
Fix: Session doesn't load when card is clicked
description: When clicking a session card on /sessions, the page redirects to /sessions/$sessionId but chat messages and issue title don't load. They only load on page refresh.

title: Match the status in the session window with the global indicator
description:
The agent status badge that shows up at the top of the session page (just below the title) doesn't always match the status that is shown on the session list page, and the status in the agent indicator at the top of the page.

The badge in the session page is usually correct (i.e. shows working while the agent is working, etc.), while the global indicator is often incorrect. Review the difference between these do and rectify this issue.

title: Reduce chat text size and fix markdown headings on mobile
description:
The session chat in the React client has text that is too large on mobile screens, and markdown headings are not rendering with proper heading styles. This is part of fixing the chat display issues.

Problems to fix:
1. Missing Typography Plugin: The project uses prose classes (prose-sm, prose-invert) but lacks the @tailwindcss/typography plugin, causing headings to render with browser defaults
2. Fixed Chat Bubble Width: Chat bubbles use a fixed max-w-[80%] without responsive adjustments  
3. Inconsistent Prose Classes: Some components use prose prose-sm while others use only prose-sm (missing base class)
4. No Responsive Text Sizing: All screens use the same prose size regardless of viewport

Solution:
- Install @tailwindcss/typography plugin
- Implement responsive prose classes (prose-sm for mobile, prose for desktop)
- Update chat bubble widths (90% mobile, 80% desktop)
- Fix prose class consistency

title: Show user badge
description: When there is a user assigned to an issue, show a badge with the user's name next to the issue in the graph view. Only show the section that is before the first @ if there is one in the value (i.e. show "user" for "user@email.com").

title: Back to sessions button when session isn't found leads to "agents" page
description: When the session can't be found, a button saying "back to agents" is shown. Clicking this links to the agent prompts page rather than the sessions page. Fix this.

title: Add logging to diagnose Claude failures
Workdir not mounted correctly and .Claude doesn't contain plans
description:
When a new agent container is started in Docker agent execution mode, incorrect folders are mounted to the new container by the Homespun application. Instead of mounting `/data/src/{project}/.clones/{id}/workdir` to `/workdir` and `/data/src/{project}/.clones/{id}/.claude` to `/home/homespun/.claude`, the entire `/data/src/{project}/.clones/{id}` folder is mounted to `/workdir`, resulting in the repository being located at `/workdir/workdir/` and the `/home/.claude/` folder just being in the container's local storage rather than the persisted storage of the parent machine. I believe this error was introduced in PRs #250 or #253.

See logs below of commands used to inspect the worker container's structure.

azureuser@vm-devvm-n0cb75:~/repos/Homespun$ docker exec homespun-issue-a4b29e8d-e1a4-4b6c-ae79-2990a21ce363-WJpWmJ ls -la /workdir

drwxr-xr-x 12 homespun homespun 4096 Feb 17 00:40 workdir

title: Change default prompt to "plan"
description: Change the default agent prompt selection from 'Build' to 'Plan' in AgentSelector.razor, IssueEdit.razor, and AgentPrompts.razor modal

title: Test issue 2
Implement TransformNodeExecutor
description:
Create the transform node executor for data manipulation without agents.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/NodeExecutors/TransformNodeExecutor.cs`

## Supported Operations
1. **parse**
   - Parse JSON/YAML from string
   - Regex extraction with capture groups
   - Output: parsed data structure

2. **filter**
   - JavaScript expression evaluation
   - Filter arrays/objects from context
   - Output: filtered data

3. **aggregate**
   - Collect outputs from multiple nodes
   - Merge objects
   - Concatenate arrays
   - Output: aggregated result

4. **map**
   - Rename keys
   - Transform values with JavaScript
   - Output: mapped data

5. **custom**
   - Execute JavaScript function
   - Full access to context
   - Return arbitrary data

## Implementation
- Use JavaScript interpreter (Jint) for expressions
- Sandbox execution for security
- Clear error messages for invalid expressions

title: Install Typescript LSP and Claude Code plugin
description: In the worker container Dockerfile, install the Typescript LSP and Claude Code Typescript plugin. The plugin should be installed with `claude plugin install typescript-lsp@claude-plugins-official` but the Typescript LSP server may also need to be installed. Review this by installing it locally first and verifying that it works, then updating the Dockerfile.

title: Tool Result Renderers
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Create specialized components for rendering different tool outputs using PromptKit components.

**PromptKit Components to Use:**
- `Tool` - Base component for visualizing AI tool usage with states (input-streaming, input-available, output-available, output-error)
- `CodeBlock`, `CodeBlockCode`, `CodeBlockGroup` - Code display with syntax highlighting via Shiki
- `Steps`, `StepsTrigger`, `StepsContent`, `StepsItem` - Collapsible step sequences
- `Reasoning`, `ReasoningTrigger`, `ReasoningContent` - Collapsible reasoning/thinking display
- `ThinkingBar` - AI thinking state indicator with optional stop action
- `TextShimmer` - Animated text loading effect

**Install Commands:**
```bash
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/tool.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/code-block.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/steps.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/reasoning.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/thinking-bar.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/text-shimmer.json"
```

**Tasks:**
1. Install and configure PromptKit Tool, CodeBlock, Steps, Reasoning, and ThinkingBar components
2. Create base ToolResultRenderer using PromptKit Tool component:
   - Leverage built-in states: input-streaming, input-available, output-available, output-error
   - Tool component includes collapsible content, state indicators, and error display
3. Create specialized renderers extending/wrapping PromptKit Tool:
   - BashToolResult: Command display with CodeBlockCode for syntax highlighting + output
   - ReadToolResult: File path + content preview with CodeBlockCode and line numbers
   - WriteToolResult: File path + diff view using CodeBlock
   - GrepToolResult: Search results with highlighted matches using CodeBlock
   - GlobToolResult: File list as tree or flat list
   - AgentToolResult: Nested agent status using Steps component
   - WebToolResult: URL + fetched content summary
4. Create ThinkingBlock using PromptKit Reasoning component:
   - Use ReasoningTrigger for "Claude is thinking..." toggle
   - ReasoningContent for expandable internal reasoning
   - Leverage `isStreaming` prop for auto-open during streaming
5. Create ToolUseBlock using PromptKit Steps component:
   - StepsTrigger shows tool invocation
   - StepsContent shows parameters
   - Links to tool result when available
6. Use CodeBlockCode with Shiki for all syntax highlighting
7. Add ThinkingBar component for active thinking state with stop action

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Each tool type renders with appropriate formatting using PromptKit Tool
- Long outputs are collapsed by default (via Tool defaultOpen=false)
- Syntax highlighting for code blocks via Shiki
- Copy functionality works (built into Tool component)
- Thinking blocks can be expanded/collapsed using Reasoning component
- ThinkingBar displays during active AI processing

title: Investigate answer endpoint of worker
description: There is an '/answer' endpoint in the Homespun worker code. This could be a legacy item that should be removed if it is handled natively in the Claude Agent SDK - investigate this directly and remove it (and all related tests) if it is not required for answering questions.

title: Add root page
Task graph loading waits for GitHub PRs to load
description: The graph

title: Update mode, prompt and model selection in session window
description:
The controls in the session window above the input box have the following issues:

- The mode selector when clicked says: "Failed to invoke 'SetSessionMode' due to an error on the server" and fails to set the new session mode.
- The model selector doesn't show the model name in text when on mobile.
- There should be a prompt selector that shows the available global and project prompts (defaulting to "No prompt"). When one is selected, it should append the text from the prompt into the end of the chat input after filling in the placeholders.

title: Emit JSON structured logs from server, worker, and client
description:
The Promtail log pipeline expects JSON-formatted log entries (with fields like Timestamp, Level, Message, Exception, SourceContext) but the server currently emits plain-text logs (e.g. `[2026-02-20 14:01:40.271] [INF] [GitHubService] ...`). This means the JSON parsing stage in promtail-config.yml silently falls through, losing structured fields like level, category, and timestamp extraction.

Switch all log outputs to JSON format:
- **Server (ASP.NET)**: Configure Serilog/logging to use a JSON formatter (e.g. CompactJsonFormatter or RenderedCompactJsonFormatter)
- **Worker (TypeScript/Hono)**: Ensure structured JSON logging with consistent field names
- **Client telemetry**: Ensure any client-side telemetry forwarded to the server follows the same JSON schema

This will enable the existing Promtail pipeline to properly extract log levels, timestamps, source contexts, and session IDs for Grafana dashboards.

title: Create live worker container tests
description:
I want to add a series of tests for the worker containers that sends them queries and expects certain response types. Because this will use live inference, I don't intend on these tests to be run automatically in CI, but instead to be just run manually while developing the worker containers.

The tests should be as follows, with each test having a new container started up in a new temporary test folder:

Test 1 - Prompts:
- With no initial prompt and on plan mode, send the query "Say 'Hello, World'".
- Expect the response "Hello, World" (not a strict assertion, just assert that there is a response and print it).

Test 2 - Reading and writing:
- In build mode with no initial prompt, send the query "Write the text 'test' to the file 'test.txt'".
- Expect that the test.txt file is created and it contains the words "test.txt".
- Edit the text in the file to "edited", then send the query "Read the file 'test.txt' and print out the answer".
- Expect that the result is "edited"

Test 3 - Asking questions, planning and clearing context:
- In plan mode with no initial prompt, send the query "I am testing the question and planning tools of this agent, as well as the ability to clear context. Remember the number 15, but don't put this into any plans that are created. Don't read any files, just use the AskUserQuestion tool to ask 'What would you like to order?', with a multiple choice question saying 'Mains' with 'steak' or 'burger' as the options, and a multiple choice question saying 'Sides', with 'chips' and 'salad' as the options with multiple answers accepted. Following this, create a plan that just says 'This is a plan for testing the UI, just repeat what the user has ordered, which is 'Steak with chips and salad.'"
- Expect that a question is returned, then answer "steak" to the first questions and "fries" and "salad" to the second question.
- Once the plan is returned, proceed with the plan and clear the context.
- When the plan has been executed, ask the question "What number was in the original prompt" and expect that 15 will not be returned as the context was cleared.

title: Add buttons for creating new inline issues on mobile
description: On mobile we are currently relying on the issue creation bar at the bottom of the screen for creating new issues. Instead of this, add buttons to the issue row control at the top and bottom of the row that provides the ability to create issues above (what would usually be done with 'shift+o') and below (what would usually be done with 'o') the currently selected issue. This should open a new inline issue editor, which should be updated with OK (shortcut: 'Enter'), OK and edit description (shortcut: 'Shift+Enter', not currently implemented on the inline control but is implemented on the issue creation bar) and cancel (shortcut: 'Escape') on the right side of the bar.

title: Fix breadcrumbs
description:
The breadcrumb links at the top of the page often don't work and are in the wrong case. For example, the current text is

Homespun/Projects/issues/12rMan/Edit

When it should be

Homespun/Projects/Homespun/Issues/Fix breadcrumbs/Edit

Please review all breadcrumbs for the pages and put together a plan to fix this. Pull and update from the remote main first and create a new PR to suit once complete. Use TDD.

title: Create REST API for Homespun
description: A REST API should be designed and implemented to allow programmatic access to the underlying functionality of Homespun. This will should simplify agentic access for testing backend functionality on live applications. The methods that are called by the API should be the same methods that are called by the application front end. It should be documented as a Swagger API.

title: Worker A2A Integration
description:
Make the worker emit A2A-compliant events instead of custom SSE events.

## Tasks
1. Install @a2a-js/sdk in worker: `npm install @a2a-js/sdk`
2. Create A2A Event Translator (new file: src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/a2a-translator.ts)
   - Translate Claude SDK events to A2A protocol:
   - `assistant` message → A2A `Message` with `role: agent`
   - `tool_use` → A2A `Message` with structured content
   - `result` → A2A `TaskStatusUpdateEvent` with state `completed`/`failed`
   - `stream_event` → A2A streaming message parts
   - `question_pending` → A2A `TaskStatusUpdateEvent` with state `input-required`
   - `plan_pending` → A2A `TaskStatusUpdateEvent` with state `input-required`
3. Implement A2A Task Lifecycle in session-manager.ts
4. Update SSE Writer to emit A2A-formatted events

## Files to Modify
- src/Homespun.Worker/package.json
- src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/session-manager.ts
- src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/sse-writer.ts
- src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/a2a-translator.ts (new)

title: Move all Homespun related items to tags in the Fleece issuesa
Allow for arbitrary agents to be run on a worktree
description: Currently only 'build' and 'plan' agents can be run with prompts injected in. A general purpose agent should be able to be started on any worktree.

title: Inline agent run panel starts session but doesn't include prompt
description: When I start an agent using the inline run panel (the run button on the issue row) it starts the agent but doesn't inject the prompt into the session. The issue detail panel works well, investigate the differences between the inline agent run and the agent control panel in the issue detail panel. We should just have one panel UI component - make the "model" and "base branch" parts collapsed by default and just show the prompt selection dropdown and run button in the new control and use it in all locations that agents can be run from.

title: Adjust new issue UI
description:
On mobile the UI for creating a new task renders incorrectly. The type and group should be on the top row and the title expanded with the accept buttons on the far right. Group should default to starting with a lower case characters.

Also allow the user to select between some different "open" statuses, namely "idea", "spec" and "next".

title: Fix starting agents on PRs and InProgress issues
New messages not shown in session window when sent
description:
When a message is sent to the worker agent from the UI (say to answer a question or execute a plan) the following messages are not being streamed back from the server over SignalR unless the page is refreshed. Please review the set up of the session page to ensure that all chat messages are being streamed back in live from the agent worker when the session page is working.

This should have been fixed by PR #559 but the messages are still not streaming into the chat window when I open one that is running.

title: Update status indicator to show different agent statuses
description: The status indicator currently just shows a blinking dot when an agent is running. Instead of this, show a blinking dot and a number next to it per status (waiting, working, awaiting an answer, awaiting plan execution, etc.) across all projects. When this is clicked, take the user to the sessions page.

title: Project Creation
description:
Implement project creation flow.

**Tasks:**
1. Create project creation page at /projects/new
2. Build form with fields:
   - Project name (required)
   - Repository URL or local path (required)
   - Initial branch (optional, default: main)
3. Add form validation with error messages using react-hook-form or similar
4. Create useCreateProject mutation hook
5. Call POST /api/projects on submit
6. Navigate to new project on success
7. Handle creation errors with user feedback
8. Add cancel button to return to projects list

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Form validates required fields before submission
- Project is created via API
- User is redirected to new project detail page
- Errors are displayed clearly
- Cancel returns to projects list

title: Hide template tools behind feature flag
Implement basic workflow execution service
description:
Create the basic workflow execution service with linear (non-parallel) execution for MVP.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/IWorkflowExecutionService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowExecutionService.cs`

## Interface Methods
- `StartWorkflowAsync(workflowId, triggerContext)` - Start execution
- `GetExecutionAsync(executionId)` - Get execution state
- `PauseExecutionAsync(executionId)` - Pause execution
- `ResumeExecutionAsync(executionId)` - Resume execution  
- `CancelExecutionAsync(executionId)` - Cancel execution

## Implementation Details (MVP - Linear Only)
- Execute nodes in topological order (one at a time)
- Track execution state (pending, running, paused, completed, failed)
- Handle node completion/failure callbacks
- Store execution state to disk for crash recovery
- Background hosted service for long-running executions

## Note
DAG parallel execution will be added in Phase 2. This task focuses on linear execution only.

title: Fix model display for new agent selection
description: The model selection UI that is in the IssueDetailPanel above the selection of an agent shows a dot-point list of the different models below a box with the current selected model. This should instead just be a normal drop-down box. Please change it to just be a drop-down box with three options: "Opus", "Sonnet" and "Haiku". Change the default to "Opus".

title: "Starting session" doesn't refresh after session is started
description: When a new agent is started the "starting session" button goes into a "loading" state. Even though the timeline shows the session has started the page must be refreshed for the button to disappear and show the "open chat" page. Review this - once the agent has been started the state of this button and the issue properties panel should change.

title: Fix session crashes
description:
# Bug Investigation: Playwright MCP Serialization Error Causing Container Crashes

## Summary

The Playwright MCP tools are returning malformed responses that contain .NET type names instead of properly serialized JSON data. This bug appears to be causing container crashes/restarts, with processes being terminated by signals (exit codes 128, 144).

## Evidence

### 1. Malformed Playwright Responses

Instead of returning actual content, Playwright MCP tools return:
```
System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary`2[System.String,System.Object]]
```

This occurs across multiple Playwright tools:
- `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate`
- `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot`
- `mcp__playwright__browser_click`
- `mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot`
- `mcp__playwright__browser_press_key`
- `mcp__playwright__browser_close`

### 2. Affected Sessions

Analysis of 11 session logs shows:
- **72+ instances** of this serialization error across 9 session files
- Sessions with Playwright usage have higher rates of abnormal termination
- Sessions end abruptly (USER message as last entry) when Playwright is heavily used

### 3. Exit Codes Indicating Signal Termination

| Exit Code | Meaning | Occurrences |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| 128 | Process killed by signal | 24 |
| 144 | Signal 16 (SIGUSR1) | 4 |

Exit codes ≥128 indicate the process was terminated by a signal, not a normal exit. This suggests the container runtime (Docker) killed the process, likely due to resource exhaustion.

### 4. Connection Errors Following Playwright Usage

Multiple sessions show `ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED` errors after Playwright tool calls, indicating the server became unavailable mid-session.

## Your Task

1. **Locate the Playwright MCP implementation** - Find where the MCP server handles Playwright tool responses

2. **Identify the serialization bug** - The code is likely calling `.ToString()` on a `List<Dictionary<string, object>>` instead of properly serializing it to JSON. Look for:
   - Response handling code for Playwright tools
   - JSON serialization of tool results
   - Any place where `List<>` or `Dictionary<>` types are converted to strings

3. **Check for resource leaks** - The repeated malformed responses may indicate:
   - Browser instances not being properly disposed
   - Memory leaks from screenshot/snapshot operations
   - Unclosed connections or streams

4. **Fix the serialization** - Ensure all Playwright tool responses are properly serialized using `System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize()` or equivalent, not `.ToString()`

5. **Add error handling** - Ensure Playwright errors don't crash the MCP server:
   - Wrap browser operations in try-catch
   - Properly dispose resources on error
   - Return structured error responses instead of crashing

## Expected vs Actual Behavior

**Expected:** Playwright tools return JSON-serialized content:
```json
{
  "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "Navigation successful"}],
  "isError": false
}
```

**Actual:** Tools return .NET type name as a string:
```
System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary`2[System.String,System.Object]]
```

## Files to Investigate

Look for files related to:
- MCP server implementation
- Playwright tool handlers
- Tool response serialization
- Any `IToolHandler` or similar interface implementations

Search patterns:
```bash
# Find Playwright-related MCP code
rg -l "playwright" --type cs
rg -l "mcp.*tool|tool.*handler" --type cs -i

# Find serialization code
rg "List<Dictionary<string" --type cs
rg "ToString\(\)" --type cs -A2 -B2 | grep -i "result\|response\|content"

# Find MCP response building
rg "ToolResult|McpResponse|CallToolResult" --type cs
```

## Reproduction Steps

1. Start a session with Playwright MCP tools enabled
2. Use any Playwright tool (navigate, click, snapshot, etc.)
3. Observe the tool response - it should show the .NET type name instead of actual content
4. After several Playwright operations, the container may crash or become unresponsive

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Playwright tools return properly serialized JSON responses
- [ ] No .NET type names appear in tool output
- [ ] Sessions with heavy Playwright usage complete without abnormal termination
- [ ] Exit codes remain 0 (normal) rather than 128+ (signal-terminated)
- [ ] No `ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED` errors after Playwright operations

## Additional Context

This bug only started occurring after Playwright MCP tools were introduced. Sessions without Playwright usage do not exhibit the crash behavior. The correlation between Playwright usage and container crashes is strong across all analyzed session logs.

title: Asychronous issue serialisation
description: Serialising issue changes is currently synchronous, i.e. when a new issue is created or edited the UI locks up until the issue has been saved to the JSONL file. Keep all issues in memory and serialise asynchronously in the background. If this would be better handled in the Fleece library, let me know (i.e. moving Fleece service into fleece library).

title: Agent goes into waiting status when plan is submitted with clear context instructions
description: When starting execution on a new plan in the session chat for an agent in docker execution mode, the agent starts executing but the status remains as 'waiting' instead of 'working' in the Homespun app. Fixed by adding missing BroadcastSessionStatusChanged calls in SendMessageAsync.

title: Fix new agents starting for PRs
description:
When I click the "Start" to start a new agent for a PR that has an existing worktree and branch, a new agent is not started. See logs below. I believe this may be due to the worktree path not being found - the worktree and folder does exist when I review the flie system, but it is named "issues/feature/improve-tool-output-aLP3LH" rather than "issues/feature/improve-tool-output+aLP3LH", which is the branch name. Try to replicate this issue in an integration test by creating a new worktree "issues/feature/improve-tool-output-aLP3LH", creating a mock GitHub PR service with the branch name "issues/feature/improve-tool-output+aLP3LH" and trying to start an agent with the "None" agent based on the branch name that is coming from the PR. This should sanitize out the "+" in the process.

title: Add tabs to top of project page
description: At the top of the project page at the projects/{id} route, the application shows the Issues and Branches pages as links rather than as visually separate tabs (that should appear like tabs but still route to different pages. Please use the Playwright MCP tools with the application run in mock mode to investigate this behaviour and iterate on a fix.

title: Add buttons for series and parallel
description: In the issues tab, there is currently a downwards arrow show if the issue is in series execution mode and nothing shown if the issue is in parallel execution mode. Instead of this, add a toggle button between the status and the title that allows the user to switch between execution modes for an issue. It should use the git-commit-vertical Lucide icon for the series mode and the git-fork icon for the parallel mode.

title: Clicking "back to session" when session isn't found leads to the agents page
Create WorkflowHub for real-time updates
Make page height full size
Notifications System
description:

Implement the notifications system with toast notifications.

**Tasks:**
1. Create toast notification component (use shadcn Toast or similar)
2. Create notification context/provider for global access
3. Connect to SignalR notifications hub
4. Handle notification types:

   - Agent status updates (started, completed, error)

title: Fix question status behaviour
description:
The "question" status was added that is triggered when a "AskUserQuestion" tool runs. This successfully causes a session to wait until a user can respond, but it is not shown in the session indicator at the top right corner of the window. Please update this so that a red dot is shown when an agent is awaiting a response to a question.

Sort the agent sessions in the sessions window such that this is always at the top.

title: Consolidate duplicate @keyframes animations to app.css
description: Move duplicate @keyframes pulse animations from AgentStatusIndicator.razor.css and WorkItem.razor.css to the global app.css file. Ensure consistent animation values (currently have different opacity and scale values). Create utility classes .animate-pulse, .animate-spin for reuse. Test with bunit tests and with playwright MCP using a mock container created with ./scripts/mock.sh.

title: Allow local only repositories
description: I would like to be able to run Homespun without the GitHub integrations as well - mostly for testing. Please review the architecture and plan to be able to detach the GitHub service for certain local-only projects. In this instance, creating a new project should just create the source folder adjacent to the other GitHub related projects. There will need to be a flag added to the project registration in homespun-data.json to note that it is not a GitHub backed project.

title: Reduce text size in session chat and fix headings
description:
The text size in the session chat in the React client is generally too large on mobile screens. Reduce the size to improve the readability and make the chat bubble width 90% of the window width.

Fix the headings in the rendered markdown so they render with heading styles - they are currently rendered with the same style as the normal text.

title: Add 'Tab' shortcut to change issue type
description: Hitting 'Tab' on an issue when it is selected and in 'select' mode should cycle through the issue types. Add a 3 second debounce to this.

title: Remaining client fixes
description:
Review and fix the following in Homespun.Web:

- The add issue above and add issue below buttons don't work. They should open the inline issue creator above or below the selected issue
- The selector to change the number of levels displayed in the graph was not ported from the Blazor app
- Add an option to the agent run prompts for "No prompt" - this should open a new session in plan mode without inject a prompt and navigate to the session page immediately.
- Add a dark mode switch to the global settings and default to the system mode.
- Change default selected model to Opus.

title: Change status text from "In Progress" to "Progress"
description: THe status buttons on the issues page currently contain the text "In Progress" when the issue status is "in progress". Instead of this, change the text in the button to just "Progress" for this status to make the button more compact - this text currently doesn't fit in the button at it's current fixed width. Leave the status as "In progress" on the edit page, as there is more room there.

title: Tab, toolbar and notification styling
description:
I would like to update the styling of the tabs, toolbars and notifications.

Tabs:
- Remove the vertical scroll of the tabs
- Fix the tab underline, it currently always stays at "issues" even after navigating to another tab

Toolbar:
- Put a light border around the toolbar and make the toolbar background blur the contents that scroll behind it as it scrolls down
- Make the button style have an outline
- Use button groups and separators to group similar functionality (see Blazor application for guidance)
- Always show all buttons at all sizes, currently some buttons like move to child don't show up on mobile screens

Notifications:
- They currently show up as white or in light mode. Include a dark mode as well.

title: Pull and sync button
description: There is a split button in the Blazor application that allows the user to pull recent changes from the git repository to main and merge the fleece issues, and to optionally commit and push the new fleece issues as a sync operation. This button hasn't been added to the React application, please add it in, in-line with the main project title on the projects page. This should be mostly implemented in the backend already, except for maybe the graph refreshing behaviour.

title: Protocol Integration Testing
description:
Test the full A2A → AG-UI message flow across all components.

## Tasks

### Unit Tests
Create new tests in tests/Homespun.Tests/Features/ClaudeCode/:
- A2AMessageParserTests.cs - Test A2A message parsing
- AGUIEventServiceTests.cs - Test A2A to AG-UI translation
- AGUIClientTests.cs - Test client event handling (bUnit)

### Integration Tests
Modify tests/Homespun.Api.Tests/:
- Test full message flow with AG-UI events
- Verify A2A events are correctly parsed from worker
- Verify AG-UI events are correctly emitted to client

### E2E Tests
Modify tests/Homespun.E2E.Tests/:
- Verify UI renders AG-UI events correctly
- Test session flow: start session, verify events reach client
- Test question flow: trigger AskUserQuestion, verify Custom event with QuestionPending
- Test plan approval: run plan mode, verify Custom event with PlanPending

## Verification Steps
1. Build worker: cd src/Homespun.Worker && npm run build
2. Build server: dotnet build src/Homespun.Server
3. Run tests: dotnet test
4. Start mock container: ./scripts/mock.sh
5. Manual verification of full flow

title: Prompts selector shows double the number of prompts
PR caching not used on project load
description: It appears that the PR cache is not being used when the projects page is first opened, resulting in a long "loading" wait while the page loads. The PR cache that is saved in the project folder (/data/src/{project}/pull_requests.jsonl) should be used for the initial load, with updated PRs loaded in the background.

title: Restart server and reconnect to workers
description: It should be possible to restart the server container and have it reconnect to worker containers that it started running before being restarted. This will need the server to be able to discover worker containers, and for worker containers to locally persist their conversations such that the server can catch up on any messages that happened during the restart.

title: Show session information tab
description: A session information panel was created but is not visible in the application. Please find this tab and enable it - it should show up on the right hand side for desktop screens and as a bottom drawer for mobile screens.

title: Phase 4: Agent Sessions & Chat
description:
## Verification Checklist

This phase implements the agent session and chat interface functionality in the **Homespun.Web** project (`src/Homespun.Web`) using PromptKit components.

**Child Issues:**
- [ ] **UHFCEz** - Chat Message List - Display conversation with streaming support
- [ ] **smQKXh** - Chat Input Component - Send messages with permission/model selection
- [ ] **KtGIWb** - Question Answering UI - Handle AskUserQuestion tool responses
- [ ] **xB0naF** - Plan Approval UI - Handle ExitPlanMode with three-option approval interface
- [ ] **CifcfQ** - Tool Result Renderers - Specialized display for tool outputs
- [ ] **6wBA7c** - Sessions List Page - Manage active and past sessions
- [ ] **KRue6L** - Agent Launcher and Controls - Start and monitor agents

**Agent Verification Instructions:**
When all child issues are complete, verify the following in `src/Homespun.Web`:
1. Confirm chat messages display with proper streaming support
2. Verify the chat input allows sending messages with model/permission selection
3. Confirm AskUserQuestion tool shows interactive options and submits answers
4. Verify Plan Approval UI shows three options (Clear Context, Continue, Reject)
5. Check that tool results (Bash, Read, Write, Grep, etc.) render with appropriate formatting
6. Confirm sessions list page shows active and past agent sessions
7. Verify agent can be started from the launcher with prompt selection
8. Check that real-time updates work via SignalR for all chat events

If any changes are required to fix these or to fix the wiring up of components, complete these fixes, build and test then create a PR. Once all tests pass in CI and there are no merge conflicts, squash merge the PR.

title: Tab, toolbar and notification styling
description:
I would like to update the styling of the tabs, toolbars and notifications.

Tabs:
- Remove the vertical scroll of the tabs
- Fix the tab underline, it currently always stays at "issues" even after navigating to another tab

Toolbar:
- Put a light border around the toolbar and make the toolbar background blur the contents that scroll behind it as it scrolls down
- Make the button style have an outline
