title: Restarting agents on existing worktrees doesn't work
description:
If a session has previously been running on a worktree after having been started on an issue, when I try to start a new agent session on that worktree it fails as it tries to first create the worktree.

Always check whether the worktree exists first and just start the agent in that worktree if it does. This seems to work OK when I start a new agent in a worktree in the "branches" page - the logic should be similar for starting a new agent on an issue if the worktree already exists.

title: Fix session display
description:
Storing a message cache and rendering it directly to screen was initially implemented in https://github.com/nick-boey/Homespun/pull/128, and message streaming was entirely removed in a recent chat. This appears to have broken the session display almost entirely - very few messages are displayed other than tool calls in the message window, and there is often a duplicate display of tool calls at the bottom of the chat session.

Could you please review the message caching system, the message format and the history of messages at /data/sessions/ as well as the current chat messages that are being sent to diagnose this issue? A list of the problems include:

- "Thinking" messages are not being displayed at all.
- Many tools show up as "Unknown".
- Occasionally messages are joined together as they are streamed in when they should really be separated as different "points". They often appear as joined sentences.
- The ExitPlanTool and AskUserQuestion tool often show a failed status.
- Neither the plan or message summary of the plan is shown when the ExitPlanTool is run.

Don't run a mock session yet, just look at the messages themselves, the message logs and the Homespun code to see if you can diagnose some issues. You should be able to find the messages related to this chat appearing in the /data/sessions/ folder (or a subfolder within) during the chat.

title: Add missing focus states to interactive components
description: Add focus ring styling to interactive components that are missing them: ChatInput.razor (textarea and button), AgentSelector.razor, ModelSelector.razor, and NotificationBanner.razor (action/dismiss buttons). Use consistent focus-visible styling with design token colors.

title: Chat Input Component
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Create the chat input component using PromptKit PromptInput with permission and model selection.

**PromptKit Components to Use:**
- `PromptInput` - Auto-resizing textarea with action buttons
- `PromptInputTextarea` - The text input area
- `PromptInputActions` - Container for action buttons
- `PromptInputAction` - Individual action button with tooltip
- `FileUpload` - Optional file attachment support

**Install Commands:**
```bash
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/prompt-input.json"
npx shadcn@latest add "https://prompt-kit.com/c/file-upload.json"
```

**Tasks:**
1. Install and configure PromptKit PromptInput component
2. Build chat input using PromptInput with:
   - PromptInputTextarea for multi-line text entry with auto-resize
   - PromptInputActions for send and action buttons
3. Add send button as PromptInputAction with loading state via `isLoading` prop
4. Create permission mode selector (dropdown in actions area):
   - Default
   - Bypass permissions
   - Accept edits
   - Plan mode
5. Create model selector dropdown in actions area:
   - Opus (default)
   - Sonnet
   - Haiku
6. Keyboard shortcuts are built-in to PromptInput:
   - Enter: Send message (via onSubmit prop)
   - Shift+Enter: New line
7. Use `disabled` prop when agent is processing
8. Store selected model/permission in Zustand for persistence
9. Handle send via SignalR or REST API
10. Optionally add FileUpload component for attachments

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Messages send correctly via onSubmit handler
- Permission mode is passed to API
- Model selection works
- Input is disabled during agent processing (via disabled prop)
- Keyboard shortcuts work as expected (built-in to PromptInput)
- Auto-resize textarea grows with content up to maxHeight

title: Fix agent run sequence
description: This issue is failing in CI at the linting stage. Fix these issues and add a note to CLAUDE.md to run the linting and type checking before creating a PR.

title: Get branch name from linked PR if one exists
description: If there is a linked PR that contains an issue ID for an issue, always use the branch name in the PR to find the clone and launch the agent in the appropriate location. If there is no PR, also check the folders in the /.clones folder to see if there are any folders with the same issue ID as the issue. Occasionally if the branch name has not been persisted and a PR is created, creating a new session for the issue can result in a new branch being created with no reference to the already created branch/PR (which has the same issue ID but different branch ID and therefore different branch name).

title: Unify session and container status
description: The session status shown in the status indicator at the top right of the page and on the Sessions page doesn't align with the container status shown on the Containers page. The Containers page status is always correct, while the Session status can be incorrect, particularly in the case of holding on to 'plan pending' and 'waiting' statuses for too long (i.e. while the agent is actually working). Remove the logic that the sessions are using to calculate their own status, and instead use the status that is being reported by the agent container, as it is correct. Given that there is one session per container, unify the /sessions and /containers pages to be just the /sessions page - I believe the only additional feature that would be required is to show the container ID in the sessions page, as the 'stop session' already kills the container.

title: Tab/Shift+Tab closes inline issue creation input
description:
When creating a new issue using keyboard controls (o/O), pressing Tab or Shift+Tab to change the lane position closes the inline input instead of adjusting the lane.

## Root Cause
In InlineIssueCreateInput.razor, the Tab key is handled but the browser's default Tab behavior is not prevented. This causes focus to move away from the input, triggering HandleBlur which cancels the issue creation.

## Expected Behavior
- Tab should increase the lane offset (making new issue a parent of adjacent issue)
- Shift+Tab should decrease the lane offset (making new issue a child of adjacent issue)
- The inline input should remain focused and visible

## Actual Behavior
- Tab/Shift+Tab causes the input to lose focus
- HandleBlur is triggered, canceling the creation
- No lane adjustment occurs

## Fix Required
Add @onkeydown:preventDefault for Tab keys OR call e.preventDefault() in the JavaScript interop for Tab key handling in InlineIssueCreateInput.razor.

## Related Tests
E2E tests in IssueHierarchyCreationTests.cs document this bug and will fail when fixed.

title: Add API integration tests
Fix agent status and session chats
Show issues related to merged PRs
description: Merged PRs should allow selection and show the linked issue information if there is one. This should allow issues to be edited, and potentially re-opened if it is found that the merged PR did not resolve the issue.

title: Complete feature migration
Render Markdown
description: The chat window and issue description should both render text to Markdown by default as this often used by agents in communication.

title: Update project page UI
Clear context and continue often doesn't work
description:
When I clear context and start implementation of a plan, this often results in the plan options UI being left at the bottom of the session message window. The session status also often stays as "waiting" or "plan ready".

Once the "clear context and continue" button is clicked, the UI should disappear and the status should be set to working unless there is an error in executing this on the worker container.

title: Plans not being returned from agents running in containers
Update Docker build and CI/CD for new multi-project architecture
description:
Update the Docker build, startup scripts, and CI/CD workflows to work with the new multi-project architecture (Homespun.Server, Homespun.Client, Homespun.Shared) and the existing Homespun.Worker (TypeScript/Hono).

## Dockerfile changes

### Current Dockerfile
The current Dockerfile builds a single `Homespun` project. It needs to be updated to:

1. **Build the WASM client** → produces static files
2. **Build the server** → produces the API server binary
3. **Package them together** → server serves the WASM client files

### New multi-stage Dockerfile
\`\`\`dockerfile
# Stage 1: Build
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:10.0 AS build
WORKDIR /src

# Install Node.js for Tailwind CSS build (client project)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y nodejs npm

# Copy solution and project files
COPY Homespun.sln .
COPY src/Homespun.Shared/Homespun.Shared.csproj src/Homespun.Shared/
COPY src/Homespun.Server/Homespun.Server.csproj src/Homespun.Server/
COPY src/Homespun.Client/Homespun.Client.csproj src/Homespun.Client/
COPY src/Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk/Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk.csproj src/Homespun.ClaudeAgentSdk/

# Restore
RUN dotnet restore

# Copy everything and build
COPY . .

# Build Tailwind CSS (in client project)
WORKDIR /src/src/Homespun.Client
RUN npm install && npm run css:build

# Publish server (which includes WASM client via ProjectReference)
WORKDIR /src
RUN dotnet publish src/Homespun.Server/Homespun.Server.csproj -c Release -o /app/publish

# Stage 2: Runtime
FROM ${BASE_IMAGE} AS final
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build /app/publish .
ENTRYPOINT ["dotnet", "Homespun.Server.dll"]
\`\`\`

### Key points
- The Server .csproj should have a ProjectReference to the Client project so `dotnet publish` automatically includes WASM output
- Server .csproj needs: `<ProjectReference Include="../Homespun.Client/Homespun.Client.csproj" />` (or use the `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebAssembly.Server` package approach)
- The published output should contain both the server DLL and the `wwwroot/_framework/` directory with WASM files
- **Homespun.Worker** (TypeScript/Hono) has its own separate Dockerfile (`src/Homespun.Worker/Dockerfile`) and builds independently — it is NOT part of the .NET server build

## start.sh changes
- Update the entrypoint to run `Homespun.Server.dll` instead of `Homespun.dll`
- Health check URL should remain the same (`/health`)

## CI/CD workflow changes

### ci.yml (CI pipeline)
- Update build command to build the full solution (which already happens with `dotnet build`)
- Update test commands if test project references changed
- Ensure Tailwind CSS build step targets the Client project
- Homespun.Worker TypeScript tests (`npm test` in `src/Homespun.Worker/`) should continue to run as before

### cd.yml (CD pipeline)
- Update the main image build to publish `Homespun.Server`
- The **Homespun.Worker** image builds from `src/Homespun.Worker/Dockerfile` — this remains unchanged
- Update build args if needed

## Solution file changes
- Add `Homespun.Shared`, `Homespun.Server`, `Homespun.Client` to the solution
- Remove the old `Homespun` project from the solution (or keep it temporarily during transition)
- `Homespun.Worker` (TypeScript) is referenced in the solution for IDE convenience but is not a .csproj project
- Update solution folder nesting

## launchSettings.json
- Create launch profiles for the Server project
- The development workflow: run `Homespun.Server` which serves both API and WASM client
- Mock mode should still work via environment variables

## Infrastructure (Bicep) changes
- Update `infra/main.bicep` container app configuration if the entry point changed
- Update any container image references
- Homespun.Worker container app configuration remains unchanged

## docker-compose changes
- Update the main service to build and run `Homespun.Server`
- The worker service continues to use `src/Homespun.Worker/Dockerfile`
- Ensure networking between Server and Worker containers is correct

## Verification
- `docker build` succeeds for both Server and Worker images
- Container starts and serves the WASM client at the root URL
- API endpoints accessible at `/api/*`
- Health check passes at `/health`
- CI pipeline passes
- Mock mode works in container
- Worker containers can be spawned by the Server for agent execution

title: Search issues
description:
Create a simple text search feature that highlights and selects issues similar to Vim.

The '/' key should open the search bar and focus on it. This should be at the top of the page. When text is typed into this bar, the relevant issues should be highlighted with a border and the text that matches should be highlighted with a yellow highlight.

Hitting enter should then "embed" the search term into the viewer and hide the search bar. The first match should be selected. Typing 'n' or 'SHIFT + n' should then scroll forwards and backwards through the search results. Escape should remove the search term for the viewer and reset to the normal state. When in the search state, all other keyboard controls should act as normal - the user should still be able to use the j/k controls to go up and down the un-matched issues.

title: Migrate to Blazor Blueprint
description: Migrate all standard components over to Blazor Blueprint while still maintaining the custom theme that was used in the application (specifically the colours). Use the Blazor Blueprint MCP tools for assistance. Update the design system at /design to suit.

title: ExitPlanMode hangs when the plan is written locally
Fix auto pulling
description:
When a new agent is run, prior to creating the clone of the branch the most recent changes to main should be automatically pulled and the fleece issues merged (the functionality that the 'Pull' button on the project page performs).

This used to work, but apparently doesn't anymore, review the actions of creating a new agent and their ordering carefully.

title: New issues created with Tab/Shift+Tab don't establish parent-child relationships
description:
When creating a new issue using keyboard controls with Tab/Shift+Tab lane modifiers, the parent-child relationships are not being established correctly.

## Expected Behavior

### When creating BELOW selected issue:
- Tab (+1 lane): New issue should become PARENT of the selected issue (issue above)
- Shift+Tab (-1 lane): New issue should become CHILD of the selected issue (issue above)

### When creating ABOVE selected issue:
- Tab (+1 lane): New issue should become PARENT of the selected issue (issue below)
- Shift+Tab (-1 lane): New issue should become CHILD of the selected issue (issue below)

## Current Status
This bug cannot be tested until PUgrNi (Tab closes input) is fixed.

## Implementation Notes
- The lane offset is tracked in InlineIssueCreateInput.razor
- The CreateIssueAsync call needs to include the parent/child relationship based on lane offset
- For Series execution mode, sort order should also be considered

## Test Coverage
E2E tests in IssueHierarchyCreationTests.cs include tests for relationship verification that will execute once PUgrNi is fixed.

title: Remove issue details panel
description: Make the issue details panel a collapsible side bar rather than a floating panel that scrolls down with the screen. It should collapse on the right and be collapsed by default. Only open when an issue is clicked (not selected with the keyboard controls).

title: Fix issue agent merging
description: The FleeceChangeDetectionService and merging of issues into main appears to adopt the changes from the issue agent branch entirely rather than merge the issues from the main branch and the issues from the issue agent branch using the timestamp sensitive merging algorithm provided by Fleece.Core. Review this and update it to use the merging algorithm for both the diff calculation and the merging of accepted.

title: Add WorkflowComplete MCP tool to worker
description:
Add the WorkflowComplete MCP tool to the TypeScript worker for agents to signal workflow node completion.

## Files to Modify
- `src/Homespun.Worker/src/services/session-manager.ts` - Add tool handling in canUseTool callback

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Worker/src/tools/workflow-tools.ts` - Tool definitions

## Tool Schema
\`\`\`typescript
WorkflowComplete: {
  description: "Signal workflow node completion with output data",
  input_schema: {
    type: "object",
    properties: {
      status: { type: "string", enum: ["success", "partial", "needs_review"] },
      outputs: { type: "object", description: "Typed output data" },
      summary: { type: "string", description: "Human-readable summary" },
      artifacts: { type: "array", items: { name, path, type } }
    },
    required: ["status", "outputs", "summary"]
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation
1. Intercept WorkflowComplete tool calls in canUseTool callback
2. Emit workflow_tool event to SSE stream with tool data
3. Allow tool execution to complete (signals session end)
4. Only enable tool when session has workflowContext set

## Pattern Reference
Follow existing AskUserQuestion/ExitPlanMode handling in session-manager.ts

title: Show placeholders for PR status
Fix question answering input
description:
There are a couple of issues that need to be fixed with the question answering control (in the Homespun.Web React client):

- Multi select options don't allow selection - selection does nothing.
- Put question content under heading and make heading text and not a badge.
- Submitting answer removes question box. Currently the box stays in place.

Also, add a processing indicator to the window while the question is executing.

Refer to the Blazor client for information on this implementation.

title: Add single container mode for mini prompts and execution mode
description: Instead of the local agent execution mode that uses the Claude Agent SDK local library, add a new worker container in the Docker conpose that starts alongside the Homespun container. This should be used for all mini prompts. Get rid of the local agent execution mode and replace it with a single container agent execution mode that just sends all queries to that single container (rather than the Docker execution mode that starts new containers). Update the start script as required.

title: Improve agent container management
description: A lot of agent containers are being left running and not cleaned stopped even after an agent has been stopped. Show a list of the running agent workers in a new tab on the projects page so that individual agents can be killed. In this table of agents, show which issue and PR the agent was started on, as well as the last time that a prompt was sent to the agent. Group these by issue such it is clear which agent containers have been superseded and can be killed immediately. Also ensure that when the 'stop agent' button is clicked in a session that **all** related agent worker containers are killed. This should be for both Azure Container Apps and Docker container execution modes.

title: Improve worktree management
description:
The branch and worktree management system needs some improvement as it blocks agents from starting and has become difficult to manage.

## Branches page

### Active worktrees panel

- This shows "Plan" and "Build" buttons for each of the worktrees. Replace these with the normal agent management control that is used in the issues details. If this is not already a separate Blazor component, extract it to one now.
- Show "lost worktrees", these being the folders that reside beside the main folder but are not associated with the branch. Tag these as being "Lost".
- Add a button to delete the lost worktrees (deleting the worktree folder completely) and to "repair" the worktree by attaching it back to a branch (if a matching branch can be found).
- Add a tag to show any worktrees (including main) that are not checked out against the branch that matches the name of the worktree. Add a button to checkout the correct target branch and to get it back on task.
- Show the current git status (number of files modified) for all worktrees.
- Add a global button to prune all worktrees.
- Show the agent session status against worktrees that have an agent running in them.

### Local branches

- Move the local branches with PRs into a new panel "Open PRs". Only include local branches without PRs in this list.
- It appears that the branches with PRs that were squash merged don't show up as being related to PRs and don't show up as "merged". Update this so that local branches are tracked against PRs that have been merged. 
- Show if branches are local only **and** remote or just local only with a tag showing that it is local only.

### Remote branches

- Show which of these branches have been merged (or squash merged) to main.
- Add a button to delete the remote branch.
- Ensure that when "checkout" is clicked that this also creates a new worktree. Currently when this is clicked it checks out the branch to the root worktree, and then tries to create the worktree. It fails to do this as two worktrees cannot be checked out with the same branch. Instead, it should create the worktree first and leave the default branch alone.

Implement this using a TDD approach.

title: Review worker container image
description: Building a worker container image currently takes a long time compared to the other images, and there appears to be issues when pulling the image from GHCR. Could you please review the GitHub Actions for CD and the Dockerfile to try and understand the issues that are occurring with the container build?

title: Add "move up" and "move down" buttons for series siblings
description: When a sibling is selected, "move up" and "move down" buttons should become available in the toolbar above the task graph view. This should allow the issue to be moved up and down the list of siblings, functionality which should be available in the Fleece issue service.

title: Show agent status
description: The agent status (working or waiting) is information that should be available. This should be displayed on the timeline next to the issues that the agents are linked to. This can just be as text for now. Ideally it should update as the status changes. Please consider how this would best be done using Blazor Interactive Rendering.

title: Create plan and build views
PR status is always "Checks running"
description: When looking at any PR status in the client the status is always "checks running". Review the PR status service and see if you can find out what the issue is here.

title: Add simple issue tree view
description:
I want to try using traditional hierarchical tree view of the issues to see if this is more appropriate. Add a button that switches the view mode to the toolbar. Instead of the current task graph view that shows the next issues at the root and their parents as leaves, flip this to show the parent issues on the left side and their children progressing down and to the right. Still show the graph nodes and edges but matching the new tree structure.

If a node has multiple parents, just show it once when it is first traversed in the tree.

Remove the merged and closed pull requests from the top of the graph in this view mode.

title: Serialize all enums as strings
description: There are a number of enums that are used in the DTOs that are passed between the C# server and the Typescript client. These are all currently being handled as their integer values, which has led to significant problems with mismatched values and errors. I would like to instead pass these values as strings: the C# side should serialize and deserialize all values assuming the incoming and outgoing values are strings, and the Typescript side should always just operate as strings. These are used all throughout the Typescript application, so be thorough on identifying any numeric values that need to be converted to their string values. I expect that many tests will need to be updated to suit.

title: Phase 8: Testing & Migration
description:
## Verification Checklist

This phase completes final testing, verification, and migration for the **Homespun.Web** project (`src/Homespun.Web`).

**Child Issues:**
- [ ] **tPeo0W** - Unit and Integration Tests - Test coverage for components and hooks
- [ ] **A7aDTp** - E2E Tests - Port existing Playwright tests
- [ ] **4t8gRw** - Feature Parity Verification - Ensure all Blazor features are replicated
- [ ] **4j2Qll** - Migration Completion - Documentation and deployment updates

**Agent Verification Instructions:**
When all child issues are complete, verify the following in `src/Homespun.Web`:
1. Confirm `npm test` runs successfully with adequate coverage (>80% on critical paths)
2. Verify Playwright E2E tests pass and cover critical user journeys
3. Confirm all Blazor features have been replicated in the React frontend
4. Verify documentation is updated (README, development setup, architecture)
5. Check that CI/CD pipeline builds and deploys the React application
6. Confirm performance benchmarks meet or exceed Blazor implementation
7. Verify deprecation plan for Blazor client is documented

Mark each child issue checkbox above as verified, then mark this phase complete.

title: Make nav menu responsive content match sidebar
description: The sidebar is only shown on larger screens, so when a smaller screen is used the responsive nav menu is used to display another sidebar. Change this such that the content between these two is identical as they both represent the same information.

title: Expand MockAgentExecutionService with realistic mock responses
description:
The MockAgentExecutionService created in the remove-claude-agent-sdk PR is minimal. Expand it to provide realistic mock responses similar to MockClaudeSessionService, including simulated tool use, thinking blocks, and configurable response content.

It should use simple keyword matching to respond in particular ways to a prompt:
- "think": Send back a thinking block, then respond with a message.
- "tool": Send back a read file tool usage and a write file tool usage, then respond with a message.
- "question": Write a message, then ask one question with single select options and one question with a multi-select option. After finishing, create a plan.
- "plan": Enter plan mode and send a plan for execution.

These should be implemented with the correct statuses returned from the agent at the correct times. Add appropriate wait times for sending the messages back asynchronously.

This keyword matching should be used to assemble the overall query - if more than one keyword is matched, the assembled query should still run in order and shouldn't duplicate the plan creation.

For example, "Use a tool and then think about the response. Ask a question and create a plan" should result in the thought block ("I'm thinking about the prompt"), a returned message ("I have thought about the message"), a tool block ("Read a file 'test.txt'", "Wrote a file 'test.txt'), a returned message ("I used some tools"), a question ("Here are two questions, one with multiple choice answers and one with single choice answers"), a returned message after the question is answered ("You responded to the first question with yellow and the second question with purple, blue and pink."), a returned message ("I am creating a plan"), a plan ("Here is my plan") and finally a message ("I am now working on the plan with cleared context").

In this example, the plan is not being sent twice.

title: Add priority markers to the graph view
description: The priorities of issues should be shown. Use the new graph sort features of the issue service provided by the Fleece Core library to group the "next" issues by priority. Colour the vertical line (which is currently grey) in the first lane with the priority of the "next" issues in each group, ranging from P4 to P0.

title: Change ring around issue nodes to indicate working agents
description: A ring is currently shown around issue nodes in the task graph view of the React client to show the items that are "next". Instead of this, I would like the ring to represent the issues that have agents currently working on them, with the colour representing the agent status.

title: Auto update fleece issues
description:
Currently the agent workers are responsible for updating Fleece issues based on the instructions in the 'prime' command. This is somewhat unreliable, so I would like to have the Homespun app update the issue statuses depending on how the PRs come in.

This should be done when the issues are synchronised and linked to PRs. The PR status takes precedence over the issue status:

- When the PR is open, the issue status should be "Review"
- When the PR is merged, the issue status should be "Complete"
- When the PR is closed, the issue status should be "Closed"

title: Fix worker container build
description: The worker container build failed in the recent CD workflow. It appears that the base image name is not being passed into the worker build. Update this.

title: Extract FormMessage component for error/success alerts
description:
Create a reusable FormMessage component to replace error/success alert patterns used in 6+ pages including ProjectCreate.razor, IssueEdit.razor, and SessionManagement.razor. Support error, success, warning, and info variants.

Test with bunit tests and with playwright MCP using a mock container created with mock.sh

title: Implement workflow trigger service
description:
Create the trigger service for managing workflow triggers.

## Files to Create
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/IWorkflowTriggerService.cs`
- `src/Homespun.Server/Features/Workflows/Services/WorkflowTriggerService.cs`

## Interface Methods
- `RegisterTriggersAsync(workflow)` - Register triggers for workflow
- `UnregisterTriggersAsync(workflowId)` - Remove triggers
- `EvaluateTriggerAsync(trigger, event)` - Check if event matches trigger

## Trigger Types
1. **Manual** - No registration needed, triggered via API
2. **Event** - Subscribe to internal event bus
3. **Scheduled** - Register with scheduler service

## Event Types to Support
- `issue_created` - When new issue is created
- `issue_status_changed` - When issue status changes
- `pr_merged` - When PR is merged
- `webhook` - External webhook triggers

title: Consolidate icon usage to Bootstrap Icons
description: The codebase currently uses a mix of inline SVG icons and Bootstrap Icons. Consolidate all icon usage to use Bootstrap Icons consistently throughout the application.

title: Finalise ACA deployment

title: Remove Test Agent from Release build
description: The 'Start Test Agent' button in the project information panel should be hidden using preprocessor directives to only show in Debug builds. This also applies to the "debug signalR events" in the session page.

title: Update agent page UI
Mount .claude folder
description:
The original cloning behaviour was meant to have the following structure (from the perspective of the main application container):

- /data/repos/{project}/.clones : Contains all clones of the main branch that agents are working on.
- /data/repos/{project}/.clones/{clone}/.claude : The Claude folder mounted for the agent. This is where the agents working on each clone store their session information. This should be mounted as /home/homespun/.claude for each agent container.
- /data/repos/{project}/.clones/{clone}/workdir : The code for the repository should be cloned in this folder. This is mounted as /workdir for each agent container.

Instead of this, only the /workdir folder seems to be mounted and the /data/repos/{project}/.clones/{clone}/ folder contains all of the cloned repo source code.

title: Fix card layout for session cards
description:
The layout for session cards (as shown on the global "Sessions" page has an issue where the "Chat" and "Stop" buttons often float off the side of the card and underneath the adjacent card. Fix this by removing the "Chat" button and making the entire card clickable to take the user to the relevant session page.

The stop button should be a smaller red icon button so it fits in the card.

The issue title also doesn't go all the way across the card but is instead stuck at a smaller width. Instead of having the cards be in multiple columns, always just have one column of cards that are full width and wrap the issue title text so it always displays the full thing.

title: Set worker mode from prompt setting
description: The agent prompts have a "mode" setting that can be either "Build" or "Plan". It appears for the current Claude implementation that the "BypassPermissions" mode (that maps to "Plan") is always used when the agent is started. The "Plan" mode should be passed in when the prompt mode is "Plan". Please review and fix.

title: Responsive Design
description:
**Project:** `src/Homespun.Web` (React frontend)

Ensure the application works well on mobile devices.

**Tasks:**
1. Review all pages on mobile viewport (375px, 768px breakpoints)
2. Fix layout issues:
   - Convert sidebar to drawer/hamburger menu on mobile
   - Stack layouts vertically on small screens
   - Ensure touch-friendly tap targets (min 44px)
3. Test issue detail panel on mobile:
   - Full-screen panel on mobile
   - Swipe to dismiss
4. Test chat interface on mobile:
   - Fixed input at bottom
   - Keyboard handling
5. Ensure all actions are accessible via touch
6. Test navigation and back button behavior
7. Add viewport meta tag if not present
8. Test on actual mobile devices if possible

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- App is fully usable on mobile devices
- No horizontal scroll issues
- All features accessible via touch
- Sidebar works as drawer on mobile
- Chat input is usable on mobile keyboard

title: Issues not being written with full description with CTRL+Enter
description: Occasionally issues are not being persisted with their full descriptions. Review the issue updating logic to ensure that the full issue description is written to the Fleece issues JSONL file in the repository. This appears to be the case when CTRL + Enter is used to save the changes instead of clicking the "Save Changes" button, and when the user hasn't lost focus on the description box.

description:

title: Consolidate toolbar buttons
description: There are multiple different buttons in three different locations that are all intended to be in the same toolbar. The "create above", "create below" and "move up", "move down" buttons are in the correct location. Move all other buttons to this location, group them with a new `ToolbarSeparator` component and make their styling all similar. The Edit and Run buttons have the correct button and tooltip styling.

title: Switch from worktrees to full clones to avoid contamination
description: I am having some issues with agents swapping branches within the worktrees of their repositories with the main branch, resulting in accidental pushes to mainn and general confusion. I want to consider just using full clones for each agent and one for the Homespun application. Do you think this would solve the problem? Let me know if you have any questions or suggestions.

title: Fix log level parsing for server logs
description:
In Grafana the client and worker logs show the log level clearly (e.g. Info, Error, etc.). The server logs, however, do not parse this correctly and they log level is not shown even though it is being captured in the "Parsed Field" as LogLevel.

The lines in the All Logs are also difficult to read - they should be colour coded to show which are coming from the server and client, and have the Message text more visible. Please try to unify the logging output between the client, server and workers as they all look quite different.

title: Add default filters
description: The filters shown for the issue graph should default to showing "next" issues and issues that are assigned to the current user. When the 'f' key is pressed, the caret in the filter box should be placed at the end of the text box for adding more filters.

title: Client AG-UI Consumer
description:
Client receives and renders AG-UI events.

## Tasks
1. Update SignalR Client (ClaudeCodeSignalRService.cs)
   - Add handlers: OnRunStarted, OnRunFinished, OnRunError
   - Add handlers: OnTextMessageStart, OnTextMessageContent, OnTextMessageEnd
   - Add handlers: OnToolCallStart, OnToolCallArgs, OnToolCallEnd, OnToolCallResult
   - Add handlers: OnStateSnapshot, OnStateDelta
2. Update Shared DTOs
   - Add AG-UI compatible event types: AGUIRunEvent, AGUITextMessageEvent, AGUIToolCallEvent
   - Map to existing ClaudeMessage/ClaudeMessageContent for rendering
3. Update Message Display Service
   - Handle AG-UI event sequences (Start/Content/End pattern)
4. Update Rendering Components
   - TextBlock.razor - Handle TextMessageContent streaming
   - ThinkingBlock.razor - Handle ReasoningMessageContent
   - ToolUseBlock.razor - Handle ToolCallStart/Args/End
   - ToolResultBlock.razor - Handle ToolCallResult

## Files to Modify
- src/Homespun.Client/Services/ClaudeCodeSignalRService.cs
- src/Homespun.Client/Services/MessageDisplayService.cs
- src/Homespun.Shared/Models/Sessions/AGUIEvents.cs (new)
- src/Homespun.Shared/Hubs/IClaudeCodeHubClient.cs
- src/Homespun.Client/Components/Chat/TextBlock.razor
- src/Homespun.Client/Components/Chat/ThinkingBlock.razor
- src/Homespun.Client/Components/Chat/ToolUseBlock.razor
- src/Homespun.Client/Components/Chat/ToolResultBlock.razor

title: Adding a new issue at the bottom of the issues shows two inputs
description: When I select the very last issue in the graph and create a new issue below using 'o', two inline issue editors appear and one is focused. Create tests to replicate this and then create a plan to fix it.

title: Manage multiple sessions
description:
Multiple sessions on the same branch should be accessible. The Claude worker should be used as the single source of truth - I believe the management of multiple sessions is possible using the Typescript SDK.

I want to have the "session" page represent all the sessions that have been run on an issue. A session tab should be added to the information panel that shows all past sessions, each of which represents a context window. Clicking on these should open previous sessions as read-only.

When the agent session page is navigated to, show the latest active session only. There should also be a button to clear context and add a new session for this agent worker container.

Clarify the difference between "agent" (a worker container running on a cloned issue branch) and a session ("a single conversation with that "agent" when working.

title: Fix permissions mode on agent launch
description: When an agent is launched using the agent control panel, the permissions mode is not set to the mode from the selected prompt but seems to always default to "BypassPermissions" or "Build". This has specifically just happened for the default "Plan" prompt.

title: Switching projects with side bar doesn't work
description: The page address and heading changes but the graph doesn't refresh when switching projects with the project list in the side bar.

title: Auto-generate branch ID when issue is created or issue title is changed
description: When a new issue is created (with a title) or the issue title is changed, the branch ID should be auto-generated asynchronously using the same logic as what happens when the button is clicked on the edit page. If the agent doesn't respond, this should fail gracefully with a notification and no change to the branch ID.

title: Show plans in session window
description:
Add a new tab to the session window that shows the plans that are generated in the sessions. They can be found in the /data/src/{project}/.clones/{issue}/.claude/plans folder as Markdown files for each issue.

Show a preview of the plan content that can be expanded and add a button to copy the plan file path or content to the clipboard.

Note that there may be multiple plan files.

title: CTRL+Enter behaviour still doesn't save descriptions while editing
description: A recent PR (#273) was meant to fix the behaviour in which saving changes to an issue from the edit page with CTRL+Enter only worked if the description lost focus. This didn't work as planned, however, and the application still doesn't save the changes made in the description box if CTRL+Enter is pressed without losing focus. Review this in closer detail and add tests specifically aimed at fixing this problem.

title: Add details to container card
description:
Update the container card on the /containers page:

- Display issue for the container in the title of the Container card on the "containers" page (the span with class `container-name`). Currently it just has the name of the container, which should be in the card below under the "Issue:" detail row.
- The "Project" also comes up as "Unkown" - ensure that this shows the project that the container is working in, and the cards are grouped by project similar to the "Sessions" page.
- The "Project" value should be a link to the project page that the container is working in.
- The "Issue" value should be a link to the project page and should select the issue on navigation (possibly with a # anchor to trigger the selection).
- When a plan is ready or there are other status updates there is a little badge at the bottom of the card saying "Plan Approval Pending" - this should just be shown in the "Status" detail row to replace the text.

title: Add 'Tab' shortcut to change issue type
description: Hitting 'Tab' on an issue when it is selected and in 'select' mode should cycle through the issue types. Add a 3 second debounce to this.

title: Show full issue title in containers window and associate with projects
description: In the containers page the containers are not being linked to the correct projects and issue information to display the name of each - it just says unknown project and the issue ID. Instead of this, it should show the full name. The name of the project and the issue ID is in the container name, use this for identification and update the displayed name.

title: Expose logs to workers
description:
The application currently logs using the PLG stack in containers that ride side by side to the worker containers. I would like to be able to have the worker containers access these logs without having to connect to tailscale. Review the connections that are available and put together a plan to have the workers read these logs so they can analyse them as they build the Homespun application.

This should take the form of instructions in the CLAUDE.md on how the worker can use curl to access logs from Loki.

title: Fix issue type colours in graph view
description: The issue type colours are mismatched with the meaning of the issue types (e.g. bugs are green). Fix this - I believe it was initially due to the numeric remapping of enum values.
